flipflop~.digest: A Period-by-Period Synthesis oscillator that alternately receives the frequencies and the other parameters from two groups
flipflop~.use_when: A Period-by-Period Synthesis oscillator that alternately receives the frequencies and the other parameters from two groups.

jeyhisto.digest: Derives histograms (probability-distribution plots) from incoming numbers by means of Binning and Razoring
jeyhisto.use_when: Derives histograms (probability-distribution plots) from incoming numbers by means of Binning and Razoring.

jeyhistosliders.digest: A dimensions-responsive bpatcher-abstraction enclosing the jeyhisto, multislider, and other objects
jeyhistosliders.use_when: A dimensions-responsive bpatcher-abstraction enclosing the jeyhisto, multislider, and other objects.

jeyhistosliders~.digest: A dimensions-responsive bpatcher-abstraction enclosing the jeyhisto~, multislider, and other objects
jeyhistosliders~.use_when: A dimensions-responsive bpatcher-abstraction enclosing the jeyhisto~, multislider, and other objects.

jeyhisto~.digest: Derives histograms (probability-distribution plots) from incoming samples by means of Binning and Razoring
jeyhisto~.use_when: Derives histograms (probability-distribution plots) from incoming samples by means of Binning and Razoring.

jeynoisesliders~.digest: A dimensions-responsive bpatcher-abstraction enclosing the jeynoise~, multislider, and other objects
jeynoisesliders~.use_when: A dimensions-responsive bpatcher-abstraction enclosing the jeynoise~, multislider, and other objects.

jeynoise~.digest: A sine and cosine wave oscillator that works based on Period-by-Period Synthesis technique
jeynoise~.use_when: A sine and cosine wave oscillator that works based on Period-by-Period Synthesis technique.

jeyrand.digest: Redistributes numbers as specified by a user-defined list-style-Probability-Distribution-Function (PDF-list)
jeyrand.use_when: Redistributes numbers as specified by a user-defined list-style-Probability-Distribution-Function (PDF-list).

jeyrandsliders.digest: A dimensions-responsive bpatcher-abstraction enclosing the jeyrand, multislider, and other objects
jeyrandsliders.use_when: A dimensions-responsive bpatcher-abstraction enclosing the jeyrand, multislider, and other objects.

jeyrandsliders~.digest: A dimensions-responsive bpatcher-abstraction enclosing the jeyrand~, multislider, and other objects
jeyrandsliders~.use_when: A dimensions-responsive bpatcher-abstraction enclosing the jeyrand~, multislider, and other objects.

jeyrand~.digest: Redistributes samples as specified by a user-defined list-style-Probability-Distribution-Function (PDF-list)
jeyrand~.use_when: Redistributes samples as specified by a user-defined list-style-Probability-Distribution-Function (PDF-list).

aoo.client.use_when: AOO peer client — sends/receives audio via the AOO peer-to-peer protocol. Configure with `connect <host> <port>`. Use AOO when you need low-latency LAN audio streaming without a central server.

aoo.receive~.digest: Receive an AOO stream from an
aoo.receive~.use_when: Receive an audio stream over AOO from a peer. Multi-source capable; outputs streams from all configured senders.

aoo.send~.digest: Send an AOO stream to an
aoo.send~.use_when: Send an audio stream over AOO to one or more peers. Pair with aoo.receive~ on the receiving side. `connect <host> <port> <id>` to add a recipient.

aoo.server.use_when: AOO discovery/relay server — coordinates AOO clients on a network. Run alongside aoo.client instances to form a peer mesh.

az.granulate~.use_when: Auzzie granular synthesis from a sample buffer — cloud-style granulator. Use for audio file granulation; reach for grainflow for explicit per-grain scheduling, or mubu.granular~ for descriptor-driven selection.

az.spectdelay~.use_when: Spectral delay processor — independent delay per FFT bin. Use for harmonic-tail / impulse-response-like effects you can't get from time-domain delay.

az.specteq~.use_when: Spectral EQ — frequency-domain shaping with arbitrary curve. Use when the response curve is too detailed for biquad/parametric chains.

az.strum~.use_when: Karplus-Strong plucked-string synthesis. Inlets for excitation, decay, tuning. Use for cheap CPU-light plucked string voices.

2threshattack~.use_when: Two-threshold attack/transient detector. 2 signal inlets (input audio, attack-detect input — typically the same), 2 outlets (attack bang, current state). Constructor args: `<low> <high>` set initial thresholds; runtime `low <value>` / `high <value>` retune. `tellmeeverything` dumps state. Use for robust onset detection on noisy or amplitude-varying material — the input must cross the LOW threshold then the HIGH threshold to fire, giving immunity to slow-rising drift and small bumps that single-threshold gates would catch.

accumulate~.digest: Adds the current incoming sample to the previous output.
accumulate~.use_when: Single-pole integrator: `out[n] = scale * in[n] + out[n-1]`, with optional clipping/wrapping. 2 signal inlets (input, reset trigger), 1 outlet. `@scale` (float) sets per-sample multiplier; `@min` / `@max` define the bounds; `@mode` (symbol) selects boundary behaviour (clip/wrap/fold/etc.); `@start` is the initial value. Use for cheap leaky-integrator accumulation in DSP — phasor-style ramps, slow envelopes, integrator filters — without rolling a +~ feedback loop with tapin~/tapout~.

analyzer~.use_when: FFT-based perceptual audio analyser — outputs pitch (Hz), pitch confidence, loudness (sones), brightness (centroid), noisiness, attack flag, list of peaks (freq/amp), Bark coefficients on multiple outlets. Constructor args: `<buffersize> <hopsize> <fftsize> <windowtype> <initialdelay> <numpitches> <numpeakstofind> <numpeakstooutput> <barkformat>` — many tuning knobs. Runtime: `npartial` queries partial count, `vibrato` reports vibrato analysis, `reattack` re-arms attack detection, `amp-range <min> <max>` sets amplitude scale. Use as a one-stop perceptual feature extractor; compare to chaining fluid.spectralshape~ + fluid.pitch~ + fluid.loudness~ (more accurate but heavier).

bdist.use_when: Beta distribution random number generator (UI-displayed). Send `bang` to draw; configure shape with `a <float>` and `b <float>` (the two beta parameters); `displaymode <name>` switches plot style; `distlist <int|list>` queries distribution at multiple points. Use to draw from an asymmetric, bounded distribution when uniform random isn't shaped enough — e.g. probabilistic envelope shapes, weighted choice with continuous bias.

bench.use_when: Control-rate benchmarking utility — measure execution time of message-passing chains. Constructor arg `<type>` selects timing source. Send `bang` to start/stop a timing run; pass through `anything` to chain into the timed path; `version` queries version. Use to profile a control-rate subgraph during optimisation — wrap the suspect chain between two `bench` calls to measure delta.

bench~.use_when: Audio-rate benchmarking utility. Constructor arg `<type>` selects timing source. Pass `signal` through to time per-vector cost; `version` queries version. Use to profile DSP performance per-vector when optimising heavy chains.

bessel.use_when: Bessel function evaluator. Send `j0 <x>` for J₀(x), `j1 <x>` for J₁(x), `jn <order> <x>` for Jₙ(x), or `jmn <order_begin> <order_end>` to evaluate a range of orders. Also accepts plain `int`/`float`/`list` for default-order evaluation. Use for FM-synthesis sideband math (Bessel functions describe FM partial amplitudes), antenna patterns, or any application needing Bessel functions without dropping into JS.

bpf.digest: bpf - break point function editor, CNMAT-style.
bpf.use_when: Break-point function editor (UI). CNMAT's alternative to native function with multi-function support and OSC compatibility. Many attrs for visual config: `@bgFuncColor`, `@gridColor`, `@lineColor`, `@drawlabels`, `@drawposition`, `@displaymode`, `@labelstart`, plus 29+ more (consult refpage). Manage functions via `addfunction <id>` / `deletefunction <id>` / `clearfunction <id>` / `clearcurrentfunction` / `deletecurrentfunction`; clear regions with `clearregion <start_x> <end_x>` or `clearregionforallfunctions`; `dump` outputs all points; `deleteselected` removes selected points. Use when native function isn't expressive enough (multiple curves, OSC-friendly state).

cambio~.digest: Signal-to-event version of change, for mostly-constant signals
cambio~.use_when: Signal-rate change detector for mostly-constant signals — emits an event only when input value changes. Drive with a signal carrying control values (e.g. snapshot~ output, control bus). Use as a signal-domain equivalent of `change` for slowly-varying control signals carried at audio rate — avoids the cost of converting to event-rate just to detect changes.

cnmatrix~.digest: Signal routing with Jitter matrices as input
cnmatrix~.use_when: Audio matrix routing using a Jit.matrix as the routing pattern. Constructor args: `<inputs> <outputs> <interpolation_mode>`. Drive routing matrix via `jit_matrix <name>` messages. Smooth between matrix configurations with `slide <ms>` or `smooth`; `fast` and `frame` toggle update timing; `list` for direct gain table input. Use to drive a matrix~-style audio router with patterns generated as Jitter matrices — generative or visual routing without per-cell message scripting.

decaying-sinusoids~.use_when: Bank of exponentially-decaying sinusoids — efficient additive synthesis of struck/plucked tones. Constructor arg: `<list-of-triplets>` (freq, amp, decay-time) defining the bank. Runtime: `list <triplets>` updates bank, `clear` empties it, `goto <seconds>` jumps playback time, `min_vtime <s>` / `max_vtime <s>` bound decay times, `rate <scalar>` time-scales decays. Send `float` to retrigger. Use for modelling bells, plates, plucked strings; cheaper than rolling N parallel cycle~ + line~ pairs.

deinterleave.digest: "De-interleave" (aka delace) elements in one list into some number of smaller lists.
deinterleave.use_when: Split one interleaved list into multiple smaller lists (delace). Constructor arg: `<index-multiple>` sets stride N (creates N outlets). Send `list` to split; `bang` to re-emit last result. Use to separate stride-N elements from a list — e.g. extract odd/even indices, separate (x, y, z) triples into three streams. Inverse of interleave.

gridpanel.digest: Simple utility for drawing a UI grid.
gridpanel.use_when: UI utility — draws a grid (lines/cells) for visual layout. Configure with `divisions <n>` (uniform), or `xdivisions <n>` / `ydivisions <n>` independently; `linewidth <pixels>` sets stroke; `idlemousing <int>` toggles mouse-tracking on hover. Use to align controls or visualise a grid in custom UIs.

harmonics~.use_when: Bank of harmonic sinusoidal oscillators — fundamental + integer multiples with per-partial amplitudes. Constructor args: `<fundamental> <amplitude-list>`. Runtime: send `float` to retune fundamental, `list <amplitudes>` to set per-partial amps, `first-amplitude <amp>` for fundamental amp, `noisiness <amount>` for stochastic detuning, `wave <shape> <max-sinusoids>` to load a preset waveform's harmonic series. `clear` zeros amps. Use for harmonic additive synthesis; cheaper than N parallel cycle~ + amplitude scaling.

interleave.digest: Interleave (lace) separate list elements into a single list
interleave.use_when: Lace separate list elements into a single interleaved list. Constructor arg: `<number-of-inlets>` sets how many input streams to interleave. Use to combine parallel streams into one stride-N list. Inverse of deinterleave.

lcm.use_when: Least common multiple of integer inputs. Constructor arg: `<list-of-integers>` for static input; runtime `int` / `list` to evaluate; `bang` to re-emit last result; `tellmeeverything` for state dump. Use for rhythmic/cyclic-pattern length calculations — e.g. when do two polyrhythms align (lcm of bar lengths), or how long until a phase pattern repeats.

list-interpolate.use_when: Linear interpolation between two lists element-by-element. Constructor args: `<steps> <max-list-length>` set interpolation resolution and capacity. Runtime: send two lists (one per inlet) to seed; `float` (0.–1.) drives the interpolation; `steps <n>` retunes resolution; `zeropad <flag>` toggles padding shorter lists with zeros vs. wrapping. Use to crossfade between parameter lists (preset interpolation, morph between configurations).

migrator.digest: Spectral/harmonic transformation, based on David Wessel's "migrate" abstraction
migrator.use_when: Spectral/harmonic transformation based on David Wessel's 'migrate' abstraction — moves partials between target spectra over time. Constructor args: `<stddev> <num_oscillators> <base_amplitude>`. Runtime: `list <freq/amp pairs>` sets target spectrum, `forcefeed <pairs>` immediately shifts to a new target, `nOsc_smooth <list>` controls oscillator-count smoothing; `@fade` (int frames) controls transition smoothness, `@nOsc` caps oscillator count, `@oscamp` is base amplitude, `@output` toggles synthesis. Use for harmonic-shifting transformations between target spectra — useful for evolving timbres, gradual morphs.

opensoundcontrol.digest: Translate between Max data and buffers in CNMAT's "OpenSoundControl" ("OSC") network protocol.
opensoundcontrol.use_when: Translate between Max data and CNMAT's OpenSoundControl buffers. **DEPRECATED** — use udpsend/udpreceive (native) for transport, and the odot package (o.expr, o.route, o.message) for OSC bundle parsing/manipulation. Legacy interface: `FullPacket <bytes> <pointer>` receives raw OSC, `OSCTimeTag <secs> <fraction>` sets timetag, `openbundle` / `closebundle` (or `[`/`]`) wrap bundles; `errorreporting <on/off>`, `readtypestrings <0/1>`, `printcontents`, `debug` for diagnostics.

osc-route.digest: Dispatch OSC messages according to an address hierarchy stripping off the portion of the address that matched
osc-route.use_when: Dispatch OSC messages by address hierarchy, stripping matched prefix. **DEPRECATED** — use odot's o.route which handles full OSC bundles with types and timetags. Legacy: constructor args list addresses, runtime `set <outlet> <address>` reconfigures; `allmessages` enumerates patterns. For simple cases the native `route` works on plain symbols; for full OSC use odot.

osc-schedule.digest: Schedule delivery of an OSC packet/bundle at a future time using an encoded timestamp
osc-schedule.use_when: Schedule OSC packet/bundle delivery at a future time via OSC timetag. **DEPRECATED** — use odot's o.schedule. Legacy: `FullPacket <bytes> <pointer>` queues a packet, `reset` clears the queue; `@maxdelay` (ms ceiling), `@packetsize` (ceiling), `@queuesize` (queue depth), `@precision` (timing tolerance) tune behaviour.

osc-timetag.digest: Generate, transform, and manipulate OSC timetags.
osc-timetag.use_when: Generate, transform, and manipulate OSC timetags. **DEPRECATED** — use odot's o.timetag. Legacy: `OSCTimeTag <int1> <int2>` constructs a timetag, `FullPacket <bytes> <pointer>` extracts a timetag from a bundle, `now` returns current time, `immediate` returns the immediate-execution timetag; `@from` / `@to` / `@op` define conversion behaviour.

oscillators~.use_when: Bank of tabulated function oscillators — many wavetable oscillators in one object with per-voice frequency and amplitude. Constructor args: `<buffer-name> <frequency/amplitude-duples>` define the wavetable and initial bank state. Runtime: `list <freq/amp pairs>` updates bank, `set <buffer-index>` selects a wavetable buffer~, `noglissbirthmode <flag>` toggles glide-on-birth behaviour, `clear` zeros voices. Use for oscbank-style additive synthesis with arbitrary waveforms; cheaper than N parallel wave~ objects.

peqbank~.use_when: Parametric EQ bank — many biquads in one object. Constructor accepts variable args defining initial bands. Runtime: `peq <F0> <Bw> <G0> <G> <GB>` adds a parametric peak (centre freq, bandwidth, initial DC gain, peak gain, edge gain — all in dB); `lowpass` / `highpass` add corresponding shelves; `bank <list>` bulk-sets bands; `biquads <a0> <a1> <a2> <b1> <b2>` for raw coefficients; `maxelem <n>` caps active filter count; `fast` toggles approximations; `clear` / `reset` wipe state. Use for multi-band parametric EQ; cheaper and easier to drive than serial biquad chains.

poly.bus~.digest: Receive audio over a virtual bus connection of a named
poly.bus~.use_when: Receive audio over a virtual named bus. Constructor args: `<name> <num-channels>`. Pairs with poly.send~. Use for cleanly routing audio between sibling poly~ instances or for late-binding audio connections without patchcords — one-to-many fanout where any number of poly.bus~ receivers can subscribe to a single named bus.

poly.send~.use_when: Send audio over a virtual named bus to all matching poly.bus~ receivers. `@name` (symbol) sets bus name, `@channel` selects channel index. Pairs with poly.bus~. Use for one-to-many audio fanout without explicit cords; works across poly~ instance boundaries.

printit.digest: print out information that is normally hidden by Max
printit.use_when: Print info that's normally hidden by Max — internal object state, attribute details, list contents at full precision. Optional constructor arg: `<label>` prepends a prefix. Pass through `bang`, `int`, `float`, `list`, `anything` — all are printed. Use for debugging when native `print` truncates or omits detail.

randdist.use_when:
Random distributions sampler — many distribution types in one object. Constructor args: ` ` set initial type. Runtime: send distribution-name messages with their parameters (e.g. `gaussian `, `bernoulli

`, `beta `, `binomial

`, `cauchy `, `chisq `, `dirichlet `, `erlang `, `bivariate_gaussian `); `bang` draws a sample; `distlist ` draws a batch. Use as a one-stop random-number source instead of separate objects per distribution.

rbfi.use_when: Radial Basis Function Interpolator (UI). Designs N-dimensional interpolations between named points. Add points with `add_point <name> <coords> <inner_radius> <outer_radius> <locked> <rgb>`; `delete_point`, `move`, `inner_radius`, `outer_radius` modify; `clear` empties; `dump` / `getnumpoints` query state; `pop` removes last. Many display attrs: `@always_draw_circles`, `@always_draw_labels`, `@bgcolor`, `@bordercolor`, `@font_size`, `@line_width_float`, `@enable_multiple_crosshairs`, plus more. Use for preset/parameter interpolation in 2D space — drag a cursor between named presets and get smoothly weighted parameter blends.

res-transform.use_when: Transformations for resonance models — manipulate (frequency, amplitude, decay) triples for use with resonators~. Constructor arg: `<list-name>` (optional). Many transforms: `attenuate <dB>` and `attenuation-spread <spread>` for amp scaling, `cluster-size <int>` for grouping, `evenfrequencyscale <scalar>` / `evengainscale <scalar>` for global scaling, `formant-form <list>` / `frequency-add <list>` / `frequency-around <window>` for spectral shaping; `clear` empties, `bang` re-emits. Use for shifting, scaling, or filtering resonance lists before driving resonators~.

resdisplay.use_when: UI display for resonance models — visualise (frequency, amplitude, decay) triples as a graph. `list` updates display; `clear` empties; `displayrange <low_hz> <high_hz>` zooms frequency axis; `sinusoids` toggles sinusoidal display mode; `bang` redraws. Configure axes with `@freqmin` / `@freqmax`, `@ampmin` / `@ampmax` (linear) and `@ampmin_log` / `@ampmax_log`, plus colour attrs (`@bgcolor`, `@bordercolor`, `@datacolor`). Use as a debugging/editing UI alongside resonators~.

resonators~.use_when: Resonant filter bank — drives many parallel 2-pole resonators from (freq, amp, decay) triples. Constructor args: `<smooth> <list>`. Runtime: `list <triples>` updates bank, `outputgain <gains>` scales per-resonator output, `squelch` zeros all amps, `bang` re-emits state, `tellmeeverything` dumps state, `clear` empties. Excitation: signal in (audio drive) or float (impulse with that amplitude). Use for modelling struck/excited resonant bodies (bells, drums, plates) with measured or synthesised resonance models.

roughness.use_when: Compute psychoacoustic roughness from sinusoidal partial data. Send `list` of partials, or `process_SDIF` to operate on an SDIF stream; `SDIFtime <seconds>` selects an SDIF frame; `scIndex` queries scale-component index; `set` updates state; `set-cbw-type <int>` selects critical-bandwidth model. Use for perceived-dissonance descriptor on detailed partial data — more capable than zsa.roughness when SDIF data is involved.

sdif-buffer.digest: Store an SDIF stream in Max.
sdif-buffer.use_when: In-memory SDIF stream container. Constructor arg: `<buffer-name>`. Load with `read-stream-number <filename> <stream>`; inspect with `framelist <filename>`, `streamlist <filename>`, `print` / `printall`; modify with `change-frametype <symbol>` / `change-streamID <int>` / `timeshift <seconds>`; `clear` empties; `debug <on/off>` for diagnostics. Use as the substrate for SDIF analysis data (resonance models, spectral envelopes) loaded from disk; pair with sdif-tuples to read and sdif-listpoke to write.

sdif-fileinfo.digest: Report information about the streams in an SDIF file
sdif-fileinfo.use_when: Report stream metadata about an SDIF file on disk. Constructor arg: `<buffer-name>`. `scan <filename>` reads metadata, `bang` re-emits, `clear` resets. Use to inspect SDIF files (stream count, frame types, time ranges) before loading them into a buffer.

sdif-info.use_when: Report metadata about a loaded SDIF stream in an sdif-buffer. Constructor arg: `<buffer-name>`. `set <buffername>` retargets, `bang` queries, `tellmeeverything` dumps state. Use for stream-aware analysis pipelines — confirm the stream's frame type and timing before reading tuples.

sdif-listpoke.digest: Poke (write) data into an SDIF-buffer with Max lists
sdif-listpoke.use_when: Write Max-list data into an sdif-buffer. Constructor arg: `<buffer-name>`. `listpoke <values>` writes, `newmatrix` / `matrixtype` / `numcolumns` configure shape, `time <seconds>` sets timestamp, `set` updates buffer reference, `errorreporting <int>` for diagnostics. Use to programmatically construct SDIF data from Max-side lists.

sdif-ranges.digest: Report ranges of values in an SDIF-buffer
sdif-ranges.use_when: Report value ranges (min/max per dimension) of data in an sdif-buffer. Constructor arg: `<buffer-name>`. `column-range <col> <matrix-type>` queries one column, `column-ranges <matrix-type>` queries all, `max-columns` reports column count, `set <buffername>` retargets. Use for autoscaling visualisations or normalising SDIF feature streams to a known range.

sdif-tuples.digest: Pull data out of an SDIF-buffer into Max lists
sdif-tuples.use_when: Pull data tuples out of an sdif-buffer as Max lists. Constructor arg: `<buffer-name>`. `columns <col1> <col2?> <col3?>` selects which columns to extract, `interp <type>` chooses interpolation between frames, `direction <int>` plays forward/backward, `concatenate <id>` merges streams, `max_rows <n>` caps output, `outputinterval <ms>` paces emission, `reltime <phase>` reads at a relative time, `print` debug-dumps. Use as the read side of an SDIF pipeline — extract per-frame analysis data for control or resynthesis.

sinusoids~.use_when: Bank of sinusoidal oscillators with per-partial frequency, amplitude, and phase. Constructor args: `<bwe> <freq/amp pairs>` (bwe = bandwidth-enhanced flag, then initial bank). Runtime: `list <pairs>` updates bank, `clear` zeros, `verbose` toggles diagnostics, `tellmeeverything` dumps state. Compare to harmonics~ (only integer-multiple partials) and oscillators~ (wavetable-based). Use for additive synthesis of arbitrary inharmonic spectra; cheaper than N parallel cycle~ objects.

slipOSC.use_when: SLIP-encoded OSC over serial. `FullPacket <bytes> <pointer>` sends a raw OSC packet over serial; receives accept `int` / `list` of bytes for SLIP framing; `printcontents`, `debug`, `errorreporting` for diagnostics. Use to send/receive OSC over a serial port (Arduino, microcontrollers) using the SLIP framing convention.

sphY.use_when: Spherical harmonics evaluator (Y_l^m functions). `eval <theta> <phi>` evaluates at a (declination, azimuth) point; `index` queries indexing; `@order` sets the spherical-harmonic order. Use for ambisonic encoding/decoding math, 3D directivity patterns, or any application needing Y_l^m functions.

thread.fork.digest: Thread-level parallelism for event domain computation
thread.fork.use_when: Fork message processing onto a separate thread. Pass through `bang`, `int`, `float`, `anything` — they continue on a worker thread. Pair with thread.join. Use to move expensive control-rate computations off the scheduler thread (computational graph processing, large list operations) without blocking audio.

thread.join.digest: Thread-level parallelism for event domain computation
thread.join.use_when: Join messages from a forked thread back to the main scheduler. Pass through `bang`, `int`, `float`, `anything` — they return to scheduler thread. Use as the merge point for thread.fork results before any further main-thread processing (UI updates, audio control).

thread.which.digest: Report information about which thread a given message lives in, and forward it.
thread.which.use_when: Report which thread (scheduler vs other) a message is currently in, then forward it. Use for debugging threading issues — confirm you're on the expected thread before a thread-sensitive operation. Pass through `bang`, `int`, `float`, `list`, `anything`; output includes thread identifier.

threefates.use_when: Three-stream stochastic event generator (probabilistic note/event distributor). Constructor args: `<max-partials> <num-params>` size the streams. Runtime: send `list` to seed/update; `tellmeeverything` dumps state. Use for compositional event generation with weighted alternation between three streams — useful for triadic/three-voice probabilistic counterpoint.

trampoline.use_when: Bounce a message back to the scheduler — defer with low overhead. Optional constructor arg: `<num-inlets-and-outlets>` (parallel pairs). Pass through `bang`, `int`, `float`, `list`, `anything`, `FullPacket` — output is queued for the next scheduler tick. Use as a lightweight defer-style alternative when you need to break a callback chain or escape a recursive context without `defer` overhead.

trend-report.digest: Keep track of trends in a stream of numbers and report about each one after it finishes.
trend-report.use_when: Track trends in a number stream (rising/falling runs) and report each completed trend. Constructor arg: `<tolerance>` sets the minimum delta to count as a trend continuation. Send `int` / `float`; output reports each completed monotonic run (length, direction, range). Use for change detection at the granularity of monotonic runs rather than individual samples — e.g. note when a controller stops climbing and starts falling.

vsnapshot~.digest: Output a list representing the signal vector associated with a given signal's input, similar to
vsnapshot~.use_when: Output one signal vector as a list (vector-rate snapshot). Constructor arg: `<interval-ms>`. `start` / `stop` enable/disable polling; `bang` polls once on demand; `int` retunes interval. Like snapshot~ but emits the whole vector rather than a single sample. Use to capture a vector of audio samples for downstream list-domain processing (e.g. FFT analysis frame, transient buffer).

waveguide~.use_when: Digital waveguide — recursive delay-line model for plucked-string and similar 1D physical models. `@nodes`, `@edges`, `@inputs`, `@outputs`, `@size` configure topology. Define connections with `connect <designator> <instructions>`; configure with `set <designator> <coefficients|range|value>`; `reset` clears state; signal in drives excitation. Use for Karplus-Strong-style synthesis with proper waveguide structure (separate forward and backward travelling waves) — more capable than tapin~/tapout~ feedback rolls.

xydisplay.digest: Create a 2d path and traverse it
xydisplay.use_when: 2D path UI — create and traverse a path in (x, y) space. `add_point <coords>` / `movepoint <index> <x> <y>` build the path; `findnearest <x> <y>` / `findnearestx <x>` / `findnearesty <y>` query proximity; `moveselected <x> <y>` moves selected; `clear` empties; `dump` reports state; `bang` retraces. Many display attrs: `@bgcolor`, `@bordercolor`, `@pointcolor`, `@drawpoint`, `@drawlabels`, `@connect_points`, `@locked`, `@parameter_enable`. Use for gestural control surfaces where dragging follows a designed trajectory.

CidLinkClient.digest: Send messages to a Cid network infrastructure
CidLinkClient.use_when: Send messages to a Cid network infrastructure.

CidLinkServer.digest: Server of a Cid network infrastructure
CidLinkServer.use_when: Server of a Cid network infrastructure.

CH-Client.digest: Connect to the Collab-Hub Server. Client Module for Collab-Hub.
CH-Client.use_when: Connect to the Collab-Hub Server. Client Module for Collab-Hub.

CH-Controls.digest: Observe Published Controls on the Collab-Hub Server.
CH-Controls.use_when: Observe Published Controls on the Collab-Hub Server.

CH-Events.digest: Observe Published Events on the Collab-Hub Server.
CH-Events.use_when: Observe Published Events on the Collab-Hub Server.
