fftz.bthresher~.use_when: Per-bin feedback with amplitude thresholds. Signal in left inlet; right two inlets accept threshold and damping signals (3 inlets total). Set per-bin parameters with `bin <bin#> <damping> <threshold>` or `alldamp <damping>` / `allthresh <threshold>` to set globally; `inf_hold <flag>` freezes feedback indefinitely; `max_hold <ms>` caps hold time. Use for buildup/feedback effects on individual frequency bins (loud bins ring, quiet bins stay clean) — native equivalent is a pfft~ with per-bin gate + delay loop, substantial.

fftz.burrow~.use_when: Cross-filter A's spectrum by B's spectral magnitude (B 'burrows into' A). 4 inlets: A signal, B signal, threshold signal, mult signal. `invert <flag>` swaps the cross-filter polarity (filter where B is quiet rather than loud); `mute <flag>` zeros output. Use for spectral cross-filtering effects where one sound shapes another's spectrum — no native equivalent.

fftz.cavoc27~.use_when: 27-rule cellular-automaton-driven oscillator-bank spectrum. Signal in left, signal out. Tunable via `@density` (initial CA density 0.–1.), `@holdtime` (ms between CA steps); messages `freeze <flag>` to halt evolution, `manual <flag>` to step on demand, `interpolate <flag>` for crossfaded transitions, `noalias <flag>` to suppress aliasing, `oscbank <flag>` to use oscbank resynthesis, `capture_spectrum <flag>` to seed CA from input audio. Use to generate evolving emergent spectral textures with no input audio required.

fftz.cavoc~.use_when: Binary cellular-automaton-driven spectrum. Tunable via `@density` (initial density), `@holdtime` (CA step rate); messages `rule <list>` to set CA rule, `bottomfreq <hz>` and `topfreq <hz>` to constrain the active band, `retune <minmult> <maxmult>` to retune the CA grid frequencies, `external_trigger <flag>` to step CA from input transients. Cousin of cavoc27~ with simpler binary state. Use for synthetic CA-driven spectra with rule-level control.

fftz.centerring~.use_when: Frequency-independent ring modulation in the spectral domain — modulates each bin's amplitude rather than the time-domain product. `randphases` randomises bin phases, `zerophases` zeros them, `seed <value>` sets the random seed. Use for spectral AM that doesn't introduce sidebands the way time-domain ring modulation does.

fftz.codepend~.use_when: Frequency-domain block convolution. `pad <flag>` enables zero-padding for non-circular convolution; `invert <flag>` swaps to spectral division (deconvolution-lite). Use for long impulse responses where time-domain convolution gets expensive — different latency/CPU profile than HIRT's multiconvolve~ (realtime) and bufconvolve~ (offline).

fftz.cross~.use_when: Cross-synthesis with thresholding — replace A's spectrum with B's where B is above threshold, otherwise keep A. `autonorm <flag>` toggles automatic gain normalisation. Use for selective spectral grafting between two sources without manual gain matching.

fftz.dentist~.use_when: Keeps the N strongest bins per frame, zeros the rest. `toothcount <int>` sets N (number of 'teeth' kept); `scramble` randomises which bins survive on next frame; `interpolate_singles <flag>` smooths transitions between selected bins; `ramptime <flag>` adds amplitude ramping; `setstate <list>` / `showstate` save and inspect bin selection. Use for radical spectral simplification — vocoder-like sparsification or generative spectral textures.

fftz.disarrain~.use_when: Spectrum scrambler with smooth crossfaded bin reordering. `bang` to rescramble; `fadetime <ms>` controls crossfade length; `setstate <pattern>` / `isetstate <pattern>` (instant) / `showstate` save and inspect the permutation; `killfade` cancels in-progress fade. `@maxfreq` caps the scrambled band. Use for evolving spectral mangling with smooth transitions between rearrangements.

fftz.disarray~.use_when: Spectrum scrambler — randomly permutes bins each frame. `bang` to rescramble; `setstate <pattern>` / `showstate` save/inspect the permutation; `reset_shuffle` returns to identity; `topfreq <hz>` caps the scrambled band; `switch_count <int>` controls scramble depth. Cheaper but more discontinuous than disarrain~ — use when you want hard-edged spectral randomisation.

fftz.drown~.use_when: Aggressive spectral noise reduction — zeros bins below a threshold. `adaptive <list>` toggles adaptive thresholding (the threshold tracks signal level). Use when traditional broadband NR isn't sufficient — accepts brutal artefacts in exchange for deep silencing. Compare to fftz.scrape~ which uses a learned noise template.

fftz.enrich~.use_when: Additive synthesis driven by an arbitrary waveform from a buffer~. Required: `@buffername <symbol>` (or `setbuf <name>`) — names a buffer~ holding the source waveform. Tune the harmonic range with `@lofreq` and `@hifreq`. `binstats` reports per-bin status. Use as a versatile additive synth voice without rolling your own oscbank.

fftz.ether~.use_when: Spectral compositing — for each bin, take whichever of two inputs has greater magnitude. `invert <flag>` flips to take the lesser (= vacancy~ behaviour). Use to combine two sounds where each occupies different spectral regions; complementary to fftz.vacancy~.

fftz.leaker~.use_when: Bin-level crossfader between two spectra. `upsieve` and `downsieve` shift the crossfade pattern up or down through bins; `randsieve` randomises which bins come from which source. Use for granular spectral morphing where you control the mix bin-by-bin via control or by the sieve patterns.

fftz.loopsea~.use_when: Loops individual FFT bins independently with separate loop points and rates per bin. `acquire_sample` to capture; `randspeed <min ms> <max ms>` randomises per-bin loop sizes; `randtransp <min> <max>` randomises per-bin transpositions; `flat_transpose` resets transpositions; `oscbank <flag>` toggles oscbank resynthesis; `printloops` lists current loop state. Use for spectral textural drones from short input — distinctive 'shimmer' sound.

fftz.mindwarp~.use_when: Warps the spectral envelope (formant-shifting style). Two control signals on additional inlets shape the warp. Use to transform timbre/formants while pitch is unaffected — vocal/instrument 'monster voice' or formant shifts. Minimal control surface beyond standard fft params and signal-driven warp.

fftz.morphine~.use_when: Spectral morphing between two input sources. `transition <flag>` toggles transition mode for slow morphs vs instantaneous interpolation. Use for spectral transitions between sounds — bin-by-bin weighted crossfade with magnitude/phase control.

fftz.multiq~.use_when: Multi-band parametric EQ in the spectral domain — many EQ bands cheaply. Drive band parameters via signal inputs. Use when you need many EQ bands at lower CPU than serial biquad chains.

fftz.pileup~.use_when: Spectral persistence — each bin decays toward its running maximum, accumulating rather than replacing. `lowfreq <hz>` and `hifreq <hz>` limit the active band; `inverse_gain <gain>` controls inverse persistence; `clear` resets the buffer; `oscbank <flag>` for oscbank resynthesis; `performance <mode>` switches behaviour modes. Use for spectral freezes, infinite reverb-like sustains, and slowly evolving spectral clouds.

fftz.pvcompand~.use_when: Per-bin compander — compress or expand amplitude separately for each frequency bin. `normalize <flag>` for output normalisation. Use for frequency-selective dynamics: e.g. compressing only loud bins to preserve quiet detail (impossible with broadband compressors).

fftz.pvgrain~.use_when: Spectral granulator — grains are individual FFT frames rather than time-domain windows. `framegrains <count>` sets grains per frame; `probability <0.–1.>` controls grain emission likelihood; `basefreq <hz>` and `topfreq <hz>` constrain the active band; `printchan <ch>` debug-prints one channel. Use for textural spectral granulation distinct from time-domain granular.

fftz.pvharm~.use_when: Phase-vocoder harmonizer — pitch-shifts without time-stretching. `@lowfreq` / `@hifreq` set the shifted band; `compressor <flag>` toggles built-in dynamics; `framestop <amp>` halts processing below an amplitude threshold; `osclimit <count>` caps simultaneous oscillators; `oscnt` queries current oscillator count. Use for clean pitch shifting on harmonic material; preserves timing.

fftz.pvoc~.use_when: Phase vocoder — independent control of time and pitch. `@lowfreq` / `@highfreq` constrain the analysis band. Standard pvoc operation: drive time-stretch from a phasor~ swept over an analysis buffer, drive pitch from a transposition factor. No native Max PV equivalent; use for time-stretching or pitch-shifting where the two must be decoupled.

fftz.pvtuner~.use_when: Frequency-quantised resynthesis — snaps each spectral peak to the nearest scale degree. Many built-in scale messages: `eq12` (equal-tempered), `diatonic`, `eastern`, `dominant_seventh_chord`, `added_sixth_major` / `added_sixth_minor`, plus user-defined scales. `basefreq <hz>` sets reference; `binfo` reports bin-to-pitch mapping; `@scale_interpolation` smooths between scales. Use for autotune-like quantisation of polyphonic or inharmonic material to a scale.

fftz.pvwarpb~.use_when: Buffer-driven spectral warp — same idea as pvwarp~ but the warp curve comes from a buffer~. Required: `@buffername <symbol>` naming the buffer holding the warp function. Use when the warp curve needs to be precomputed, drawn (in waveform~), or animated as buffer contents.

fftz.pvwarp~.use_when: Spectral frequency warping — maps the frequency axis through a function defined by control values. `bottomfreq <hz>` / `topfreq <hz>` set the warped band; `autofunc <min> <max>` generates an automatic warp curve. Use for inharmonic transformations, formant shifts, alien retunings.

fftz.reanimator~.use_when: Spectral texture mapping — analyses a target sound's spectrum and resynthesizes any input sound to match that spectrum. Required constructor arg: `<analysis-length>` (number of frames to capture). Workflow: `analyze` to capture target audio into the internal buffer, then play any signal through to be resynthesized as the captured texture. `freeze_and_march <speed>` plays through analysis frames at a chosen rate; `inverse <flag>` flips magnitudes; `oscbank <flag>` toggles oscbank synthesis; `framecount` and `meminfo` report state. Use for 'play X as if it were Y' spectral texture mapping.

fftz.resent~.use_when: Spectral sample buffer with per-bin playback control. `acquire_sample` records; `bin <bin#> <speed>` sets per-bin playback speed; `addspeed <speed>` and `addsphase <phase>` increment globally; `linespeed <s1> <p1> <s2> <p2>` and `linephase <b1> <p1> <b2> <p2>` interpolate across bin ranges; `bang` triggers playback. Use as a building block for stutter/freeze/replay/scrub effects in the spectral domain.

fftz.residency_buffer~.use_when: Spectral sample buffer using an external buffer~ for storage. Required: `@buffername <symbol>`. `acquire_sample` records; `bang` plays back; `calcbuf <duration>` sizes the buffer for a given recording length; `oscbank <flag>` for oscbank resynthesis; `playthrough <flag>` passes input through during record; `meminfo` reports state. Use when frames must be visualised, edited, or shared between objects.

fftz.residency~.use_when: Internal-buffer spectral sample memory. `@size` sizes the buffer (frames); `acquire_sample` / `acquire_stop` start/stop recording; `bang` plays back; `oscbank <flag>` for oscbank resynthesis; `playthrough <flag>` passes input through during record. Use for spectral freeze/stutter when you don't need a separate buffer~ object.

fftz.schmear~.use_when: Spectral smearing — blurs (low-passes) the spectrum across time. `schmimp <list>` defines a custom convolution kernel for the smear; `shift <int>` sets directional bias; `threshold <float>` ignores quiet bins; `@size` and `@interpolation` tune the smear depth. Use for cloudy washed textures, reverb-like blurring without using a reverb.

fftz.scrape~.use_when: Targeted spectral noise reduction — removes specific spectral profiles. Minimal control surface beyond standard fft params; thresholds and template are built into the algorithm. Use when fftz.drown~ is too blunt and you have a clean noise sample to subtract.

fftz.shapee~.use_when: Spectral shape transfer — imposes one signal's spectral envelope onto another's spectrum. Use for vocoder-style 'A spoken through B' effects done at the spectral-envelope level. Minimal API beyond standard fft params and the two signal inputs.

fftz.swinger~.use_when: Cross-synthesis combining magnitudes of A with phases of B (or vice versa). Use for the classic spectral cross-synthesis sound — A's pitch / phase structure with B's timbre, or vice versa.

fftz.taint~.use_when: Spectral multiplication — multiplies the spectra of A and B bin-by-bin. `invert <flag>` swaps to spectral division; `pad <gain>` adds a constant to avoid divide-by-zero in invert mode. Use for ring-modulation-like spectral interactions; sparse where both sources are sparse.

fftz.thresher~.use_when: Damped bin-level feedback — feedback only on bins above threshold, with a damping factor. `synthresh <factor>` sets feedback threshold; `transpose <factor>` retunes feedback; `oscbank <flag>` for oscbank resynthesis. Use for resonant bin-feedback that decays naturally — less prone to runaway than fftz.bthresher~.

fftz.vacancy~.use_when: Spectral compositing — for each bin, take whichever of two inputs has lesser magnitude. `invert <flag>` flips to greater (= ether~ behaviour); `rms <flag>` switches comparison metric to RMS; `swapphase <flag>` exchanges phases between inputs. Inverse of fftz.ether~. Use to extract the 'quiet' parts of two sources.

fftz.xsyn~.use_when: Cross-synthesis with separate envelope and spectrum control. Use for vocoder-style cross-synthesis where envelope and fine spectral detail come from different sources. Minimal API — drive the two inputs and let the algorithm separate envelope from spectrum.

flowSwing_audioWarp.digest: NI Grid based time-warp of sound samples
flowSwing_audioWarp.use_when: NI Grid based time-warp of sound samples.

flowSwing_envelop.digest: NI Grid based sequencer and envelop generator
flowSwing_envelop.use_when: NI Grid based sequencer and envelop generator.

flowSwing_nested.digest: Sequencer with 2 hierarchically nested NI Grids
flowSwing_nested.use_when: Sequencer with 2 hierarchically nested NI Grids.

flowSwing_noise.digest: Synthesizer with tone-to-noise control and mc based polyphony
flowSwing_noise.use_when: Synthesizer with tone-to-noise control and mc based polyphony.

flowSwing_onsetDetect.digest: Onset detection and automatic creation of warp markers
flowSwing_onsetDetect.use_when: Onset detection and automatic creation of warp markers.

flowSwing_subDiv.digest: Non Integer subdivision of a phasor
flowSwing_subDiv.use_when: Non Integer subdivision of a phasor.

fluid.ampfeature~.use_when: Realtime amplitude differential feature — outputs a slow/fast envelope difference signal used as an onset/event indicator. `@highpassfreq` (default 85 Hz) high-passes input before envelope extraction (removes DC/rumble); `@fastrampup` / `@fastrampdown` (default 1 ms) and `@slowrampup` / `@slowrampdown` (default 100 ms) tune the two envelope time constants; `@floor` (default -144 dB) sets noise gate. Send `reset` to clear envelope state. Use as a low-cost activity detector before a full spectral onset analysis — cheaper than fluid.onsetfeature~ when amplitude is enough.

fluid.ampgate~.use_when: Amplitude-based gate detector with hysteresis and lookahead. Constructor arg: `<maxsize>` (sample buffer). Tune onset/offset thresholds (`@minlengthabove` / `@minlengthbelow`, both in ms), silence handling (`@minsilencelength`), and lookahead/lookback (`@lookahead` / `@lookback`) to avoid false triggers; `@highpassfreq` (default 85 Hz) pre-filters input. `reset` clears gate state. Use when you need event boundaries (note on/off) from a level threshold without false triggers — the lookahead lets the gate confirm sustain before opening.

fluid.ampslice~.use_when: Amplitude-based slicer — detects onsets via slow/fast envelope detrending and emits slice triggers. Same envelope-time-constant attrs as fluid.ampfeature~ (`@fastrampup/down`, `@slowrampup/down`, `@highpassfreq`, `@floor`); add `@onthreshold` / `@offthreshold` (dB) for slice gating and `@minslicelength` (ms) to suppress short slices. `reset` clears state. Use for realtime onset slicing on percussive or amplitude-modulated material.

fluid.audiofilesin.use_when: Collect audio metadata from a directory (entry point for batch corpus analysis). Send `list <directory-path>` to scan; outputs file paths, sample rates, channel counts. Use as the first stage of a corpus pipeline — feed the file list into fluid.bufcompose~ to assemble a single analysis buffer or into fluid.buf*~ analysers in a loop.

fluid.audiotransport~.use_when: Realtime spectral interpolation between two sounds via optimal-transport algorithm. 1 signal in (mix of source A and B in stereo or via separate inlets — consult refpage), `@interpolation` (0.–1.) drives the morph, `@fftsettings` tunes FFT window. `reset` clears state. Use for high-quality spectral morphing between two live sources — phase-coherent, more natural-sounding than amplitude crossfading. For offline use fluid.bufaudiotransport~.

fluid.buf2list.digest: Copy the contents of a buffer~ to a list
fluid.buf2list.use_when: Copy buffer~ contents to a Max list (audio→list bridge). `@source <buffer-name>` and `@startframe` / `@startchan` define the read region; `@axis 0` reads frame-major, `@axis 1` reads channel-major. Send `bang` to copy; `buffer <name>` retargets source. Use to extract feature vectors, analysis frames, or short audio segments into list-domain processing chains.

fluid.bufampfeature~.use_when: Buffer-based amplitude differential feature — offline equivalent of fluid.ampfeature~. Same envelope attrs (`@fastrampup/down`, `@slowrampup/down`, `@highpassfreq`, `@floor`) plus buffer addressing (`@source`, `@startframe`, `@numframes`, `@startchan`, `@numchans`). `@features <buffer>` writes per-frame feature curve. `bang` runs analysis; `cancel` aborts long jobs. Use when audio is already in a buffer — typically faster and more accurate than realtime.

fluid.bufampgate~.use_when: Buffer-based amplitude gate detection — offline equivalent of fluid.ampgate~. Constructor arg: `<maxsize>`. Same gate attrs (`@minlengthabove/below`, `@lookahead/back`, `@minsilencelength`, `@highpassfreq`) plus `@indices <buffer>` to write slice-point pairs. Use to find event boundaries in a buffered audio file in one offline pass — the indices buffer gives you (start, end) pairs ready for fluid.bufcompose~ slicing.

fluid.bufampslice~.use_when: Buffer-based amplitude slicer — offline counterpart of fluid.ampslice~. Same envelope and threshold attrs plus `@indices <buffer>` for slice points and standard buffer addressing (`@source`, `@startframe/numframes`, `@startchan/numchans`). Produces a list of slice points for an entire file in one pass. Use for batch slicing where realtime accuracy isn't enough.

fluid.bufaudiotransport~.use_when: Buffer-based optimal-transport spectral morph between two buffers. `@source <bufA>`, `@destination <output>`, plus a second-source attribute (consult refpage); `@interpolation` drives morph position; `@fftsettings` and `@maxfftsize` tune analysis. Higher quality than realtime fluid.audiotransport~. Use for offline interpolation between two sounds — useful for batch generation of in-between timbres.

fluid.bufchroma~.digest: A histogram of pitch classes on a Buffer
fluid.bufchroma~.use_when: Pitch-class histogram (chroma vector) for a buffer. Constructor arg: `<numchroma>` (default 12 = chromatic); `@minfreq` / `@maxfreq` constrain analysis band, `@maxnumchroma` caps allocation, plus standard buffer addressing and `@features <buffer>` for output. Use for offline key/chord/harmonic-content analysis — feeds into corpus search by harmonic similarity.

fluid.bufcompose~.use_when: Buffer compositing — concatenate, layer, copy with sample-precise placement. `@source <buf>` and `@startframe`/`@numframes`/`@startchan`/`@numchans` define the read region; `@destination <buf>` and `@deststartframe`/`@deststartchan` define write position; `@gain` and `@destgain` mix existing destination content with source. `bang` runs; `cancel` aborts. Use as the FluCoMa swiss-army knife for assembling buffers programmatically — corpus prep, splicing, layering.

fluid.bufflatten~.use_when: Reduce a multichannel buffer to fewer channels (or mono). `@source` / `@destination`, `@axis 1` flattens channels (sum to mono), `@axis 0` flattens frames; `@startchan`/`@numchans`/`@startframe`/`@numframes` for region. Use to mono-down a stereo recording before mono-only analysis (most FluCoMa descriptors expect mono input).

fluid.bufhpss~.digest: Buffer-Based Harmonic-Percussive Source Separation Using Median Filtering
fluid.bufhpss~.use_when: Buffer-based harmonic/percussive source separation via median filtering. Constructor args: `<harmfiltersize> <percfiltersize>`. `@harmfiltersize` (default 17, frequency-axis median width — picks out horizontal harmonic ridges), `@percfiltersize` for time-axis (vertical transient ridges); `@harmthresh` and `@percthresh` set masking strength; `@maskingmode 0` (soft) / `1` (hard); `@harmonic <buf>` and `@percussive <buf>` (consult refpage) name output buffers. Use to split a recording into harmonic and percussive components offline — higher quality than realtime fluid.hpss~.

fluid.bufloudness~.digest: A Loudness and True-Peak Descriptor on a Buffer
fluid.bufloudness~.use_when: ITU-R BS.1770 loudness and true-peak descriptor on a buffer. Constructor arg: `<maxwindowsize>`. `@hopsize` (default 512) and window controls; `@kweighting 1` enables K-weighted loudness (LUFS standard); `@truepeak 1` enables true-peak detection; `@padding` adds analysis padding; standard buffer addressing. `@features <buf>` writes per-frame loudness/peak curves. Use as the standard loudness descriptor for ML features or normalisation — far more accurate than peak-amplitude.

fluid.bufmelbands~.digest: A Perceptually Spread Spectral Contour Descriptor on a Buffer
fluid.bufmelbands~.use_when: Mel-band spectral analysis on a buffer. Constructor arg: `<numbands>`. `@minfreq` / `@maxfreq` define analysis band (default 20–20000 Hz); `@maxnumbands` caps allocation; standard FFT and buffer attrs; `@features <buf>` writes per-frame mel-band curves. Use as a perceptually-spread spectral descriptor for offline analysis or as preprocessing for offline MFCC extraction.

fluid.bufmfcc~.digest: Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients as Spectral Descriptors on a Buffer
fluid.bufmfcc~.use_when: MFCC on a buffer. Constructor args: `<numcoeffs> <numbands>` (typical: 13 coefficients from 40 mel bands). `@maxnumcoeffs` and `@maxnumbands` cap allocation; `@minfreq` / `@maxfreq`; standard FFT and buffer attrs; `@features <buf>` writes the MFCC curve. Use as the standard timbral feature for offline corpus analysis — feeds into fluid.kmeans~/fluid.knnclassifier~ for similarity matching.

fluid.bufnmfcross~.digest: Resynthesise a target sound based on a source sound
fluid.bufnmfcross~.use_when: Cross-synthesis — resynthesise a target sound using bases extracted from a source. `@output <buf>` for resynthesis output; `@continuity` (default 7) constrains temporal continuity; `@iterations` (default 50) sets NMF iteration count; `@polyphony` (default 11) caps simultaneous bases; standard FFT attrs. Use for spectral hybridisation: 'play sound A using the spectral building blocks of sound B' — distinctive cross-synthesis aesthetic.

fluid.bufnmfseed~.digest: Non-Negative Double Singular Value Decomposition on a Buffer
fluid.bufnmfseed~.use_when: Non-negative double-SVD seeding for NMF — smarter initialisation than random. `@bases <buf>` and `@activations <buf>` write seed matrices; `@maxcomponents` (default 200) caps component count; `@coverage` (default 0.5) controls how much spectral variance the seed covers. Use as preprocessing for fluid.bufnmf~ when random initialisation produces unstable decompositions.

fluid.bufnmf~.digest: Buffer-Based Non-Negative Matrix Factorisation on Spectral Frames
fluid.bufnmf~.use_when: Non-negative matrix factorisation on spectral frames. `@components` (default 1) sets target rank; `@bases <buf>` and `@activations <buf>` name output matrices; `@basesmode` and `@actmode` control whether bases/activations are random-init, fixed, or seeded; `@iterations` controls NMF iteration count; standard FFT attrs. Use to decompose audio into a small number of recurring spectral templates and their activations — source separation, sound-pattern discovery, dictionary learning.

fluid.bufnoveltyfeature~.use_when: Buffer-based novelty curve. Constructor args: `<kernelsize> <filtersize>`. `@algorithm` selects novelty algorithm; `@kernelsize` (default 3) is similarity-matrix kernel size; `@filtersize` (default 1) smooths the novelty curve; standard FFT and buffer attrs; `@features <buf>` writes the novelty curve. Use to extract a continuous novelty signal from a buffer for offline visualisation, thresholding, or downstream slicing.

fluid.bufnoveltyslice~.use_when: Buffer-based novelty slicer — segments a buffer at points of structural change. Same `@algorithm` / `@kernelsize` / `@filtersize` controls as bufnoveltyfeature~ plus `@indices <buf>` for slice points and a threshold attr. Use for music-structure segmentation (sections, phrases) rather than note-level onsets — finds change-points in a similarity matrix.

fluid.bufonsetfeature~.use_when: Buffer-based spectral-difference onset feature. `@metric` (default 0) selects difference metric (L1, L2, cosine, etc.); `@filtersize` (default 5) smooths the curve; `@framedelta` controls comparison delay; standard FFT and buffer attrs; `@features <buf>` writes the curve. Use to extract the onset detection function as a curve for visualisation, custom thresholding, or sonification.

fluid.bufonsetslice~.use_when: Buffer-based spectral-difference onset slicer. Same `@metric` / `@filtersize` / `@framedelta` controls as bufonsetfeature~ plus `@indices <buf>` and a threshold attr. Use for note-level segmentation of a buffer — more accurate than amplitude-based slicing on tonal material because spectral changes catch pitched onsets that don't peak amplitudes.

fluid.bufpitch~.use_when: Pitch (f0) and confidence on a buffer. `@algorithm` (default 2) selects estimator (yin, mpc, hps); `@minfreq` (default 20 Hz) / `@maxfreq` (default 10000 Hz) constrain search; standard FFT and buffer attrs; `@features <buf>` writes per-frame (pitch, confidence) pairs. Use for offline pitch tracking — more accurate than realtime fluid.pitch~ because the algorithm can use larger windows.

fluid.bufscale~.use_when: Linear remap of buffer values to a new range. `@inputlow` / `@inputhigh` define source range; output range via destination attrs (consult refpage); `@clipping 1` clips outliers; standard buffer addressing. Use to normalise buffer amplitude or rescale a feature curve buffer to a known range before downstream processing.

fluid.bufselectevery~.digest: Extract every N samples / channels from a buffer
fluid.bufselectevery~.use_when: Decimate — extract every Nth sample/channel from a buffer. `@framehop` (default 1) sets sample stride; `@chanhop` (default 1) sets channel stride; `@destination <buf>` for output, plus standard buffer addressing. Use for downsampling without filtering or to extract one channel of a multichannel buffer.

fluid.bufselect~.digest: Select and copy specific values from a buffer
fluid.bufselect~.use_when: Extract specific samples or channels from a buffer by index. `@indices` (list of frame indices, default [-1] = all) and `@channels` (list of channel indices, default [-1] = all); `@source` / `@destination`. Use to copy out an arbitrary subset of a buffer's data — e.g. extract only frames at known slice points into a corpus buffer.

fluid.bufsinefeature~.use_when: Sinusoidal peak tracking on a buffer — extract sinusoidal partial tracks. Constructor arg: `<numpeaks>`. `@detectionthreshold` (default -96 dB) gates peak detection; `@frequency <buf>` and `@magnitude <buf>` name output buffers; `@frequnit` and `@magunit` select Hz vs MIDI vs raw and dB vs linear; standard FFT and buffer attrs. Use for harmonic analysis, partial extraction, or as input to sinusoidal resynthesis.

fluid.bufsines~.use_when: Sinusoidal modelling and resynthesis on a buffer — separates audio into sinusoidal partials and a residual. `@detectionthreshold` (default -96 dB) gates peaks; `@birthlowthreshold` / `@birthhighthreshold` control track birth (-24 / -60 dB); `@bandwidth` (default 76 cents) sets tracking width; standard FFT and buffer attrs plus output buffer attrs (sinusoids and residual — consult refpage). Use for high-quality time-stretch/pitch-shift, harmonic isolation, or to extract just the inharmonic residual.

fluid.bufspectralshape~.digest: Seven Spectral Shape Descriptors on a Buffer
fluid.bufspectralshape~.use_when: Seven spectral-shape descriptors on a buffer in one pass: centroid, spread, skewness, kurtosis, rolloff, flatness, crest. `@minfreq` / `@maxfreq` constrain band; standard FFT and buffer attrs; `@features <buf>` writes a 7-column-per-frame analysis curve. Use as a one-stop offline timbral feature extractor — much cheaper than chaining seven separate descriptors.

fluid.bufstats~.digest: Statistically summarise a time series in a Buffer
fluid.bufstats~.use_when: Reduce a per-frame feature curve in a buffer to summary statistics (mean, std, min, max, percentiles). `@low` (default 0%), `@middle` (default 50%), `@high` (default 100%) set percentile cutoffs; `@numderivs` (default 0) computes stats on derivatives too; standard buffer addressing. Use to reduce a long feature curve to a fixed-length summary vector for ML — e.g. per-slice feature → per-slice stats.

fluid.bufstft~.digest: Perform a Short-Time Fourier Transform on one channel of a buffer
fluid.bufstft~.use_when: Short-time Fourier transform on one channel of a buffer. `@inverse 0/1` selects forward/inverse; `@magnitude <buf>` writes magnitudes (consult refpage for phase output too); `@padding` adds analysis padding; standard FFT and buffer addressing attrs. Use when you need direct STFT magnitudes/phases for custom analysis without writing a pfft~ subpatcher.

fluid.bufthreaddemo~.digest: A Tutorial Object to Experiment with Multithreading in FluidBuf* Objects
fluid.bufthreaddemo~.use_when: Tutorial object demonstrating multithreading in FluidBuf* objects. Tracks job time in `@time` and result symbol in `@result`. Skip in production — use only for learning the FluCoMa threading model.

fluid.bufthresh~.use_when: Gate processor for buffers — zero values below a threshold (consult refpage for threshold attr name). `@source` / `@destination`, plus standard buffer addressing. Use to clean low-level noise from a feature curve buffer or signal buffer before downstream analysis.

fluid.buftransientslice~.use_when: Transient-based slicer on a buffer — segments at sharp transients via order-statistic filtering. `@blocksize` (default 256) sets analysis block; `@order` (default 20) is autoregressive order; `@padsize` adds boundary padding; `@clumplength` (default 25) merges nearby transients; `@minslicelength` (default 1000) suppresses short slices; `@indices <buf>` writes slice points. Use for percussive note segmentation — complements onsetslice~ (spectral) and ampslice~ (amplitude).

fluid.buftransients~.use_when: Transient extractor on a buffer — separates transient and tonal components via AR modelling. Same `@blocksize` / `@order` / `@padsize` / `@clumplength` controls as buftransientslice~ plus output buffer attrs (transients, residual — consult refpage). Use to isolate clicks/attacks for separate processing or to remove them from a recording.

fluid.chroma~.digest: A histogram of pitch classes in Realtime
fluid.chroma~.use_when: Realtime pitch-class histogram. Constructor arg: `<numchroma>`. `@minfreq` / `@maxfreq`; `@maxnumchroma` caps allocation; `@normalize 0/1`; standard FFT attrs. `reset` clears state. Output is a chroma vector signal. Use for live key/chord detection or harmonic-aware control.

fluid.concataudiofiles.use_when: Concatenate audio files into one buffer. `@destination <buf>` names the output; send `list <file-paths>` to concatenate. Use as a corpus-prep utility — combine many short files into one long buffer with known segment boundaries (track the boundaries separately for indexing).

fluid.datasetquery~.use_when: Query/filter a FluidDataSet. Build query with `addcolumn <int>` (include a column), `addrange <start> <count>` (include a range), `filter <col> <condition> <value>` (predicate), `and <col> <condition> <value>` (chain conditions), `limit <n>` (cap result size); `clear` resets the query. Use to extract subsets of a dataset (e.g. all points within a feature range) before clustering or classification.

fluid.dataset~.digest: A set of data associated with identifiers.
fluid.dataset~.use_when: Dictionary-like container for ML feature vectors keyed by identifier. Constructor arg: `<name>`. Add/remove with `addpoint <id> <buf>` / `deletepoint <id>` (each point is one row, length determined by buffer size); `frombuffer <buf> <transpose> <labelset>` bulk-loads from a buffer; `dump` for full state; `cols` reports column count; `clear` empties. Use as the data substrate for FluCoMa's ML algorithms (kmeans, knn, mlp, pca, umap) — replaces ad-hoc coll/dict structures.

fluid.gain~.use_when: Realtime buffered gain change. `@gain` (default 1.0) sets multiplier. Use only inside FluCoMa pipelines (when you specifically want FluCoMa's threading-aware gain) — for general gain use native `*~` or `live.gain~`.

fluid.grid~.digest: Constrain a 2D DataSet into a Grid.
fluid.grid~.use_when: Constrain a 2D dataset to a regular grid. `@axis` selects which axis to grid first (0 or 1); `@extent` sets grid resolution; `@oversample` increases grid density. `fittransform <src> <dst>` fits and writes; `refer <name>` references an existing dataset by name. Use to snap clustered/embedded points to grid cells for sequencer-like or pad-like corpus browsing.

fluid.hpss~.digest: Harmonic-Percussive Source Separation Using Median Filtering
fluid.hpss~.use_when: Realtime harmonic/percussive source separation via median filtering. Constructor args: `<harmfiltersize> <percfiltersize>`. Same `@harmfiltersize` (default 17), `@harmthresh`, `@maskingmode` controls as fluid.bufhpss~. Use for live separation of pitched and percussive content. For higher quality offline use fluid.bufhpss~.

fluid.jit.plotter.use_when: Jitter-accelerated 2D plotter for very large datasets. 2 inlets / 2 outlets (UI events). Configure with `colorscheme <name>`, `bgcolor <r> <g> <b> <a>`, `panfactor <factor>`; mark a point with `highlight <id>` and `highlightscale <scale>`; `clear` empties. Use when fluid.plotter is too slow for datasets with thousands of points — visualising a UMAP embedding of a corpus, or scatter-plotting hundreds of slices.

fluid.kdtree~.digest: Efficient lookup of data using a k-d tree.
fluid.kdtree~.use_when: K-d tree for efficient nearest-neighbour lookup on a FluidDataSet. Constructor arg: `<name>`. `fit <dataset>` builds the tree; `knearest <buf> <k?>` finds k nearest neighbours of a query buffer; `knearestdist <buf> <k?>` returns distances too; `@numneighbours` (default 1) and `@radius` set defaults; `cols` / `dump` / `clear` for inspection. Use when you need fast NN queries on a large dataset (corpus browsing by feature similarity) — much faster than fluid.dist for large N.

fluid.kmeans~.use_when: K-means clustering on a FluidDataSet. Constructor arg: `<name>`. `fit <dataset>` learns clusters; `fitpredict <dataset> <labelset>` learns and assigns each point to its cluster; `fittransform <src> <dst>` writes per-point distance-to-centroids; `@numclusters` (default 4), `@maxiter` (default 100). Use to group similar items in a corpus into k clusters — basic unsupervised classification.

fluid.knnclassifier~.use_when: K-nearest-neighbours classifier. Constructor arg: `<name>`. `fit <dataset> <labelset>` trains on labelled data; `predict <dataset> <labelset>` classifies new points; `@numneighbours` (default 3), `@weight 1` enables distance-weighted voting; `load <dict>` restores a saved model. Use for labelled-corpus classification — given a feature vector, predict which class it belongs to.

fluid.knnregressor~.use_when: K-nearest-neighbours regression. Constructor arg: `<name>`. `fit <source> <target>` trains; `predict <source> <target>` predicts continuous values; `@numneighbours` (default 3), `@weight 1` enables distance weighting. Use to predict continuous values (synthesis parameters) from a feature vector blended from k nearest training points — feature-driven cross-synthesis.

fluid.labelset~.digest: A set of labels associated with identifiers.
fluid.labelset~.use_when: Container for string labels keyed by identifier. Constructor arg: `<name>`. `addlabel <id> <label>` / `deletelabel <id>` to populate; `getids <labelset>` queries; `cols` / `dump` / `clear` for inspection. Use alongside fluid.dataset~ to attach class labels to feature vectors for supervised ML.

fluid.list2buf.digest: Copy a list to a buffer~
fluid.list2buf.use_when: Copy a Max list into a buffer~. `@destination <buf>`, `@startframe` / `@startchan` for write position; `@autosize 1` resizes destination to fit; `@axis` selects frame-major vs channel-major write. Send `list <values>` to copy. Use as a bridge from list-domain data (feature vectors, control sequences) into audio-domain processing.

fluid.loudness~.digest: A Loudness and True-Peak Descriptor in Realtime
fluid.loudness~.use_when: Realtime ITU-R BS.1770 loudness and true-peak. Constructor arg: `<maxwindowsize>`. `@windowsize` (default 1024), `@hopsize` (default 512), `@kweighting 1` (LUFS), `@truepeak 1`, `@select` lets you pick output channels (`loudness`, `peak`, or both). Use as the standard loudness descriptor for live monitoring or feature extraction — far more accurate than RMS / peak.

fluid.mds~.use_when: Multidimensional scaling — non-linear dimensionality reduction preserving pairwise distances. `@numdimensions` (default 2) sets target dimensionality; `@distancemetric 1` selects Euclidean (other values: alternative metrics). `fittransform <source> <dest>` fits and writes; `refer <name>` references existing dataset. Use to visualise a high-dim feature space in 2D/3D where local distances matter.

fluid.melbands~.digest: A Perceptually Spread Spectral Contour Descriptor
fluid.melbands~.use_when: Realtime mel-band spectral descriptor. Constructor arg: `<numbands>`. `@minfreq` (default 20), `@maxfreq` (default 20000); `@normalize 1` for normalised output; `@maxnumbands` caps allocation; standard FFT attrs. Use for live perceptually-spaced spectral analysis.

fluid.mfcc~.digest: Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients as Spectral Descriptors in Realtime
fluid.mfcc~.use_when: Realtime MFCC. Constructor args: `<numcoeffs> <numbands>` (typical: 13 coeffs from 40 bands). Same `@minfreq`/`@maxfreq`/`@maxnum*` controls as fluid.melbands~ plus standard FFT attrs. Use for live timbral feature extraction — feeds into fluid.knnclassifier~ / fluid.mlpclassifier~ for live classification.

fluid.mlpclassifier~.use_when: Multi-layer perceptron classifier. Constructor arg: `<name>`. `fit <dataset> <labelset>` trains; `predict <dataset> <labelset>` classifies; `@hiddenlayers [3 3]` (default) defines layer sizes; `@activation 2` (sigmoid by default); `@learnrate 0.01`, `@momentum 0.5`, `@batchsize 50`, `@maxiter 1000` are standard SGD knobs. `load <dict>` restores a saved model. Use for non-linear supervised classification when k-NN's decision boundaries are too coarse.

fluid.mlpregressor~.use_when: Multi-layer perceptron regression. Constructor arg: `<name>`. `fit <source> <target>` trains a non-linear mapping between two datasets; `predict <source> <target>` evaluates. Same `@hiddenlayers`, `@activation`, `@learnrate`, `@momentum 0.9` (default), `@batchsize`, `@maxiter` controls as mlpclassifier. Use for non-linear feature-to-control mappings — ML-driven cross-synthesis, gesture-to-parameter learning.

fluid.nmffilter~.digest: Realtime Non-Negative Matrix Factorisation with Fixed Bases
fluid.nmffilter~.use_when: Realtime NMF filtering — uses pre-trained bases to filter incoming audio. Constructor arg: `<maxcomponents>`. `@bases <buf>` (required) names the pre-trained bases buffer; `@iterations` (default 10) sets per-frame NMF iterations; `@maxcomponents` (default 20). Use for live source separation when bases are learned offline (via fluid.bufnmf~) and applied to new audio.

fluid.nmfmatch~.digest: Realtime Non-Negative Matrix Factorisation with Fixed Bases
fluid.nmfmatch~.use_when: Realtime NMF matching — outputs activation strength of pre-trained bases on incoming audio. Same args/attrs as fluid.nmffilter~ but the output is per-base activation rather than filtered audio. Use to detect 'how much of each template is present' in live audio — pattern recognition, instrument identification by spectral signature.

fluid.nmfmorph~.use_when: Spectral morph between sounds via NMF bases. `@source <buf>` and a second-source attr (consult refpage); `@activations <buf>` provides the base-activation pattern; `@interpolation` (0.–1.) drives morph; `@autoassign 1` auto-pairs bases by similarity; standard FFT attrs. Use for spectral morphing constrained to a learned dictionary of components — more controllable than free spectral morphing.

fluid.normalize~.use_when: Linearly normalise a FluidDataSet to `[@min, @max]` (default [0, 1]). Constructor arg: `<name>`. `fit <dataset>` learns range; `fittransform <src> <dst>` learns and writes; `inversetransform <src> <dst>` reverses. Use to rescale feature columns before ML — required preprocessing for many algorithms.

fluid.noveltyfeature~.use_when: Realtime novelty curve. Constructor args: `<kernelsize> <filtersize>`. Same `@algorithm` / `@kernelsize` / `@filtersize` controls as fluid.bufnoveltyfeature~ plus standard FFT attrs. Use for live detection of structural change (section transitions) rather than note onsets.

fluid.noveltyslice~.use_when: Realtime novelty-based slicer. Same controls as fluid.noveltyfeature~ plus a threshold attr. Use for live segmentation at structural boundaries — phrase or section endpoints rather than notes.

fluid.onsetfeature~.use_when: Realtime spectral-difference onset detection function. `@metric 0` selects difference type; `@filtersize 5` smooths; `@framedelta 0` is comparison delay; standard FFT attrs. Use as the curve underlying fluid.onsetslice~ when you want to threshold or process the curve yourself before slicing.

fluid.onsetslice~.use_when: Realtime spectral-difference onset slicer. Same `@metric` / `@filtersize` / `@framedelta` controls as fluid.onsetfeature~ plus a threshold and `@minslicelength` (default 2). Use for live note-level onset detection — more accurate than amplitude on tonal material.

fluid.pca~.use_when: Principal component analysis on a FluidDataSet — linear dimensionality reduction. Constructor arg: `<name>`. `fit <dataset>` learns components; `fittransform <src> <dst>` projects; `inversetransform <src> <dst>` reconstructs. `@numdimensions 2` (default) sets target; `@whiten 0/1` toggles whitening (unit variance per component). Use for linear dim reduction when your features have strong linear structure — fast, interpretable, deterministic.

fluid.pitch~.use_when: Realtime pitch (f0) descriptor. `@algorithm 2` (default = mpc) — also yin, hps available; `@minfreq 20`, `@maxfreq 10000` constrain search; `@select` selects output channels (`pitch`, `confidence`, or both); standard FFT attrs. Compare to fzero~ / sigmund~ — fluid.pitch~ exposes confidence directly and lets you choose algorithm. For higher accuracy offline use fluid.bufpitch~.

fluid.plotter.digest: Plot data in a two-dimensional graph.
fluid.plotter.use_when: 2D plotter UI for visualising datasets. 2 inlets / 2 outlets. `colorscheme <name>` selects palette; `bgcolor <r> <g> <b> <a>` sets background; `pointsize <id> <size>` and `pointcolor <id> <r> <g> <b> <a>` style individual points; `highlight <id>` selects a point; `clear` empties. Use to scatter-plot a 2D embedding (PCA/MDS/UMAP output) for interactive corpus exploration. For very large datasets prefer fluid.jit.plotter.

fluid.robustscale~.digest: Apply Robust Scaling to a FluidDataSet
fluid.robustscale~.use_when: Robust (median/IQR-based) scaling of a FluidDataSet. Constructor arg: `<name>`. `@low 25` and `@high 75` (default = IQR) set percentiles for scaling. `fit` / `fittransform` / `inversetransform` like fluid.normalize~. Use instead of normalize~ when features have outliers — robustscale~ is unaffected by extreme values that would skew min/max-based scaling.

fluid.sinefeature~.use_when: Realtime sinusoidal peak tracking. Constructor arg: `<numpeaks>`. `@detectionthreshold` (default -96 dB) gates peak detection; `@frequnit` and `@magunit` choose Hz vs MIDI and dB vs linear; `@maxnumpeaks` caps allocation; standard FFT attrs. Use for live harmonic analysis or partial-track-driven control.

fluid.sines~.use_when: Realtime sinusoidal modelling and resynthesis — separates input into sinusoidal partials and a residual live. Same `@detectionthreshold`, `@birthlowthreshold` / `@birthhighthreshold`, `@bandwidth` controls as fluid.bufsines~. Use for live time/pitch manipulation, harmonic isolation, or extracting just the residual for noise-only processing.

fluid.skmeans~.use_when: Spherical k-means — clusters using cosine similarity instead of Euclidean distance. Constructor arg: `<name>`. `fit <dataset>` trains; `encode <src> <dst>` projects to cluster activations; `encodepoint <src-buf> <dst-buf>` projects one point; `@numclusters 4`, `@encodingthreshold 0.25`, `@maxiter 100`. Use when feature magnitudes shouldn't matter (only direction), e.g. clustering MFCCs or normalised feature vectors.

fluid.spectralshape~.digest: Seven Spectral Shape Descriptors in Realtime
fluid.spectralshape~.use_when: Realtime version of fluid.bufspectralshape~ — seven spectral descriptors in one object. `@minfreq` / `@maxfreq` constrain band; `@power 0/1` switches power vs magnitude domain; `@rolloffpercent 95` (default) sets the rolloff threshold; standard FFT attrs. Use for live timbral feature extraction.

fluid.standardize~.use_when: Standardise a FluidDataSet (zero mean, unit variance per column). Constructor arg: `<name>`. `fit` / `fittransform` / `inversetransform` like normalize~ / robustscale~. Use as the standard preprocessing for ML algorithms that assume standardised input (PCA, MLP, etc.).

fluid.stats.digest: Rolling mean and standard deviation on control inputs
fluid.stats.use_when: Rolling mean and standard deviation on control-rate inputs. Constructor arg: `<inputsize>`. `@history` (default 2) sets window length; `@autosize 1` adapts to incoming list size. `clear` / `reset` zero state. Use to smooth or summarise a feature stream before downstream processing — list-domain version of average~ for control-rate work.

fluid.stftpass~.use_when: FFT/IFFT roundtrip with no processing. Standard FFT attrs (`@fftsettings`, `@maxfftsize`). Use as a baseline / sanity-check or as a starting template when building a custom spectral processor — the inputs/outputs and threading model match the rest of the FluCoMa real-time objects.

fluid.transientslice~.use_when: Realtime transient-based slicer. Same `@blocksize` / `@order` / `@padsize` / `@clumplength` / `@minslicelength` controls as fluid.buftransientslice~ plus `@skew` (default 0.0) for asymmetric detection and a threshold attr. Use for live percussive segmentation; complements fluid.onsetslice~ (spectral) and fluid.ampslice~ (amplitude).

fluid.transients~.use_when: Realtime transient extractor — separates transient and tonal components live via AR modelling. Same `@blocksize` / `@order` / `@padsize` / `@clumplength` / `@skew` / `@threshback` controls as fluid.buftransients~. Use for live click/attack isolation or removal — apply different processing to transients vs steady-state.

fluid.umap~.digest: Dimensionality Reduction with Uniform Manifold Approximation and Projection
fluid.umap~.use_when: Uniform Manifold Approximation and Projection — non-linear dimensionality reduction. Constructor arg: `<name>`. `@numdimensions 2` (default) sets target; `@numneighbours 15` controls local-vs-global balance (lower = more local); `@mindist 0.1` controls cluster tightness; `@iterations 200`, `@learnrate 0.1` are SGD knobs. `fit <dataset>` and `fittransform <src> <dst>`; `load <dict>` restores. Use to embed a high-dim feature space in 2D/3D preserving local structure — typically the best embedding for browsing a sound corpus.

fluid.waveform~.use_when: Visualise audio waveforms and FluidDataSet rows. `image <buf>` displays a buffer; `features <buf> <color?>` overlays a feature curve; `slices <markers> <reference>` shows slice markers over a reference waveform; `bgcolor` sets background; `remove <layer>` removes a layer; `clear` empties. Use as the FluCoMa-aware waveform display — better integrated with the buffer and dataset ecosystem than native waveform~.

fl.abs~.digest: Calculates the absolute value of each value in the input frame
fl.abs~.use_when: Calculates the absolute value of each value in the input frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.accumpos~.digest: Finds the index where the cumulative sum reaches a specified percentage of its total
fl.accumpos~.use_when: Finds the index where the cumulative sum reaches a specified percentage of its total. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.acosh~.digest: Calculates the hyperbolic arccosine of each value in the input frame
fl.acosh~.use_when: Calculates the hyperbolic arccosine of each value in the input frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.acos~.digest: Calculates the arccosine (in radians) of each value in the input frame
fl.acos~.use_when: Calculates the arccosine (in radians) of each value in the input frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.and~.digest: Calculates the logical and of the left and right input frame values
fl.and~.use_when: Calculates the logical and of the left and right input frame values. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.asinh~.digest: Calculates the hyperbolic arcsine of each value in the input frame
fl.asinh~.use_when: Calculates the hyperbolic arcsine of each value in the input frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.asin~.digest: Calculates the arcsine (in radians) of each value in the input frame
fl.asin~.use_when: Calculates the arcsine (in radians) of each value in the input frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.atan2~.digest: Calculates the arctangent using two arguments (left operand is y, right operand is x)
fl.atan2~.use_when: Calculates the arctangent using two arguments (left operand is y, right operand is x). Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.atanh~.digest: Calculates the hyperbolic arctangent of each value in the input frame
fl.atanh~.use_when: Calculates the hyperbolic arctangent of each value in the input frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.atan~.digest: Calculates the arctangent (in radians) of each value in the input frame
fl.atan~.use_when: Calculates the arctangent (in radians) of each value in the input frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.audiotrigger~.digest: Schedules frames based on non-zero audio input
fl.audiotrigger~.use_when: Fire frames when audio input crosses a threshold. Use as a transient-driven scheduler instead of fixed-period fl.interval~.

fl.biquadcoeff~.use_when: Compute biquad filter coefficients (b0, b1, b2, a1, a2) from filter-design parameters. Output goes into fl.biquad~.

fl.biquad~.digest: Applies a biquad filter to the input
fl.biquad~.use_when: Biquad filter applied to an input frame. Pair with fl.biquadcoeff~ to compute coefficients from cutoff/resonance.

fl.cartopol~.digest: Converts from cartesian values at the inputs to polar values at the outputs
fl.cartopol~.use_when: Cartesian → polar conversion across a frame pair (x at top inlets, y at bottom). Outputs r and theta. Use for spectral magnitude/phase work.

fl.cbrt~.digest: Calculates the cube root of each value in the input frame
fl.cbrt~.use_when: Calculates the cube root of each value in the input frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.ceil~.digest: Calculates the ceiling function of each value in the input frame
fl.ceil~.use_when: Calculates the ceiling function of each value in the input frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.centroid~.digest: Calculates the centroid of the input frame
fl.centroid~.use_when: Calculates the centroid of the input frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.chain~.digest: Schedules frames in the future at times specified times in the input frame
fl.chain~.use_when: Schedule frames at times specified inside an input frame — frame contents are timestamps, output is a sequence of triggered frames. Use for irregular event scheduling driven by arbitrary timing data.

fl.chop~.digest: Chops input frames into equally sized output frames of a specified size
fl.chop~.use_when: Chops input frames into equally sized output frames of a specified size. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.clip~.digest: Clips the incoming frame to the range delimited by the two parameters
fl.clip~.use_when: Clips the incoming frame to the range delimited by the two parameters. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.combinetags~.digest: Combines a number of tagged input frames into a single tagged output frame
fl.combinetags~.use_when: Merge multiple tagged input frames into a single tagged output frame. Use to consolidate parallel processing branches that each carry tagged metadata.

fl.complex.conj~.digest: Calculates the complex conjugate of each complex value at the inputs
fl.complex.conj~.use_when: Calculates the complex conjugate of each complex value at the inputs. FrameLib complex-frame analogue of `fl.conj~` (see package_concepts.md FrameLib section for complex-frame protocol).

fl.complex.cosh~.digest: Calculates the hyperbolic cosine of each complex value at the inputs
fl.complex.cosh~.use_when: Calculates the hyperbolic cosine of each complex value at the inputs. FrameLib complex-frame analogue of `fl.cosh~` (see package_concepts.md FrameLib section for complex-frame protocol).

fl.complex.cos~.digest: Calculates the cosine of each complex value at the inputs
fl.complex.cos~.use_when: Calculates the cosine of each complex value at the inputs. FrameLib complex-frame analogue of `fl.cos~` (see package_concepts.md FrameLib section for complex-frame protocol).

fl.complex.divide~.digest: Divides complex values in the left pair of input frames by those in the right pair
fl.complex.divide~.use_when: Divides complex values in the left pair of input frames by those in the right pair. FrameLib complex-frame analogue of `fl.divide~` (see package_concepts.md FrameLib section for complex-frame protocol).

fl.complex.expr~.digest: Calculates the result of a mathematical expression involving complex numbers
fl.complex.expr~.use_when: Like fl.expr~ but operates on complex frame pairs. Use for spectral expressions that mix real and imaginary parts.

fl.complex.exp~.digest: Calculates the exponent base e (e^x) of each complex value at the inputs
fl.complex.exp~.use_when: Calculates the exponent base e (e^x) of each complex value at the inputs. FrameLib complex-frame analogue of `fl.exp~` (see package_concepts.md FrameLib section for complex-frame protocol).

fl.complex.log10~.digest: Calculates the logarithm base 10 of each complex value at the inputs
fl.complex.log10~.use_when: Calculates the logarithm base 10 of each complex value at the inputs. FrameLib complex-frame analogue of `fl.log10~` (see package_concepts.md FrameLib section for complex-frame protocol).

fl.complex.log~.digest: Calculates the logarithm base e of each complex value at the inputs
fl.complex.log~.use_when: Calculates the logarithm base e of each complex value at the inputs. FrameLib complex-frame analogue of `fl.log~` (see package_concepts.md FrameLib section for complex-frame protocol).

fl.complex.minus~.digest: Subtracts complex values in the right input frame pair from those in the left pair
fl.complex.minus~.use_when: Subtracts complex values in the right input frame pair from those in the left pair. FrameLib complex-frame analogue of `fl.minus~` (see package_concepts.md FrameLib section for complex-frame protocol).

fl.complex.plus~.digest: Adds complex values in the two pairs of input frames
fl.complex.plus~.use_when: Adds complex values in the two pairs of input frames. FrameLib complex-frame analogue of `fl.plus~` (see package_concepts.md FrameLib section for complex-frame protocol).

fl.complex.pow~.digest: Raises left operand values to the power of right operand values (both complex)
fl.complex.pow~.use_when: Raises left operand values to the power of right operand values (both complex). FrameLib complex-frame analogue of `fl.pow~` (see package_concepts.md FrameLib section for complex-frame protocol).

fl.complex.sinh~.digest: Calculates the hyperbolic sine of each complex value at the inputs
fl.complex.sinh~.use_when: Calculates the hyperbolic sine of each complex value at the inputs. FrameLib complex-frame analogue of `fl.sinh~` (see package_concepts.md FrameLib section for complex-frame protocol).

fl.complex.sin~.digest: Calculates the sine of each complex value at the inputs
fl.complex.sin~.use_when: Calculates the sine of each complex value at the inputs. FrameLib complex-frame analogue of `fl.sin~` (see package_concepts.md FrameLib section for complex-frame protocol).

fl.complex.sqrt~.digest: Calculates the square root of each complex value at the inputs
fl.complex.sqrt~.use_when: Calculates the square root of each complex value at the inputs. FrameLib complex-frame analogue of `fl.sqrt~` (see package_concepts.md FrameLib section for complex-frame protocol).

fl.complex.tanh~.digest: Calculates the hyperbolic tangent of each complex value at the inputs
fl.complex.tanh~.use_when: Calculates the hyperbolic tangent of each complex value at the inputs. FrameLib complex-frame analogue of `fl.tanh~` (see package_concepts.md FrameLib section for complex-frame protocol).

fl.complex.tan~.digest: Calculates the tangent of each complex value at the inputs
fl.complex.tan~.use_when: Calculates the tangent of each complex value at the inputs. FrameLib complex-frame analogue of `fl.tan~` (see package_concepts.md FrameLib section for complex-frame protocol).

fl.complex.times~.digest: Multiplies complex values in the two pairs of input frames
fl.complex.times~.use_when: Multiplies complex values in the two pairs of input frames. FrameLib complex-frame analogue of `fl.times~` (see package_concepts.md FrameLib section for complex-frame protocol).

fl.constant~.use_when: Outputs a specified constant. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.contextcontrol~.digest: Controls a named context via Max messages.
fl.contextcontrol~.use_when: Per-context control object — set runtime properties on a named FrameLib context. `multithread 0/1` toggles parallel execution (OFF default); messages drive realtime/non-realtime mode and advance time in NRT. Locate at top of patch alongside the scheduler that defines the context.

fl.convert~.digest: Converts the input vector according to a specified conversion
fl.convert~.use_when: Converts the input vector according to a specified conversion. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.convolve~.digest: Convolves two inputs (either real or complex)
fl.convolve~.use_when: Convolves two inputs (either real or complex). Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.copysign~.digest: Copys the signs of the right input frame onto the magnitudes of the left input frame
fl.copysign~.use_when: Copys the signs of the right input frame onto the magnitudes of the left input frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.correlate~.digest: Correlates two inputs (either real or complex)
fl.correlate~.use_when: Correlates two inputs (either real or complex). Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.cosh~.digest: Calculates the hyperbolic cosine of each value in the input frame
fl.cosh~.use_when: Calculates the hyperbolic cosine of each value in the input frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.cos~.digest: Calculates the cosine (for arguments in radians) of each value in the input frame
fl.cos~.use_when: Calculates the cosine (for arguments in radians) of each value in the input frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.crest~.digest: Calculates the crest factor of the input frame
fl.crest~.use_when: Calculates the crest factor of the input frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.diff~.digest: Calculates the absolute differences between values in the two input frames
fl.diff~.use_when: Calculates the absolute differences between values in the two input frames. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.dispatch~.digest: Dispatches multiple inputs dynamically to multiple outputs
fl.dispatch~.use_when: Dispatches multiple inputs dynamically to multiple outputs. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.divide~.digest: Divides the left input frame values by the right input frame values
fl.divide~.use_when: Divides the left input frame values by the right input frame values. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.equals~.digest: Compares frame values for equality (returns 1 or 0)
fl.equals~.use_when: Compares frame values for equality (returns 1 or 0). Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.erfc~.digest: Calculates the complimentary error function of each value in the input frame
fl.erfc~.use_when: Calculates the complimentary error function of each value in the input frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.erf~.digest: Calculates the error function of each value in the input frame
fl.erf~.use_when: Calculates the error function of each value in the input frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.exp2~.digest: Calculates the exponent base 2 (2^x) of each value in the input frame
fl.exp2~.use_when: Calculates the exponent base 2 (2^x) of each value in the input frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.expr~.digest: Calculates the result of a mathematical expression
fl.expr~.use_when: Evaluate a mathematical expression element-wise across input frames. Variables `$f1`, `$f2`, … bind to inlets. Use as the FrameLib analogue of MSP `expr~` whenever a chain of fl.* math operators would be unwieldy.

fl.exp~.digest: Calculates the exponent base e (e^x) of each value in the input frame
fl.exp~.use_when: Calculates the exponent base e (e^x) of each value in the input frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.fft~.digest: Calculate the real or complex Fast Fourier Transform of the input(s)
fl.fft~.use_when: Forward FFT of an input frame — emits paired complex frames (real + imaginary). Pair with fl.ifft~ for round-trip spectral processing. `@size N` sets transform size.

fl.filtertags~.digest: Filters tagged frames using a set of specified tags
fl.filtertags~.use_when: Filters tagged frames using a set of specified tags. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.firphase~.digest: Transform FIR coefficients to preserve the amplitude spectrum but alter the phase
fl.firphase~.use_when: Transform FIR coefficients to preserve the amplitude spectrum but alter the phase. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.flatness~.digest: Calculates the flatness (as a ratio) of the input frame
fl.flatness~.use_when: Calculates the flatness (as a ratio) of the input frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.floor~.digest: Calculates the floor function of each value in the input frame
fl.floor~.use_when: Calculates the floor function of each value in the input frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.fold~.digest: Folds the incoming frame into the range delimited by the two parameters
fl.fold~.use_when: Folds the incoming frame into the range delimited by the two parameters. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.framedelta~.digest: Outputs the deltas between consecutive frames
fl.framedelta~.use_when: Outputs the deltas between consecutive frames. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.framemax~.digest: Calculates the maximum value of the input frame
fl.framemax~.use_when: Calculates the maximum value of the input frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.framemin~.digest: Calculates the minimum value of the input frame
fl.framemin~.use_when: Calculates the minimum value of the input frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.frommax~.use_when: Turn Max messages into frames. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.gaussian~.digest: Generates frames of normally distributed random values
fl.gaussian~.use_when: Generates frames of normally distributed random values. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.geomean~.digest: Calculates the geometric mean of the input frame
fl.geomean~.use_when: Calculates the geometric mean of the input frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.greaterthaneq~.digest: Compares input frames using a greater than or equal to operation (returns 1 or 0)
fl.greaterthaneq~.use_when: Compares input frames using a greater than or equal to operation (returns 1 or 0). Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.greaterthan~.digest: Compares input frames using a greater than operation (returns 1 or 0)
fl.greaterthan~.use_when: Compares input frames using a greater than operation (returns 1 or 0). Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.hypot~.digest: Calculates the length of a hypotenuse given the two operands as the other lengths
fl.hypot~.use_when: Calculates the length of a hypotenuse given the two operands as the other lengths. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.ifft~.digest: Calculate the real or complex inverse Fast Fourier Transform of the inputs (real and imaginary)
fl.ifft~.use_when: Inverse FFT — combines complex frame pair (real + imaginary) into a time-domain frame. Counterpart to fl.fft~.

fl.info~.digest: Outputs the sample rate, number of channels and length of a specified buffer
fl.info~.use_when: Outputs the sample rate, number of channels and length of a specified buffer. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.interval~.digest: Schedules frames at regular intervals set by the interval parameter
fl.interval~.use_when: Scheduler — emits frames at fixed sample intervals. The most common entry point for a FrameLib network. `@interval N` sets the period in samples; `@id <name>` sets the context (see package_concepts.md). Without this (or another scheduler) at the head of a connected network, nothing fires.

fl.join~.digest: Concatenates all input frames into a single output frame
fl.join~.use_when: Concatenates all input frames into a single output frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.kernelsmooth~.digest: Smooth an input based on a provided smoothing kernel
fl.kernelsmooth~.use_when: Smooth an input based on a provided smoothing kernel. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.kurtosis~.digest: Calculates the kurtosis of the input frame

fl.lag~.digest: Delay vector type input by a whole number of frames
fl.lag~.use_when: Delay vector type input by a whole number of frames. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.length~.digest: Calculates the length of the input frame
fl.length~.use_when: Number of samples in an input frame.

fl.lessthaneq~.digest: Compares input frames using a less than or equal to operation (returns 1 or 0)
fl.lessthaneq~.use_when: Compares input frames using a less than or equal to operation (returns 1 or 0). Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.lessthan~.digest: Compares input frames using a less than operation (returns 1 or 0)
fl.lessthan~.use_when: Compares input frames using a less than operation (returns 1 or 0). Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.log10~.digest: Calculates the logarithm base 10 of each value in the input frame

fl.log2~.digest: Calculates the logarithm base 2 of each value in the input frame

fl.lookupstring~.digest: Lookup a specified string from an internally stored list of strings
fl.lookupstring~.use_when: Lookup a specified string from an internally stored list of strings. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.lookup~.digest: Lookup values from one input using a table provided at another
fl.lookup~.use_when: Table lookup — index into an input table frame using a position frame. Use for waveshaping or non-linear mappings.

fl.makestring~.digest: Creates a tagged string by concatenating strings and numbers
fl.makestring~.use_when: Creates a tagged string by concatenating strings and numbers. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.makewindow~.digest: Generates a specified window function across each output frame
fl.makewindow~.use_when: Generates a specified window function across each output frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.map~.digest: Maps values from input to output via a specified scaling
fl.map~.use_when: Maps values from input to output via a specified scaling. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.maxpos~.digest: Calculates the index of the maximum value of the input frame

fl.max~.use_when: Calculates the maximums of pairs of values in the two input frames. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.mean~.digest: Calculates the mean of the input frame
fl.mean~.use_when: Arithmetic mean of an input frame.

fl.medianfilter~.digest: Applies a median or other specified percentile filter to the input
fl.medianfilter~.use_when: Applies a median or other specified percentile filter to the input. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.minus~.digest: Subtracts the right input frame values from the left input frame values
fl.minus~.use_when: Subtracts the right input frame values from the left input frame values. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.min~.digest: Calculates the minimums of pairs of values in the two input frames
fl.min~.use_when: Calculates the minimums of pairs of values in the two input frames. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.modulo~.digest: Calculates the left input frame values modulo the right input frame values
fl.modulo~.use_when: Calculates the left input frame values modulo the right input frame values. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.movingaverage~.digest: Calculates per sample moving averages and standard deviations
fl.movingaverage~.use_when: Calculates per sample moving averages and standard deviations. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.multitaper~.digest: Calculates a multitaper power (or amplitude) spectrum from a real input
fl.multitaper~.use_when: Calculates a multitaper power (or amplitude) spectrum from a real input. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.nanfilter~.digest: Filter NaNs from the input either by replacing or by removing them
fl.nanfilter~.use_when: Filter NaNs from the input either by replacing or by removing them. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.nonzero~.digest: Outputs the indices of input samples that are non-zero
fl.nonzero~.use_when: Outputs the indices of input samples that are non-zero. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.notequals~.use_when: Compares frame values for inequality (returns 1 or 0). Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.not~.digest: Calculates the logical not of each value in the input frame
fl.not~.use_when: Calculates the logical not of each value in the input frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.now~.use_when: Outputs the current time. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.once~.digest: Schedules a single frame at the begining of time that lasts forever
fl.once~.use_when: Schedules a single frame at the begining of time that lasts forever. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.onepole~.use_when: Applies  a one-pole 6dB/octave filter to the input. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.or~.use_when: Calculates the logical or of the left and right input frame values. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.pack~.use_when: Packs multiple frame streams into a multistream output. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.pad~.digest: Pads the input frame with a repeated specified value
fl.pad~.use_when: Pads the input frame with a repeated specified value. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.read~.use_when: Reads from a specified host buffer given an input frame of positions. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.reverse~.use_when: Computes the reverse of the input frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.rms~.digest: Calculates the rms of the input frame

fl.select~.use_when: Selects output from a number of inputs. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.sink~.use_when: Outputs frames to the host environment as audio by overlap-adding into an output buffer. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.sin~.use_when: Calculates the sine (for arguments in radians) of each value in the input frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.sort~.use_when: Sorts an input frame into ascending or descending order. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.split~.use_when: Splits input frames into two frames, given a specified split point. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.spread~.digest: Calculates the spread of the input frame
fl.spread~.use_when: Calculates the spread of the input frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.sqrt~.digest: Calculates the square root of each value in the input frame
fl.sqrt~.use_when: Calculates the square root of each value in the input frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.stddev~.use_when: Standard deviation of an input frame.

fl.store~.digest: Stores frames remotely for later recall
fl.store~.use_when: Write a frame to a context-scoped named slot. Pair with fl.recall~ of the same name. Use for cross-network sharing within a context; same name in a different context is independent storage.

fl.streamid~.digest: Outputs the stream id for each stream in a multistream connection
fl.streamid~.use_when: Outputs the stream id for each stream in a multistream connection. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.
