fl.subframe~.use_when: Output a specified segment of an input frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.sum~.digest: Calculates the sum of the input frame
fl.sum~.use_when: Calculates the sum of the input frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.svf~.digest: Applies a state variable filter to the input
fl.svf~.use_when: Applies a state variable filter to the input. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.tag~.digest: Adds tags to vectors either in order to set parameters or for routing purposes
fl.tag~.use_when: Set a tag (named per-frame metadata) on an input frame. Pair with fl.combinetags~ or fl.gettag~ for tag-driven branching.

fl.tanh~.digest: Calculates the hyperbolic tangent of each value in the input frame
fl.tanh~.use_when: Calculates the hyperbolic tangent of each value in the input frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.tan~.digest: Calculates the tangent (for arguments in radians) of each value in the input frame
fl.tan~.use_when: Calculates the tangent (for arguments in radians) of each value in the input frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.ticks~.use_when: Counts incoming frames. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.timedelta~.digest: Calculates the time interval between consecutive incoming frames
fl.timedelta~.use_when: Calculates the time interval between consecutive incoming frames. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.timemean~.digest: Calculates the mean per sample over the most recent frames
fl.timemean~.use_when: Calculates the mean per sample over the most recent frames. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.timemedian~.digest: Calculates the specified percentile per sample over the most recent frames
fl.timemedian~.use_when: Calculates the specified percentile per sample over the most recent frames. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.timer~.digest: Calculates the time elapsed between the each trigger input and the previous reset
fl.timer~.use_when: Calculates the time elapsed between the each trigger input and the previous reset. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.timestddev~.digest: Calculates the standard deviation per sample over the most recent frames
fl.timestddev~.use_when: Calculates the standard deviation per sample over the most recent frames. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.times~.digest: Multiplies values in the two input frames
fl.times~.use_when: Multiplies values in the two input frames. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.tomax~.use_when: Convert FrameLib frames → MSP signal. Bridge from FrameLib output back into standard MSP audio. Required when feeding FrameLib output into ezdac~ or any MSP object.

fl.trace~.digest: Outputs values from the input as audio, continuing the last value till a new frame arrives
fl.trace~.use_when: Outputs values from the input as audio, continuing the last value till a new frame arrives. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.trunc~.digest: Calculates the truncated value of each value in the input frame
fl.trunc~.use_when: Calculates the truncated value of each value in the input frame. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.uniform~.digest: Generates frames in which all values are identical
fl.uniform~.use_when: Generates frames in which all values are identical. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.unpack~.digest: Unpacks multistream input into individual streams
fl.unpack~.use_when: Unpacks multistream input into individual streams. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.untag~.digest: Retrieves vectors from a tagged frame according to a specified set of tags
fl.untag~.use_when: Retrieves vectors from a tagged frame according to a specified set of tags. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

fl.window~.digest: Multiplies the input frame by a specified window
fl.window~.use_when: Generate a window function as a frame. Standard windows (Hann, Hamming, Blackman, etc.) selected via `@window` attr. Use as an analysis/synthesis window for FFT framing.

fl.wrap~.digest: Wraps the incoming frame into the range delimited by the two parameters
fl.wrap~.use_when: Wraps the incoming frame into the range delimited by the two parameters. Element-wise FrameLib operator on input frames.

bufconvolve~.use_when: Convolve or deconvolve two buffers offline (non-realtime). Operation is selected by message: 'convolve' for forward convolution, 'deconvolve' for inverse. Deconvolution mode supports several attribute-driven options (deconvmode, deconvphase, deconvfilter, deconvrange, deconvdelay) for regularised inverse-filter design. For realtime convolution use multiconvolve~ instead. Native Max has no offline convolution — rolling this yourself is a long pfft~ + complex-multiply + ifft chain.

bufresample~.digest: Resample the contents of a buffer~ to transpose or change sample rate
bufresample~.use_when: Rewrite a buffer at a new sample rate or transposition with high-quality offline resampling. Four message modes: 'resample' (target sample rate), 'transpose' (cents/ratio), 'both' (combined), and 'filter' (apply only the antialiasing filter). Different from groove~/sfplay~, which play back at a rate without altering the source — bufresample~ produces a new buffer.

bufreverse~.digest: Reverse the contents of a buffer
bufreverse~.use_when: Reverse a buffer in place. Triggered by the 'process' message. Native alternatives are a JS peek~/poke~ loop or jit.matrix tricks; this is a single object and faster.

hirt.convolutionreverb~.digest: The HIRT Convolution Reverb for Max
hirt.convolutionreverb~.use_when: Turnkey convolution reverb abstraction with built-in IR file loading. Optional 'reverbname' arg sets the instance name. Messages: 'open' / 'close' to toggle the GUI window, 'iradd' / 'irfolder' / 'irmenu' to load IRs, 'irmode' for impulse-handling mode. Use instead of building a multiconvolve~-based reverb when you want a self-contained reverb unit. For non-reverb convolution (cabinets, time-domain filtering, custom IRs from a buffer) use hirt.convolver~ or multiconvolve~ directly.

hirt.convolver~.use_when: General-purpose realtime convolver abstraction — convolve any signal with any IR. Optional 'convolvername' arg. Messages mirror the reverb version: 'open' / 'close' for GUI, 'iradd' / 'irfolder' / 'irmenu' to load IRs. Choose this over hirt.convolutionreverb~ when you're not building a reverb (cabinet IRs, EQ-as-IR, time-domain filtering).

hirt.irmaker~.digest: HIRT Impulse Response Maker - Impulse Response Measurement and Auditioning
hirt.irmaker~.use_when: All-in-one IR measurement abstraction — generates the excitation, plays it, captures the response, and produces the IR. Messages: 'sweep' to start a sweep measurement, 'impulse' for an impulse measurement, 'stop' to abort, 'save' to write the IR, 'settings' for config, 'open'/'close' for GUI, 'left'/'right'/'centre' for channel routing. Use when measuring a real-world IR (room, speaker, hardware) without driving the measurement chain manually. For custom workflows (multichannel, calibration) use irsweeps~ + irmeasure~ + irextract~ instead.

iralign~.use_when: Time-align multiple IRs so their direct-sound peaks coincide. Messages: 'align' (mutual alignment) or 'alignto' (align to a reference). Reach for this before iraverage~ or comparison work where IRs were captured at different distances or with different latencies.

iraverage~.digest: Average multiple IRs in the time or frequency domain
iraverage~.use_when: Average multiple IR measurements (time or frequency domain) to reduce measurement noise. Messages: 'average' (frequency-domain averaging) or 'process' (time-domain). Standard step after capturing several takes of the same IR.

irextract~.digest: Process recorded measurements from the irmeasure~ object
irextract~.use_when: Companion to irmeasure~ — turns a recorded sweep response into the actual IR. Sweep type is set by message: 'sweep' (exponential sine sweep), 'mls' (max-length sequence), 'pink' / 'white' / 'brown' (noise variants). Messages: 'getir' to retrieve the result, 'dump' for raw data, 'clear' to reset. Use when irmeasure~ has captured raw measurement data and you need to extract the IR from it.

irinvert~.use_when: Build an inverse filter from an IR. Messages: 'invert' for the basic case, 'mimo' / 'mimoto' for multi-input multi-output (matrix) inversion. Use for room correction, speaker equalisation, or any 'undo this system's response' workflow.

irmeasure~.digest: Measure IRs of single or multichannel systems using a range of excitation signals
irmeasure~.use_when: Generate excitation signals and capture the system response for IR measurement. Optional num_ins / num_outs args set channel counts. Excitation types by message: 'sweep', 'mls', 'pink', 'white', 'brown', 'tone'. Workflow messages: 'activeins' / 'activeouts' to gate channels, 'extract' to compute the IR, 'getir' / 'dump' to retrieve, 'reprocess' to recompute with new settings, 'stop' / 'clear'. Lower-level than hirt.irmaker~ — use when you need control over the measurement chain (custom sweep, multichannel system, calibration).

irnonlin~.digest: Transform IRs for harmonics of system into those required by a Hammerstein model
irnonlin~.use_when: Hammerstein-model decomposition of harmonics from a system measurement — for modeling weakly nonlinear systems (tubes, distortion). Messages: 'convert' (in-place) or 'convertto' (separate destination). Niche; reach for this only when doing nonlinear system identification.

irphase~.digest: Change the phase of an IR
irphase~.use_when: Convert an IR's phase characteristic while preserving its magnitude response. Phase mode is selected by message: 'minphase' (minimum-phase, no pre-ringing), 'maxphase' (maximum-phase), 'linphase' (linear-phase, symmetrical impulse), 'mixedphase' (blended), 'allpass' (allpass component only). Use when you need a specific phase property — e.g. zero pre-ringing for a linear-phase EQ from a measured curve.

irplapprox~.digest: Approximate the frequency response of an IR with a set of freq/dB pairs
irplapprox~.use_when: Distil an IR's frequency response into a compact set of frequency/dB pairs (piecewise-linear approximation). Triggered by 'approx' message. Use when you need a parametric description of a response, e.g. to drive a parametric EQ from a measured curve.

irreference~.digest: Estimate system IRs by relative comparison of two recorded inputs
irreference~.use_when: Derive an IR by comparing a reference signal to a recorded system output, when you can't drive the system with a controlled sweep. Optional num_ref_ins arg. Workflow messages: 'rec' to start recording, 'finish' to stop, 'extract' / 'reprocess' / 'getir' to compute and retrieve, 'activeins' / 'clear' / 'dump' / 'stop'. Use when measurement is in-situ and the reference signal is whatever happens to be playing.

irstats~.digest: Calculate room acoustic parameters and measurements from an IR
irstats~.use_when: Compute room acoustic parameters from an IR — RT60, EDT, clarity, definition, and related metrics. Triggered by 'stats' message. Use this for any acoustic analysis task; the calculations are non-trivial and shouldn't be reimplemented.

irsweeps~.digest: Generate measurement signals to a buffer
irsweeps~.use_when: Write a measurement excitation signal into a buffer. Signal type by message: 'sweep' (exponential sine sweep), 'invsweep' (inverse sweep, paired with 'sweep' for deconvolution), 'mls', 'pink', 'white', 'brown'. First step of a manual IR measurement workflow that pairs with irmeasure~ and irextract~.

irtransaural~.digest: Generate filters for the playback of binaural material over speakers
irtransaural~.use_when: Generate crosstalk-cancellation filters so binaural recordings play back correctly over loudspeakers. Two algorithm choices by message: 'lattice' (lattice-form filter) or 'shuffler' (shuffler-form filter). Use only for transaural / loudspeaker-binaural playback — niche but specific.

irtrimnorm~.digest: Trim (by RMS level), crop, or pad multiple IRs, with or without group normalisation
irtrimnorm~.use_when: Trim, crop, pad, and normalise multiple IRs as a batch, with optional group normalisation. Each operation has 'in-place' and 'to-destination' variants: 'trim' / 'trimto', 'crop' / 'cropto', 'pad' / 'padto'. Standard cleanup step before using captured IRs in convolution.

iruser~.digest: Create an IR with a specified frequency response and a given output phase
iruser~.use_when: Synthesise an IR from a target frequency response and a chosen output phase. Triggered by 'make' message. Use to build EQ-curve IRs, target-response filters, or any case where you want to specify a response and get an IR.

irvalue~.digest: Return dB amplitude of IR from frequency input
irvalue~.use_when: Query an IR's magnitude (in dB) at a given frequency. 'set' message loads the IR, then a float to the inlet returns the dB value at that frequency. Use as a measurement utility — read out a specific frequency's level from an IR rather than visualising the whole curve.

morphfilter~.use_when: Apply time-varying filtering to a buffer with morphing gain (offline). Messages: 'addfilter' to push filter stages, 'clear' to reset, 'process' to run. Use when you need a filter whose response changes over the duration of a buffer in a controlled way — e.g. spectral fades, EQ automation baked into a sample.

multiconvolve~.digest: Multichannel realtime zero- or low- latency convolution
multiconvolve~.use_when: The main realtime convolver for multichannel signals at zero or low latency. Args: num_ins?, num_outs?, latency_mode? — latency_mode is 'zero' (uniform-partition, lowest latency, more CPU) or 'low' (non-uniform partition, very low latency with better CPU efficiency). Messages: 'set' to load IR(s), 'clear'. Use anywhere you need realtime convolution and want full control (custom IRs, multichannel routing, partition tuning). For a reverb-shaped UI use hirt.convolutionreverb~ instead.

spectrumdraw~.digest: Display spectra from realtime inputs or buffers
spectrumdraw~.use_when: Display realtime or buffer-based spectra in a Max UI without rolling your own pfft~ → jit.matrix chain. Messages: 'set' to assign a buffer, 'select' to choose a curve, 'freeze' to hold the display. Use for IR/spectrum visualisation in HIRT-based or general spectral patches.
