sbb.env.ad.digest: An attack-decay envelope with built-in attenuator.
sbb.env.ad.use_when: RNBO attack-decay envelope with attenuator. Use for percussive sounds where you don't need sustain/release.

sbb.env.adsr.digest: A linear attack-decay-sustain-release envelope with built-in attenuator.
sbb.env.adsr.use_when: RNBO ADSR envelope (linear) with built-in attenuator. Stereo input, attack/decay/sustain/release in ms. Use in RNBO patches as the standard amp envelope; cleaner than building it from gen~ primitives.

sbb.env.adsr.stereo.digest: A stereo attack-decay-sustain-release envelope with built-in attenuator.
sbb.env.adsr.stereo.use_when: RNBO stereo ADSR envelope. Same as sbb.env.adsr but with stereo signal path.

sbb.env.analog.digest: An analog-style (exponential) attack-decay-sustain-release envelope with built-in attenuator.
sbb.env.analog.use_when: RNBO analog-style (exponential curves) ADSR envelope. Use when you want the characteristic non-linear curve of analog hardware envelopes.

sbb.env.follower.digest: number of samples to use in computing envelope
sbb.env.follower.use_when: RNBO envelope follower — track signal amplitude via RMS over a sliding window. `samples` arg sets window length.

sbb.env.lpg.use_when: A lowpass gate circuit emulation. RNBO synth building block — pre-built primitive for use inside RNBO patches.

sbb.env.slope.use_when: An analog-style ADSR envelope. RNBO synth building block — pre-built primitive for use inside RNBO patches.

sbb.filter.bp.use_when: A biquad bandpass filter. RNBO synth building block — pre-built primitive for use inside RNBO patches.

sbb.filter.br.use_when: A biquad band reject filter. RNBO synth building block — pre-built primitive for use inside RNBO patches.

sbb.filter.hp.use_when: A biquad highpass filter. RNBO synth building block — pre-built primitive for use inside RNBO patches.

sbb.filter.ladder.use_when: A ladder filter emulation. RNBO synth building block — pre-built primitive for use inside RNBO patches.

sbb.filter.lp.use_when: A biquad lowpass filter. RNBO synth building block — pre-built primitive for use inside RNBO patches.

sbb.mod.arp.digest: See max-usage tab to view note input/output
sbb.mod.arp.use_when: See max-usage tab to view note input/output. RNBO synth building block — pre-built primitive for use inside RNBO patches.

sbb.mod.lfo.digest: A low frequency oscillator with multiple shapes.
sbb.mod.lfo.use_when: A low frequency oscillator with multiple shapes. RNBO synth building block — pre-built primitive for use inside RNBO patches.

sbb.mod.rand.digest: A signal-rate randomness source and a random sample-and-hold in one.
sbb.mod.rand.use_when: A signal-rate randomness source and a random sample-and-hold in one. RNBO synth building block — pre-built primitive for use inside RNBO patches.

sbb.mod.seq.digest: A signal-rate randomness source and a random sample-and-hold in one.
sbb.mod.seq.use_when: A signal-rate randomness source and a random sample-and-hold in one. RNBO synth building block — pre-built primitive for use inside RNBO patches.

sbb.osc.additive.use_when: emphasize even or odd partials. RNBO synth building block — pre-built primitive for use inside RNBO patches.

sbb.osc.analog.digest: Anti-aliased waveforms and noise oscillators with pulse-width modulation and hard sync
sbb.osc.analog.use_when: Anti-aliased waveforms and noise oscillators with pulse-width modulation and hard sync. RNBO synth building block — pre-built primitive for use inside RNBO patches.

sbb.osc.fm.use_when: See helpfile for sbb.osc.fm. RNBO synth building block — pre-built primitive for use inside RNBO patches.

sbb.osc.granular.use_when: A granular synthesis voice. RNBO synth building block — pre-built primitive for use inside RNBO patches.

sbb.osc.karplus.digest: A Karplus-Strong physical model for a plucked string.
sbb.osc.karplus.use_when: A Karplus-Strong physical model for a plucked string. RNBO synth building block — pre-built primitive for use inside RNBO patches.

sbb.osc.wavetable.digest: Generate a periodic waveform. The default waveform is one cycle of a cosine wave, but can be set to reference a named buffer.
sbb.osc.wavetable.use_when: Generate a periodic waveform. The default waveform is one cycle of a cosine wave, but can be set to reference a named buffer. RNBO synth building block — pre-built primitive for use inside RNBO patches.

sbb.util.sustain.digest: Sustain on/off. When you turn it off, sustain outputs all the note-offs it received while sustaining.
sbb.util.sustain.use_when: Sustain on/off. When you turn it off, sustain outputs all the note-offs it received while sustaining. RNBO synth building block — pre-built primitive for use inside RNBO patches.

sbb.util.xfade.digest: Crossfade between the two sources, using the range of -50. to 50.
sbb.util.xfade.use_when: Crossfade between the two sources, using the range of -50. to 50. RNBO synth building block — pre-built primitive for use inside RNBO patches.

16step-seq.digest: A 16-step sequencer which patterns can be created by random or by supplying a list (see below) - the pattern can be changed gradually by applied operations.
16step-seq.use_when: 16-step sequencer with random or list-supplied patterns. Constructor arg: `<step-time-ms>`. Patterns can be created randomly, supplied as a list, or modified gradually by applied operations (e.g. randomly change one cell). Use as a self-contained step sequencer when you need pattern evolution rather than fixed sequences.

@.digest: Scales a standard slider (ranging from 0 to 127) between min and max. The type of the first argument specifies the output type.
@.use_when: Range-scaled slider — maps a 0–127 standard slider into an arbitrary range. Constructor args: `<min> <max>`; the type of the first argument (int vs float) determines the output type. Use as a one-line replacement for scale + slider chains.

BPM2ms.digest: Converts beats per minute (BPM) into milliseconds.
BPM2ms.use_when: Convert beats-per-minute to milliseconds (per beat). Use as the standard tempo→duration conversion for sequencers and metronomes.

DblBang.digest: Detects single or double bang (like single or double mouse clicks)
DblBang.use_when: Detect single vs double bang (like single/double-click). Constructor arg: `<threshold-ms>` (default 250). Use to extend a single trigger source into two distinct events for two different actions.

ED-rhythm.use_when: Generic ED-based rhythm generator. Constructor arg: `<seed>` (default 3). Use as the basic ED-driven rhythm stream.

ED-trans.digest: Generates a transition between two entry delays (ED) within a certain time span producing either a ritardando or an accelerando. A number (= time in ms) sent to the leftmost inlet starts the process.
ED-trans.use_when: Transition between two EDs over a time span — produces ritardando (ED rising) or accelerando (ED falling). Constructor args: `<start-ED> <end-ED>` (default 75, 300). Send time-in-ms to start. Use for tempo transitions over a defined duration.

ED2EP.digest: Converts ED (= entry delays) into EP (= entry points). Argument = offset (can be negative). NB: Only works with integers!
ED2EP.use_when: Convert entry delays to entry points (cumulative). Constructor arg: `<offset>` (default 0). Integer-only. Use for converting relative timing to absolute timing.

Labs.digest: Recompiled as Universal Binary for Intel and ARM processors by Jeremy Bernstein (May 2022)
Labs.use_when: List absolute value — apply `abs` element-wise to a stored list when triggered. Use as the list-domain absolute value.

Ladd.digest: Recompiled as Universal Binary for Intel and ARM processors by Jeremy Bernstein (May 2022)
Ladd.use_when: List add — store a list in the right inlet; int/float in left adds to all elements (or operate list+list element-wise). Constructor args: `<list>` (default 4 3 2). Use for element-wise list addition.

Ldiv.digest: List in right inlet is stored. Int or Float in right replaces all members of stored list. 0s in right are converted to 1s.
Ldiv.use_when: List divide — element-wise division; 0s in the divisor list are converted to 1s to avoid division-by-zero. Use as the list-domain `/`.

Lmult.digest: Recompiled as Universal Binary for Intel and ARM processors by Jeremy Bernstein (May 2022)
Lmult.use_when: List multiply — element-wise multiplication. Same usage pattern as Ladd. Use as the list-domain `*`.

Lpow.digest: Note: 0^0 and( negative number)^(a fraction) are not defined. If that happens, the left value is passed through.
Lpow.use_when: List power — element-wise exponentiation. Constructor args: `<list>` (default 1 2 3 4.). Note: 0^0 and (negative)^(fraction) are passed through unchanged. Use as the list-domain `pow`.

Lrem.digest: List in right inlet is stored. Int or Float in right replaces all members of stored list. 0s in right will always return 0.
Lrem.use_when: List modulo — element-wise modulo; 0s in the divisor list always return 0 (avoids divide-by-zero crash). Use as the list-domain `%`.

Lround.digest: Recompiled as Universal Binary for Intel and ARM processors by Jeremy Bernstein (May 2022)
Lround.use_when: List round — round each list element to a grid. Constructor arg: `<grid>` (default -1 = round to integer). Use as the list-domain version of native `round`.

Lsub.digest: Recompiled as Universal Binary for Intel and ARM processors by Jeremy Bernstein (May 2022)
Lsub.use_when: List subtract — element-wise subtraction. Same usage pattern as Ladd. Use as the list-domain `-`.

Lsum.digest: Recompiled as Universal Binary for Intel and ARM processors by Jeremy Bernstein (May 2022)
Lsum.use_when: Sum of list elements. Use as a one-shot list-summation utility (avoids zl + iter chains).

MSP-iovs~.digest: Sets the I/O vector size of MSP. Watch the DSP Status window!
MSP-iovs~.use_when: Set MSP I/O Vector Size. Constructor arg: `<size>` (default 256). Use for runtime IO-vector reconfiguration; watch DSP Status window.

MSP-sigvs~.digest: Sets the signal vector size of MSP. Watch the DSP Status window!
MSP-sigvs~.use_when: Set MSP Signal Vector Size. Constructor arg: `<size>` (default 64). Use for runtime sig-vector reconfiguration.

MSP-sr~.digest: Sets the sampling rate of MSP. Watch the DSP Status window!
MSP-sr~.use_when: Set MSP sample rate. Constructor arg: `<sr>` (default 44100). Use for runtime sample-rate reconfiguration.

MSP-takeover~.digest: 1 sent to the inlet (or set as the argument) puts the scheduler in audio interrupt. 0 turns it off. Watch the DSP Status window!
MSP-takeover~.use_when: Set scheduler-in-audio-interrupt mode. Constructor arg: `<flag>` (default 0). Use to programmatically engage takeover timing.

QT-PercPlayer.digest: Plays the General MIDI Percussion sounds directly from Quicktime. The 1st inlet specifies the instrument to be played (75 = Claves, 37 = SideStick), the 2nd inlet defines the velocity (0-127).
QT-PercPlayer.use_when: Play General MIDI percussion sounds via QuickTime. 1st inlet = instrument number (75=Claves, 37=SideStick), 2nd inlet = velocity. macOS-only (legacy). Use only on systems with QuickTime General MIDI available.

QT-SndPlayer.digest: Plays the General MIDI instruments directly from Quicktime. The 1st argument specifies the instrument to be played (1 = GrandPiano, 175 = WoodBlock), the 2nd the duration (in ms).
QT-SndPlayer.use_when: Play General MIDI instrument sounds via QuickTime. Constructor args: `<instrument> <duration-ms>` (default 1=GrandPiano, 500). macOS-only (legacy). Use only on systems with QuickTime General MIDI available.

RTC-seed.digest: Seeds the different random generators of MAX. Sends out a global message called "RTC-rs" to all RTC's random based objects.
RTC-seed.use_when: Seed all RTC random generators by sending the global `RTC-rs` message. Use at session start to seed reproducible random behaviour across the whole patch.

VolAmb.digest: Controlling the ambitus between minimum and maximum dynamic with a single controller.
VolAmb.use_when: Single-controller dynamic-ambit control — map one CC to a min-max dynamic range. Use for performance-friendly velocity/dynamics control via a single fader.

add%.digest: Adding a certain percentage <int, float> to values <int, float> in left inlet.
add%.use_when: Add a configurable percentage to incoming values. Constructor arg: `<percent>` (default 20). Use to apply percentage-based offsets without scale + + chains.

alea.digest: A bang in the left inlet makes a random selection of a supply sent to right inlet before. Note that there is no repetition check of chosen elements.
alea.use_when: Random selection from a supply (sent to right inlet) with NO repetition check. Bang in left inlet draws. Use as the basic Koenig ALEA selection principle — drawn elements may repeat in close succession.

alea-rhythm.digest: Sends out rhythm bangs at random times from a geometrical time row defined by a a min. and max. entry delay (ED) and a certain number of ED-values.
alea-rhythm.use_when: Random rhythm at random times from a geometric ED row. Constructor args: `<min-ED> <max-ED> <num-EDs>` (default 100, 1000, 12 ms). Right outlet bangs at chosen EDs. Use for ALEA-principle rhythmic streams.

anti-bis.digest: Filters out immediate repetitions of integers. Right output sends a bang whenever this occurs.
anti-bis.use_when: Repetition filter — suppress immediate (bis) repetitions of integers. Right output bangs when a repetition is filtered. Use to enforce no-direct-repeat constraints.

anti-bis&osc.digest: Filters out direct repetitions and when a number has occured the penultimate time, right output sends a bang when this occurs.
anti-bis&osc.use_when: Filter both immediate repetitions and oscillations (a-b-a) — suppresses returns to penultimate values. Use for stricter no-repetition than anti-bis.

anti-interval.digest: Filters out a certain interval (which is the argument of this function) of a stream of notes and outputs a bang from right outlet whenever this interval would have occured.
anti-interval.use_when: Filter a specific interval. Constructor arg: `<interval>` (default -1 = no filter). Right outlet flashes when filtered. Use to forbid a specific melodic interval.

anti-octave.digest: Filters out octaves between two consecutive notes and outputs a bang from right outlet whenever an octave has occured.
anti-octave.use_when: Octave filter — suppress octaves between consecutive notes. Right outlet flashes. Use in counterpoint to avoid parallel octaves.

anti-octave&prime.digest: Filters out octaves and primes (i.e. tone repetitions) between two consecutive notes and outputs a bang from right outlet whenever this would have occured.
anti-octave&prime.use_when: Filter octaves AND primes (unison repetitions) between consecutive notes. Use for counterpoint where both unison and octave parallels must be suppressed.

anti-osc.digest: Filters out an integer which has occured the penultimate time. Right output sends a bang when this occurs. Does not filter out immediate repetition as it is done by"anti-bis".
anti-osc.use_when: Oscillation filter — suppress returns to penultimate values (a-b-a patterns). Right outlet flashes. Use to break trill-like oscillations.

banger.digest: Recompiled as Universal Binary for Intel and ARM processors by Jeremy Bernstein (May 2022)
banger.use_when: Multi-outlet bang distributor. Constructor arg: `<count>` (default 16). Use as a fan-out for one bang into N parallel triggers.

between.digest: Bang (or any input) in left inlet outputs a random number between min <int, float> and max <int, float> excluding max.
between.use_when: Output a random number between min and max (max excluded). Constructor args: `<min> <max>` (default 1, 12). Bang or any input triggers. Use as the standard bounded uniform random.

between-lin.digest: Bang (or any input) in left inlet outputs a random number <float> of a linear scale between min <int, float> and max <int, float> with a certain number of elements.
between-lin.use_when: Random number on a linear scale between min and max with a fixed number of elements. Constructor args: `<min> <max> <num-elements>` (default 1, 2, 5). Use for quantised-linear random within a range.

between-lin-urn.use_when: Like between-lin but with no-repetition (urn-style). Constructor args: `<min> <max> <num-elements>`. Use when you want quantised-linear random without immediate repetition.

between-log.digest: Bang (or any input) in left inlet outputs a random number <float> of a logarithmic scale between min <int, float> and max <int, float> with a certain number of elements.
between-log.use_when: Random number on a logarithmic scale between min and max with a fixed number of elements. Constructor args: `<min> <max> <num-elements>` (default 1, 2, 13). Use for log-scale random — e.g. frequencies / durations where perceptual spacing is logarithmic.

between-log-urn.use_when: Like between-log with no-repetition. Constructor args: `<min> <max> <num-elements>`. Use for log-scale random without repetition.

block-bang.digest: Filters out bangs that arrive before a certain time span ("blocking time") is over.
block-bang.use_when: Filter out bangs arriving within a 'blocking time' after a previous bang. Constructor arg: `<blocking-ms>` (default 2000). Use to enforce a minimum interval between events.

brown-melody.use_when: Brownian melody generator. Constructor args: `<min-pitch> <max-pitch> <step>` (default 24, 108, 0.1). Use for stepwise random melodic generation.

brown-rhythm.use_when: Brownian rhythm — Brownian walk on the ED scale. Constructor args: `<min-ED> <max-ED> <num-EDs> <step>` (default 100, 1000, 12, 0.2). Use for smoothly evolving ED streams.

brownian.use_when: General-purpose Brownian random walk (already covered above as random distribution).

butfirst.digest: Outputs the input list without its first element.
butfirst.use_when: Output the input list without its first element (cdr-like). Use for list head/tail processing.

butlast.digest: Outputs the input list without its last element.
butlast.use_when: Output the input list without its last element. Use for list tail-removal in compositional algorithms.

check-octaves.digest: Checks whether an interval list sums up to an octave and outputs the list only when it does not contain any hidden octaves..
check-octaves.use_when: Test whether an interval list sums to an octave; output the list only if it does NOT contain hidden octaves. Use for hidden-octave avoidance in counterpoint.

check-row.digest: Outputs the input row if it is a proper twelve-tone row; otherwise a bang is sent out of the right outlet.
check-row.use_when: Validate a 12-tone row — output if it's a proper twelve-tone row (all pitch classes 0–11 used exactly once); bang on right outlet otherwise. Use for verifying generated or input rows.

choice-rhythm.use_when: Random rhythm with chosen number of distinct EDs. Constructor args: `<min-ED> <max-ED> <num-EDs> <choice-count>` (default 100, 1000, 12, 6). Use for rhythms restricted to a subset of available EDs.

choose.digest: An int n sent to the left inlet outputs n randomly chosen elements from a given supply. Contains a repetition check.
choose.use_when: Choose n elements at random from a supply with repetition check. `<n>` in left inlet sets count; supply on right. Use for distinct-sample drawing — e.g. pick 5 unique notes from a 12-tone supply.

choose-intervals.digest: flashes when the sum of the chosen intervals would have been an octave
choose-intervals.use_when: Choose intervals at random with octave-flash detection. Constructor arg: `<count>`. Right outlet flashes when chosen interval sums would have made an octave. Use in 12-tone composition contexts where octave avoidance matters.

collect.digest: Collects numbers (ints or floats) and symbols sent to the left inlet into a list. A bang in the right inlet clears the list.
collect.use_when: Collect numbers/symbols sent to the left inlet into a list. Bang in right inlet clears. Use as a list builder when accumulating values over time.

contrario.digest: A number <int, float> in left inlet outputs a complimentary number. The axis number <float, int> has to be sent to the right inlet or given as an argument to the function.
contrario.use_when: Output the complement of a number around an axis — `2*axis - x`. Constructor or right-inlet arg: `<axis>`. Use for melodic inversion: transform a melodic line into its inversion around a pivot.

cos-slider.digest: Transforms a linear slider into a cosine function.
cos-slider.use_when: Transform a linear slider into a cosine-mapped slider. Use for non-linear slider mapping (e.g. for parameters where sensitivity should bunch at the extremes).

count-bang.digest: Counts the number of received bangs.
count-bang.use_when: Count received bangs. Use as a bang counter — sums total bangs since last clear.

countdown.digest: An int sent to the inlet causes the object to start a countdown (in seconds).
countdown.use_when: Start a countdown in seconds (int sent to inlet). Use for timed event scheduling.

cp-pan~.use_when: Constant-power panner (Chris Dobrian). Constructor arg: `<initial-pan>` (default 0.4). Use for stereo panning that maintains perceived loudness across the field.

cresc-decresc.use_when: Generate a velocity transition over a duration. Constructor args: `<start-vel> <end-vel> <duration-ms>` (default 1, 127, 5000). Up = cresc, down = decresc. Use for programmable dynamic transitions.

crossfade~.digest: Crossfades two input signals according to a sine function which guarantees that loudness of the result is constant.
crossfade~.use_when: Sine-shaped crossfade between two signals — guarantees constant total loudness. Use as a click-free 2-input crossfader.

dB2lin.use_when: Convert dB to linear amplitude. Use as the inverse of lin2dB; chain with native math objects when you need both representations.

del-fb~.use_when: Delay with feedback. Constructor args: `<delay-ms> <feedback-dB>` (default 8000, -12). Use for self-running delay textures with built-in feedback gain.

display-row.digest: Displays a numerical 12-tone-row in musical notation.
display-row.use_when: Display a numerical 12-tone row in musical notation. Use for visualising rows during composition.

divmod.digest: divmod is the same as / and % for positive left operands. For negative left operands divmod sticks to modulo arithmetic wheras / and % do something else. divmod does not allow right operands below 1 (they become 1). Only works with integers.
divmod.use_when: Combined divide + modulo with consistent negative-number behaviour. Constructor arg: `<divisor>`. divmod sticks to modulo arithmetic for negative left operands (where native `/` and `%` do something else). Use when arithmetic with negative dividends must wrap consistently — pitch math, cyclic indexing into negative ranges.

dur-from-ED.digest: Calculates note durations from entry delays (ED) and a duration factor. dur-factor = 1: legato; dur-factor < 1: portato, staccato; dur-factor > 1: pedal effect
dur-from-ED.use_when: Calculate note durations from EDs and a duration factor. Factor 1=legato, <1=portato/staccato, >1=pedal. Constructor arg: `<duration-factor>` (default 1). Use to derive note durations from rhythmic EDs.

first.digest: Outputs the first element of a list.
first.use_when: Output the first element of a list (car). Use for list head extraction.

first-bang.digest: Outputs only the first bang of a stream of bangs (e.g. rhythm) until the right inlet receives a (reset) bang.
first-bang.use_when: Output only the first bang in a stream until a reset bang arrives on the right inlet. Use for rising-edge detection on bang streams.

first-shot.digest: Outputs only the first message of a stream of messages (bangs, ints, floats, symbols, lists) until the right inlet receives a (reset) bang.
first-shot.use_when: Output only the first message of any type until reset. Use as the message-typed version of first-bang.

frq2note.digest: Converts frequency into a note (= MIDI key number).
frq2note.use_when: Convert frequency (Hz) to MIDI note number. Use as the standard ftom for control-rate values.

geom.digest: Int in right inlet outputs the nth member of a geometrical row. The other parameters are reference value <int, float> and the proportional factor q <float>.
geom.use_when: Nth element of a geometric row with reference value and proportion factor q. Constructor args: `<reference> <q>` (default 20, 1.25). Use for geometric scaling of values.

grid-rhythm.use_when: Time-grid based rhythm. Constructor args: `<grid-ms> <multiples-list> <selection>` (default 125, 10, 3). Use for grid-quantised rhythms — pick multiples of a base grid.

groove-rhythm.digest: Generates a rhythm based on a time grid and a list of multiples, from which elements are picked according to the chosen selection principle.
groove-rhythm.use_when: Rhythm based on a time grid and a list of multiples chosen by selection principle. Constructor arg: `<grid-ms>` (default 125). Use for groove-style patterns derived from grid-multiples.

group.digest: 1992 by James McCartney; rewritten as an abstraction 2006 by Karlheinz Essl
group.use_when: Group numbers into sublists of size n. Constructor arg: `<group-size>` (default 4). Use to chunk a stream into fixed-size groups (e.g. for chord building from a melodic stream).

group-rhythm.use_when: Grouped-rhythm generator. Constructor args: `<min-ED> <max-ED> <num-EDs> <group-prob>` (default 100, 1000, 12, 0.5). Use for rhythmic streams with chord-/grace-note-style groupings.

group-rhythm2.use_when: Variant of group-rhythm with different grouping behaviour. Use as the alternate group-rhythm flavour when the grouping logic of the original isn't quite right.

groups.digest: 1993-98 by Karlheinz Essl after Gottfried Michael Koenig, vs. 2.1
groups.use_when: Multi-group splitter — splits a list into groups by a configurable scheme. Use for hierarchical list grouping in compositional algorithms.

inc-dec.digest: Functional implementation of the graphical user interface object IncDec.
inc-dec.use_when: Functional implementation of the GUI IncDec object. Use as a programmable inc/decrement source without the GUI footprint.

infinity-row.digest: Discovered by Per Nørgård (1959), further variants by Peter Richter (1996). NB: The fourth, sixteenth, sixtyfourth etc. note of the row is identical!
infinity-row.use_when: Per Nørgård's infinity row (1959 / Peter Richter 1996 variants) — every 4th, 16th, 64th... element of the row equals the first. Constructor args define starting parameters. Use for infinity-row-based generative composition.

insert.use_when: Insert an element into a list at a position. Index 0 places at the start; bigger-than-list-size appends at the end. Use for ordered list construction.

integ.digest: Accumulates input values <int, float> until a bang (or any input) is sent to the right inlet.
integ.use_when: Integrate (accumulate) input values until a bang in the right inlet resets. Use as a running-sum integrator with explicit reset.

intv2ratio.digest: Converts an interval (int = tempered or float = any micro-interval) into a ratio.
intv2ratio.use_when: Convert an interval (semitones, int = tempered or float = microtonal) to a frequency ratio. Use for working with just-intonation analysis from semitone deviations.

inv.digest: Calculates the inversion of a number.
inv.use_when: Calculate the inversion of a number — like contrario but with implicit axis. Use for quick numerical inversions without specifying an axis.

key-bang.digest: Sends out a bang whenever a certain key is pressed. The argument is the ASCII-number of that key.
key-bang.use_when: Send a bang when a specific key is pressed. Constructor arg: `<ASCII-code>` (default 13 = return). Use for keyboard-triggered events without rolling key + select chains.

last.digest: Outputs the last element of a list.
last.use_when: Output the last element of a list. Use for list tail-element extraction.

lin2dB.use_when: Convert linear amplitude to dB. Use whenever you need the dB form of an amplitude value (display, threshold comparison).

listlen.digest: Outputs the length of a list or anything arriving at its inlet. Bang in left inlet retriggers output of length of previous list. List or anything in inlet sends length of list out the outlet.
listlen.use_when: Output the length of an incoming list (or anything). Bang re-emits previous length. Use to query list size for downstream sizing logic.

listreg.digest: 1992 by James McCartney; rewritten as an abstraction 2006 by Karlheinz Essl
listreg.use_when: List register — store and retrieve a list. Use as a one-list memory cell.

loop-buff~.digest: Loops a buffer and changes the transposition resp. speed. A negative transposition factor plays the buffer backwards.
loop-buff~.use_when: Buffer-based stereo looper with transposition control. Constructor args: `<buffer-name> <pitch>` (default snd, 0.73). Negative pitch plays backwards. Use for buffer-based loop playback with pitch / speed control.

loop-sf~.digest: Loops a selected mono sndfile and changes the transposition resp. speed. A negative factor plays the file backwards.
loop-sf~.use_when: Soundfile-based stereo looper with transposition. Negative factor plays backwards. Use for direct-from-file looped playback (no buffer pre-load needed).

make-ED-scale.digest: Generates a geometrical scale of entry delays (ED) between a min. ED and a max. ED with a certain number of elements.
make-ED-scale.use_when: Geometric scale of entry delays between min and max ED with N elements. Constructor args: `<min-ED> <max-ED> <count>` (default 250., 1000., 6). Use to build the ED scale that drives most RTC rhythm objects.

make-choice-list.digest: max. list element (must be larger than min. list element)
make-choice-list.use_when: Generate a list of choices within size and value bounds. Constructor args: `<size> <max> <min-val> <max-val>`. Use for parameterised choice-list generation in compositional algorithms.

make-scale.digest: Generates an integer list with a certain number of ints starting from a given value.
make-scale.use_when: Generate an integer scale of n elements starting from a value. Constructor arg: `<count>` (default 1). Use for arithmetic scale generation.

make-trans-scale.use_when: Generate a transition scale between start and end values with n elements. Constructor args: `<start> <end> <count>` (default 2., 1., 13). The transition type (linear, exp, log) is configurable. Use for parametric scale interpolation.

markov.digest: Creates a matrix where transitions are only possible between adjacent elements. Result similar to "sneak" random!
markov.use_when: Markov-chain transition object — input matrix where row i column j gives the probability of transitioning from state i to state j. Use for Markov-modelled state sequences (melodies, rhythms, formal structures).

markov-harmony.digest: Flashes when there are no transitions possible ("I am stuck!")
markov-harmony.use_when: Markov harmonic transitions with stuck-state flash on dead ends. Use for Markov-modelled chord-progression generation with explicit feedback when transitions are impossible.

markov-rhythm.use_when: Markov-chained entry-delay (ED) selection. Constructor arg: `<seed>` (default 5). Right inlet receives the rhythmic supply, third inlet a Markov matrix; left inlet integer chooses the next ED accordingly. Use for Markov-modelled rhythmic streams.

matrix12.digest: 1994-2006 by Charles Baker and R. Albert Falesch, vs. 2.0
matrix12.use_when: 12-tone matrix generator — input a row, output the standard 12×12 transposition/inversion matrix used in serial analysis. Use for generating the basic 12-tone matrix from a row.

max-cursor.digest: 1 or 0 send to the inlet (or given as the argument) shows or hides the mouse cursor.
max-cursor.use_when: Show/hide mouse cursor (1=show, 0=hide). Use for kiosk / fullscreen patches that need to hide the cursor programmatically.

max-menubar.digest: 1 or 0 send to the inlet (or given as the argument) turns Max's menubar mode on or off.
max-menubar.use_when: Toggle Max's menubar mode (1/0). Constructor arg: `<initial>` (default 1). Use for fullscreen presentation patches.

max-overdrive.digest: 1 or 0 send to the inlet (or given as the argument) turns Max's Overdrive mode on or off.
max-overdrive.use_when: Toggle Max's Overdrive mode (1/0). Constructor arg: `<initial>` (default 0). Use to programmatically engage scheduler-in-audio-interrupt for tighter timing.

max-quit.use_when: Immediately quit Max. Use only with great care — typically for scripted exits, kiosk shutdown.

member.digest: The number  list at the right inlet is searched for occurences of the ints that arrive at the left inlet. The output is a one if the list includes the int and zero if it doesn't.
member.use_when: Test whether each int in the left-inlet stream occurs in the right-inlet list. Output 1 (member) or 0 (not). Use as a membership predicate for list filtering.

metro-dev%.digest: Introduces irregularities to an equal pulsation by deviating from a given pulsation speed ("inégalité", "rubato", "human feeling)."
metro-dev%.use_when: Metronome with percentage deviation from base pulsation — adds 'inégalité' / rubato / human-feel. Constructor args: `<base-ms> <deviation-percent>` (default 250., 15.). Use for humanised metro-style timing.

ms2sec.digest: Converts milliseconds into seconds. The argument sets the number of decimal places.
ms2sec.use_when: Convert milliseconds to seconds. Constructor arg: `<decimal-places>` (default 2). Use whenever a millisecond value should be displayed as seconds with controlled precision.

multiple.use_when: Multiple-of-N tester. Use to detect when an int is a multiple of a given divisor — useful for periodic event triggering.

neutral-harmony.digest: Building an interval row by alternating a given interval (e.g. 3) with its compliment (e.g. 8). Outputs pitch classes (i.e. notes without any register informations).
neutral-harmony.use_when: Build an interval row by alternating an interval with its complement (e.g. 3 alternating with 8). Constructor arg: `<interval>` (default 6). Output is pitch classes. Use for symmetric-interval harmonic structures.

normalize-row.digest: Normalization of a twelve-tone row - the pitch classes are transposed in a way that the new row always starts with 0 (i.e. pitch class c).
normalize-row.use_when: Normalise a 12-tone row so it starts with pitch class 0 (transposes the row down). Use for canonical-form storage of rows.

note2cents.digest: Shows how many cents a microtonal note <float> is deviating from a tempered one.
note2cents.use_when: Show how many cents a microtonal note (float) deviates from the nearest tempered semitone. Use when working with microtonal tunings — exposes the deviation explicitly for display or quantisation.

note2frq.digest: Converts a note (= MIDI key number or MIDI cents) into frequency.
note2frq.use_when: Convert a MIDI note number (or MIDI cents float) to frequency in Hz. Use as the standard mtof — accepts microtonal floats too.

note2pitch.digest: Outputs the pitch class <integer> (i.e. a note without register information) of a note (i.e. MIDI note number) <int>.
note2pitch.use_when: Extract the pitch class (0–11) from a MIDI note number — essentially `% 12`. Use for octave-equivalent comparisons (chord recognition, pitch-class set theory).

note2st.digest: Outputs the intervals of an incoming stream of notes. The argument resp. the number sent to the right inlet determines the reference note.
note2st.use_when: Output intervals (in semitones) between consecutive notes of a stream. Constructor arg: `<reference-note>` (default 60). Use to convert an absolute note stream into a relative interval stream.

nth.digest: 1993 by Serge Lemouton/IRCAM, rewritten as an abstraction 2010 by Karlheinz Essl
nth.use_when: Output the nth element of a list (0-indexed). Use as a list indexer.

panning.digest: Equal power panning based on John Chowning's paper "Simulation of Moving Sound Sources".
panning.use_when: Equal-power panning based on Chowning's 'Simulation of Moving Sound Sources'. Use for ambient / moving-source spatial models with proper psychoacoustic basis.

pass.digest: vs. 1.1: seedable by sending an int via the "RTC-rs" message
pass.use_when: Probability gate — let a configurable percentage of inputs through. Constructor arg: `<percent>` (default 50). Seedable via `RTC-rs <int>`. Use for stochastic event filtering.

pass-bang.digest: Let every nth bang (= argument resp. int sent to the right inlet) pass.
pass-bang.use_when: Let every nth bang pass through. Constructor or right-inlet arg: `<n>` (default 15). Use as a beat divider — fire on every Nth bang.

per2pass.digest: Converts a periodicity factor <float between 0 and 1> into a percentage value <int between 0 and 100> for the pass-object.
per2pass.use_when: Convert a periodicity factor (0.–1. float) to a percentage value (0–100 int) for the pass object. Use to translate musical 'periodicity' (1.=fully periodic) into pass-object probabilities.

periodic.use_when: Periodic value generator. Constructor args: `<period> <bias>` (default 10, 0.5). Use as a structured periodic oscillator on values rather than signals.

permutate.digest: The permutation mechanism prevents direct repetitions of notes!
permutate.use_when: Permutation with no direct repetitions. Use as the no-immediate-repetition permutation variant.

permutate-row.digest: Permutates a given row using its own structure as a permutation program. NB: The given row must be a twelve-note-row which smallest index is 0.
permutate-row.use_when: Permutate a 12-tone row using its own structure as the permutation program — input must be a row whose smallest index is 0. Use for structural permutation in serial composition.

permute.digest: 1992 by James McCartney, UB-build 2006 by R. Albert Falesch
permute.use_when: Permutation generator — outputs successive permutations of a list. Optional list arg sets the default. Use for systematic permutation in serial composition.

pianoteq6-player.digest: Controls how fast this energy is transferred: the higher the inertia, the longer it takes to be transferred.
pianoteq6-player.use_when: Driver for Pianoteq 6 (commercial physical-modelling piano). 'Inertia' attribute controls energy-transfer rate. Use only when Pianoteq 6 is installed.

pianoteq7-player.digest: Controls how fast this energy is transferred: the higher the inertia, the longer it takes to be transferred.
pianoteq7-player.use_when: Driver for Pianoteq 7. Same notes as pianoteq6-player. Use with Pianoteq 7 installations.

piece.digest: Outputs the elements between a start.index ("from") and an end.index ("to") from a numerical list send to the leftmost inlet before.
piece.use_when: Output the elements between a start and end index from a numerical list. Use to extract a sublist range.

pitch-from-intervals.use_when: Generate a pitch sequence from randomly selected intervals. Constructor arg: `<num-intervals>` (default 3). Use for interval-driven melodic generation.

pitch-from-row-mod.digest: flashes when the next transposition starts
pitch-from-row-mod.use_when: Modular extraction of pitches from a 12-tone row, with transposition flash on outlet. Use for row-based generative pitch streams with phase tracking.

pitch2note.digest: Combines pitch class and register into a note (i.e. MIDI note number).
pitch2note.use_when: Combine a pitch class (0–11) and a register (octave number) into a MIDI note number. Constructor arg: `<register>` (default 4 = middle). Use as the inverse of note2pitch.

play.use_when: General-purpose play trigger. Constructor args: `<velocity> <duration-ms> <channel>` (default 127, 1000, 1). Use as a one-stop note-emit object.

play-samp~.use_when: Stereo sample player. Use as the standard sample-playback voice in RTC-lib audio chains.

position.digest: Sorts a list in the left inlet using the index numbers in the right inlet.
position.use_when: Sort a list using indices supplied in the right inlet. Use as a custom-order sorter — supply a permutation in the right inlet to apply it to the left list.

random-env.digest: RTC implementation of Sonology's Random Envelope Generator as it was defined by Gottfried Michael Koenig et al. Thanks to Kees Tazelaar for the inspiration!
random-env.use_when: Random envelope generator (RTC implementation of Sonology's REG). Constructor args define value and time bounds. Use for generative envelope shapes with structured randomness.

random-norep.digest: Outputs random numbers <int> without direct (= immediate) repetitions.
random-norep.use_when: Random integers with no immediate repetition. Constructor arg: `<max>` (default 3). Use for cleaner random-walk-style streams where consecutive identical values must be avoided.

random-pan~.use_when: Random panning over time. Constructor arg: `<rate>` (default 0.4). Use for stochastic spatialisation.

random-ramp.digest: A simple musical example for using the random-ramp object:
random-ramp.use_when: Generate randomly fluctuating ramps within a value scale and ramp-duration range. Constructor args: `<min-val> <max-val> <num-vals> <min-dur> <max-dur>` (default 0, 255, 7, 1000, 4000 ms). Use for generative LFO-like control with structured randomness.

random-ramp-urn.use_when: Like random-ramp but with no-repetition (urn-style value selection). Same args. Use for ramp generation that explores all values before repeating.

random-rampf.use_when: Float version of random-ramp. Constructor args: `<min-val-float> <max-val-float> <num-vals> <min-dur> <max-dur>`. Use for fractional value scales (vs integer-valued random-ramp).

random-rampf-urn.use_when: Float, no-repetition version of random-ramp. Same args. Use for the float, urn-style variant of random ramps.

random-row.digest: Generates a twelve-tone row by random. The elements of the row range from 0 to 11.
random-row.use_when: Generate a random twelve-tone row (random permutation of 0–11). Use as the standard 12-tone row generator for serial composition.

randomseeder.digest: Seeds the different random generators of MAX. Sends out a global message called "RTC-rs" to all RTC's random based objects.
randomseeder.use_when: Seed all RTC random generators (alternative to RTC-seed). Same effect — sends `RTC-rs` globally. Use whichever fits naming preference.

ratio.digest: Chooses elements from a supply <int list> which are repeated according to a multiple list <int list> by using the "series" selection principle. - NB: The supply must be a list of numbers!
ratio.use_when: Choose elements from a supply repeating each according to a multiple list, using the SERIES selection principle. Right outlet flashes when supply is exhausted. Use for proportional repetition patterns — e.g. element A occurs 3x, B occurs 2x, C occurs 1x in a structured sequence.

ratio2intv.digest: Converts a ratio into an interval (int = tempered or float = any micro- interval).
ratio2intv.use_when: Inverse of intv2ratio — convert a frequency ratio to an interval in semitones. Use for converting just-intonation ratios back to a familiar interval representation.

reanimate.use_when: Send a bang on right outlet if no input bang has arrived for x ms. Constructor arg: `<reanimation-ms>` (default 3000). Use as a 'wake-up' or watchdog timer.

remove.digest: Removes the nth element of a list. When the position (= index) <int> is bigger than the the list size, the last element will be truncated.
remove.use_when: Remove the nth element from a list (truncates the last if n > size). Use for list-element deletion by index.

remove-dc~.digest: Removes the DC offset of an audio signal.
remove-dc~.use_when: DC-offset removal filter. Use as a single-object DC blocker on signal chains where DC accumulation is possible (long convolution, chained nonlinearities).

repchord-rhythm.digest: Generates a rhythm which repetition rate and chord size are depending on the index of the chosen ED. The following parameters allow you to model a lot of different musical situations:
repchord-rhythm.use_when: Rhythm with chord repetition rate and chord size depending on the chosen ED's index. Constructor args: `<min-ED> <max-ED> <num-EDs> <max-chord> <periodicity>` (default 50, 2000, 12, 6, 0.8). `periodicity` (0–1) sets repetition probability. Use for ED-index-driven rhythmic-chord patterns.

repeat-ED.digest: Int in left inlet sends out a certain number of rhythm bangs with an entry delay (ED) given in right inlet.
repeat-ED.use_when: Send n bangs at a given ED. Int in left = bang count, ED in right. Constructor arg: `<ED-ms>` (default 300). Use for n-bang bursts at fixed timing.

replace.use_when: Replace an element in a list at a given position. Out-of-range positions are clamped. Use for in-place list updates.

reverse.digest: 1992 by James McCartney; rewritten as an abstraction 2006 by Karlheinz Essl
reverse.use_when: Reverse a list. Use as the list-domain reverse — bang re-emits last reversed list.

rit-acc.digest: Generates a ritardando or an accelerando, depending on the slope parameter. If it is < 1, the tempo becomes faster, when it is > 1, the speed slows down.
rit-acc.use_when: Generate a ritardando or accelerando via a slope parameter. Constructor args: `<initial-ED> <slope>` (default 75, 1.1). Slope <1 = accel, >1 = rit. Use for tempo-curve transitions.

rota.digest: A bang in the left inlet starts cycling through the supply sent to the middle inlet before. A bang sent to the rightmost inlet resets the cycling mechanism.
rota.use_when: Cyclic with bang-driven offset. Bang in left inlet starts cycling through the supply; bang in right inlet resets. Use as Koenig's ROTA — cyclic with explicit reset semantics.

rotate.digest: Cyclically rotate a list. Bang in left inlet retriggers output. List in left inlet gets rotated. Int in right inlet sets rotation.
rotate.use_when: Cyclically rotate a list. Constructor arg: `<rotation>` (default 2). Bang in left re-triggers; int in right resets rotation amount. Use for rotation-based variation of list patterns.

roundto.digest: A number sent to right inlet is rounded onto a certain "grid" <int, float> sent to right inlet.
roundto.use_when: Round to an arbitrary grid (int or float). Right inlet sets the grid value (default 0.5). Use for snap-to-grid behaviour with non-integer grids — e.g. quantize to 0.25 (16th-note grid) or 0.5 (eighth grid).

samp10~.digest: Allows a polyphony of up to 10 voices at the same time.
samp10~.use_when: 10-voice polyphonic stereo sample player. Use for moderate-polyphony sample playback.

samp20~.digest: Allows a polyphony of up to 20 voices at the same time.
samp20~.use_when: 20-voice polyphonic stereo sample player. Use for higher-polyphony sample playback when samp10~ runs out of voices.

scale-changer.digest: open to construct a specific start scale
scale-changer.use_when: Construct a starting scale interactively. Use for runtime scale construction in performance contexts.

schweller.use_when: Crescendo-decrescendo (swell) generator. Constructor args define velocity range and timing (default 15, 127, 3000, 0.25). Use for automatic swell shapes (German 'Schweller' = swelling).

scramble.digest: Scrambles the order of a list of numbers. Bang in left inlet retriggers output. List in inlet gets scrambled and sent out the outlet.
scramble.use_when: Random shuffle of a list. Bang re-triggers (re-shuffles). Use as the standard list scrambler.

sect.digest: 1992 by James McCartney; rewritten as an abstraction 2006 by Karlheinz Essl
sect.use_when: List intersection — output the elements common to two input lists. Use for set-intersection on numerical lists.

sel-princ.digest: Changes between the selection principles ALEA, SERIES and SEQUENCE (as defined by G. M. Koenig) and ROTA according to the number sent to the middle inlet.
sel-princ.use_when: Selection-principle switcher — toggle between ALEA, SERIES, SEQUENCE, ROTA based on a number sent to the middle inlet. Constructor arg: `<initial-mode>` (default 3). Use to expose Koenig's selection-principle choice as a runtime control parameter.

sequence.digest: A bang in the left inlet outputs the next element of a supply <any list> sent to right inlet before. When the list is exhausted it is looped.
sequence.use_when: Cyclic supply iterator — bang outputs the next element; supply on right inlet. Loops when exhausted; right outlet bangs on the last element. Use as the Koenig SEQUENCE selection principle — strict cyclic ordering.

serial-rhythm.digest: Serial permutation (i.e. with repetition check) of entry delays (ED) from a geometrical row defined by a min. ED, a max. ED and a certain number of ED-values.
serial-rhythm.use_when: Serial permutation of EDs from a geometric row with repetition check. Constructor args: `<min-ED> <max-ED> <num-EDs>` (default 100, 1000, 12). Use as the SERIES-principle rhythmic stream.

series.digest: Outputs a bang when the supply is exhausted - i.e. when the last element of the supply has been chosen and the supply has to be refreshed again.
series.use_when: Random with repetition check — like alea but each element is used exactly once before any repeats (urn-style). Right outlet bangs when the supply is exhausted (at which point it refreshes). Use as Koenig's SERIES selection principle.

show.digest: Displays a list in a message box.
show.use_when: Display a list in a message box. Use as an inline display widget for list values.

show-pitchclass.digest: Shows the pitch class of a MIDI note number.
show-pitchclass.use_when: Display the pitch class of a MIDI note number with note-name labelling (C, C#, etc.). Use as a debugging / display widget for note streams.

slice.digest: Splits up a list in two. Argument determines the number of elements of list to send out the left outlet. The rest goes out the right outlet.
slice.use_when: Split a list into two — left outlet gets the first `<count>` elements, right outlet gets the rest. Constructor arg: `<count>` (default 4). Use to head/tail-split a list.

smooth-line.digest: Interpolates smoothly between consecutive input values.
smooth-line.use_when: Smooth interpolation between consecutive input values. Constructor arg: `<smoothing-ms>` (default 1000). Use as a click-free value smoother for parameter streams.

sneak.use_when: Markov-style random where transitions are only between adjacent elements — like a constrained random walk. Use for melodic contours that move stepwise rather than jumping.

sneak-random.digest: Outputs random number between 0 and n-1 whereas two adjacent numbers are always direct neighbours. 2nd argument: start value.
sneak-random.use_when: Random integer 0..(n-1) where consecutive outputs are always adjacent. Constructor args: `<n> <start-value>`. Use for constrained-step random walks (smoother contours than full random).

sneak-rhythm.digest: Generates a rhythm which "sneaks" between neigboured ED of a scale - this will result in a sort of continuous ritardando/accelerando/rubato etc.
sneak-rhythm.use_when: Rhythm that 'sneaks' between neighbouring EDs of a scale, producing continuous ritardando/accelerando/rubato. Constructor args: `<min-ED> <max-ED> <num-EDs>` (default 100, 1000, 12). Use for organic tempo fluctuation.

sort.digest: 1992 by James McCartney; rewritten as an abstraction 2006 by Karlheinz Essl
sort.use_when: Sort a list — direction argument (`-1` = descending, default). Use as the list sort utility.

sqrt-pan~.use_when: Square-root law panner. Constructor arg: `<initial-pan>` (default 0.4). Use for the sqrt panning law (gentler L/R taper than constant-power).

st2pitch.digest: Accumulates incoming intervals and outputs pitch classes (i.e. notes without register information) at the left outlet resp. notes (= MIDI note numbers) at the right outlet.
st2pitch.use_when: Inverse of note2st — accumulate semitone intervals starting from a reference, output pitch classes (left) and notes (right). Constructor arg: `<reference>` (default 60). Use to convert interval streams back to note streams.

sum.use_when: Sum of input list elements. Use as a one-shot summation utility.

super-rhythm.digest: select a given supply of ED's
super-rhythm.use_when: Composite rhythm model — combines an ED supply, a selection principle, and probabilistic periodicity. Constructor args define supply and behaviour (default 3, 10, 1, 4, 0.7). Use as RTC-lib's most complete rhythm-generation primitive.

trans.use_when: Nth member of a transition row between two values in n steps. Constructor args: `<start> <end> <count> <type>` (default 500, 2000, 12, 0). Use for indexed-access to interpolated transition values.

trans-exp.digest: 1993-2010 by Karlheinz Essl, Frank Barknecht and R. Albert Falesch
trans-exp.use_when: Nth member of an exponential transition row. Constructor args: `<start> <end> <count>` (default 500, 2000, 12). Use for exponential interpolation curves.

trans-lin.digest: nth member of a linear transition row
trans-lin.use_when: Nth member of a linear transition row. Same args as trans-exp. Use for linear interpolation curves.

trans-log.digest: nth member of a logarithmic transition row
trans-log.use_when: Nth member of a logarithmic transition row. Same args as trans-exp. Use for log interpolation curves.

transp-pitch.digest: Transposes pitch classes by a certain interval.
transp-pitch.use_when: Transpose pitch classes by an interval (mod 12). Use for pitch-class set transposition without leaving the 0–11 space.

transpose-row.use_when: Transpose a 12-tone row by a given interval. Use for systematic row transposition in serial composition.

union.digest: Outputs a list which combines the atoms of  both input lists without duplicates
union.use_when: List union — output the combined elements of two lists with duplicates removed. Use for set-union on numerical lists.

unique.digest: Outputs a list comprised of those items in the left input list that are not contained in the right input list.
unique.use_when: List difference — output elements in the left list that are NOT in the right list. Use for set-difference / list filtering by exclusion.

xrandom.digest: maximum range of random numbers (must be larger than 1)
xrandom.use_when: Random with completion notification — outputs all numbers in range, then bangs when exhausted. Constructor arg: `<max>` (must be > 1). Use as a self-resetting urn that signals when a full cycle completes.

rtt.clockin~.digest: Convert a trigger-based clock signal into a phasor-based one
rtt.clockin~.use_when: Convert a trigger-based clock signal into a phasor-based one.

rtt.clock~.digest: Generate ramp signals in beats per minute.
rtt.clock~.use_when: Generate ramp signals in beats per minute.

rtt.counter~.digest: Count and resequence impulses and steps
rtt.counter~.use_when: Count and resequence impulses and steps.

rtt.euclidean~.digest: Generate patterns using the euclidean algorithm
rtt.euclidean~.use_when: Generate patterns using the euclidean algorithm.

rtt.feel~.digest: Modulate a phasor ramp for loose timing.
rtt.feel~.use_when: Modulate a phasor ramp for loose timing.

rtt.loop~.digest: Convert a phasor signal into a step counter
rtt.loop~.use_when: Convert a phasor signal into a step counter.

rtt.makecc~.digest: Convert signals to MIDI control change
rtt.makecc~.use_when: Convert signals to MIDI control change.

rtt.notes~.digest: Store and playback a sequence of notes.
rtt.notes~.use_when: Store and playback a sequence of notes.

rtt.resync~.digest: Reset the phase of a ramp signal.
rtt.resync~.use_when: Reset the phase of a ramp signal.

rtt.scala~.digest: Map MIDI note values to any scale
rtt.scala~.use_when: Map MIDI note values to any scale.

rtt.sequence~.digest: Store and play back lists of values
rtt.sequence~.use_when: Store and play back lists of values.

rtt.swing~.digest: Create variations in a ramp's slope for shuffle.
rtt.swing~.use_when: Create variations in a ramp's slope for shuffle.

rtt.upright~.digest: Convert any signal to a phasor-style clock.
rtt.upright~.use_when: Convert any signal to a phasor-style clock.

sadam.dom.digest: A Document Object Model (DOM) interface for Max.
sadam.dom.use_when: A Document Object Model (DOM) interface for Max.

sadam.envelopeGenerator.digest: Expr-based envelope generator for function objects
sadam.envelopeGenerator.use_when: Expr-based envelope generator for function objects.

sadam.fromBytes.digest: Convert a sequence of bytes into numbers
sadam.fromBytes.use_when: Convert a sequence of bytes into numbers.

sadam.gcd.digest: Compute the Greatest Common Divisor of two integers
sadam.gcd.use_when: Compute the Greatest Common Divisor of two integers.

sadam.lcm.digest: Compute the Least Common Multiple of two integers
sadam.lcm.use_when: Compute the Least Common Multiple of two integers.

sadam.limits.digest: Get minimum and maximum finite values of Max data types
sadam.limits.use_when: Get minimum and maximum finite values of Max data types.

sadam.lzo.digest: Lossless data compression and decompression using the LZO library
sadam.lzo.use_when: Lossless data compression and decompression using the LZO library.

sadam.normalize~.digest: An object that sets the gain of a source to the level of a reference signal
sadam.normalize~.use_when: An object that sets the gain of a source to the level of a reference signal.

sadam.phasor~.digest: Phasor object with maximal resting state
sadam.phasor~.use_when: Phasor object with maximal resting state.

sadam.prime.digest: Compute the closest prime numbers and the prime factorization of a positive integer
sadam.prime.use_when: Compute the closest prime numbers and the prime factorization of a positive integer.

sadam.sax.digest: A Simple API for XML (SAX) interface for Max.
sadam.sax.use_when: A Simple API for XML (SAX) interface for Max.

sadam.sortLists.digest: Sort a set of lists simultaneously
sadam.sortLists.use_when: Sort a set of lists simultaneously.

sadam.standardMap~.digest: A chaotic oscillator based on Chirikov's Standard Map
sadam.standardMap~.use_when: A chaotic oscillator based on Chirikov's Standard Map.

sadam.stat.digest: Get mean and standard deviation of a number sequence
sadam.stat.use_when: Get mean and standard deviation of a number sequence.

sadam.tcpReceiver.digest: Receive data from the network using the TCP protocol
sadam.tcpReceiver.use_when: Receive data from the network using the TCP protocol.

sadam.tcpSender.digest: Send data through the network using the TCP protocol
sadam.tcpSender.use_when: Send data through the network using the TCP protocol.

sadam.toBytes.digest: Convert numbers into a sequence of bytes
sadam.toBytes.use_when: Convert numbers into a sequence of bytes.

sadam.udpClient.digest: UDP Sender/Receiver object with fixed source port
sadam.udpClient.use_when: UDP Sender/Receiver object with fixed source port.

sadam.udpReceiver.digest: Receive data from the network using the UDP protocol
sadam.udpReceiver.use_when: Receive data from the network using the UDP protocol.

sadam.udpSender.digest: Send data through the network using the UDP protocol
sadam.udpSender.use_when: Send data through the network using the UDP protocol.

sdt.bouncing~.digest: [solids, control] Control algorithm to render bouncing sounds.
sdt.bouncing~.use_when: Control algorithm that schedules a series of impacts with realistic timing decay (a ball bouncing). Drives sdt.impact~ via the standard SDT control→interactor signal connection. Configure with `@height` (initial drop height), `@restitution` (bounce energy retention 0–1), `@irregularity` (timing jitter). Send `bang` to start a bouncing sequence. Use as the trigger source for bouncing-object sounds; pair with sdt.impact~ + sdt.modal/inertial for the resonating body.

sdt.breaking~.digest: [solids, control] Control algorithm to render breaking sounds.
sdt.breaking~.use_when: Control algorithm emitting a burst of impact events for breaking/shattering. Drives sdt.impact~. `@crushingEnergy` and `@storedEnergy` control overall power, `@fragmentation` controls how many pieces, `@granularity` controls fragment size. Send `bang` to fire one breaking event. Use for glass / ceramic / wood breaking; pair with sdt.impact~ + sdt.modal for the body.

sdt.bubble~.use_when: Liquid model — single bubble pop. `@radius` sets bubble size (drives pitch); `@riseFactor` controls the chirp glide as the bubble surfaces. Send `bang` to emit one bubble. Use directly as a bubble source; layer many sdt.bubble~ instances for boiling, fizzing, underwater textures.

sdt.crumpling~.digest: [solids, control] Control algorithm to render a granular, crumpling/crushing sound.
sdt.crumpling~.use_when: Granular impact stream for crushing/crumpling textures (paper, leaves, gravel). Same parameter shape as sdt.breaking~ — `@crushingEnergy`, `@fragmentation`, `@granularity` — but emits a continuous stream rather than one event. Drives sdt.impact~. Use for prolonged crushing/crumpling rather than single break events.

sdt.dcmotor~.use_when: Self-contained electric motor synthesis (no interactor needed). Optional `Buffer-size:int` argument tunes internal buffer length. Tunable timbre via `@coils` (number of brush sparks per revolution), `@harshness`, `@reson` (resonance), and per-source gains: `@airGain`, `@brushGain`, `@gearGain`, `@rotorGain`, `@gearRatio`. Drive RPM via signal input. Use as a robotic / mechanical source without rolling a noise+filter chain.

sdt.demix~.use_when: Three-component source separation: splits input audio into transient, tonal, and residual signals. Optional `window-size` and `smoothing-kernel-radius` constructor args set FFT window and median-filter radius. `@tonalThreshold` and `@noiseThreshold` gate the tonal/residual decision; `@overlap` controls FFT overlap. Use as a component-aware processor — apply different effects to each component in parallel and recombine.

sdt.envelope~.digest: Envelope follower with settable attack and release times.
sdt.envelope~.use_when: Envelope follower with separately tunable `@attack` (rise time, ms) and `@release` (fall time, ms). Compare to native `average~ + rampsmooth~` (close behaviour but coarser control). Use sdt.envelope~ when you need clean attack/release control as one object — particularly when attack and release should differ significantly (e.g. fast attack, slow release for amplitude tracking).

sdt.explosion~.use_when: Supersonic explosion synthesis. Optional constructor args set internal `scatter-size` and `propagation-size` buffers. `@blastTime`, `@scatterTime` shape the explosion envelope; `@distance` simulates listener distance (delays + filtering); `@dispersion`, `@waveSpeed`, `@windSpeed` model atmospheric propagation. Send `bang` to fire. Use for film/game explosion sources — much more realistic than noise+envelope.

sdt.fluidflow~.digest: [liquids] Sound of water dripping, waterfalls, burbling streams, etc.
sdt.fluidflow~.use_when: Liquid flow synthesis — drips, waterfalls, burbling streams. Optional `polyphony:int` argument caps simultaneous bubbles. `@avgRate` controls drip rate; `@minRadius`/`@maxRadius`/`@expRadius` shape bubble size distribution; `@minDepth`/`@maxDepth`/`@expDepth` shape depth (cavity resonance) distribution; `@riseCutoff` filters the rise chirp. Use for continuous water sources where a single sdt.bubble~ wouldn't suffice.

sdt.friction~.digest: [solids, interactor] Friction interaction between two solid objects.
sdt.friction~.use_when: Friction interactor between two solid bodies. Constructor args required: `<id0> <id1> <max-outlets>` — the two body IDs to bind (matching sdt.modal/sdt.inertial Unique_IDs) and outlet count. Driving signal in left inlet provides relative velocity. Tunable contact via `@kStatic` / `@kDynamic` (friction coefficients), `@breakAway` (stick-slip threshold), `@dissipation`, `@noisiness`, `@force`. `@contact0` / `@contact1` select pickup points on each body. Use for bowed strings, scraping, stick-slip squeaks, finger-on-rim glassy tones.

sdt.impact~.digest: [solids, interactor] Impact interaction between two solid objects.
sdt.impact~.use_when: Impact interactor between two solid bodies. Constructor args required: `<id0> <id1> <max-outlets>` — the two body IDs to bind. Driving signal provides impact velocity. Tunable via `@stiffness` (hardness of contact), `@dissipation` (energy loss), `@shape` (Hertzian contact exponent), `@contact0` / `@contact1` (pickup points). Drive with bangs or velocity events from sdt.bouncing~ / sdt.breaking~ / sdt.crumpling~. Use for collisions: ball-on-plate, hammer-on-bell, stone-on-ground.

sdt.inertial.use_when: Point-mass body — non-resonating object that responds to forces. Constructor arg required: `Unique_ID:symbol`. `@mass` sets the inertial mass; `@fragmentSize` sets break-fragment scale. Drive with `strike <displacement> <velocity>` messages or via interactor coupling. Use as the 'striker' end of an interaction (e.g. the hammer in impact, the slider in friction) — for the resonating end use sdt.modal.

sdt.modal.use_when: Modal resonator — multi-mode resonating body. Constructor args: `<Unique_ID> <Modes> <Pickup-points>` (e.g. `mybell 32 2` for a 32-mode bell with 2 pickup positions). `@activeModes` caps how many modes are computed (CPU control). Define mode bank via `@freqs` (list of mode frequencies in Hz), `@decays` (per-mode decay times), `@pickup` (per-pickup mode amplitudes). `@fragmentSize` controls break-fragment behaviour. Use as the resonating object in physical models — bells, plates, bars, drums; configure mode lists per material.

sdt.motor~.use_when: Combustion engine synthesis. Optional `Buffer-size:int` constructor arg. Many tunable params: `@cycle` (2 or 4 stroke), `@cylinderSize`, `@compressionRatio`, `@asymmetry`, `@backfire`, `@expansion`, `@exhaustSize`, `@extractorSize`, plus throttle/RPM via signal input. Use for car/motorcycle/generator sources with realistic engine character.

sdt.myo~.use_when: Myoelastic vocal activity detector (voice activity detection on vocal signals). `@lowFrequency`, `@highFrequency`, `@dcFrequency` define the analysis bandpass; `@threshold` sets activity gate. Use as a gate or feature for vocal processing — e.g. trigger an effect only when voice is present, or extract vocal segments from a recording.

sdt.pitchshift~.use_when: Frequency-domain pitch shifter. Optional `window-size:int` constructor arg. `@ratio` sets pitch shift (1.0 = no shift, 2.0 = octave up); `@overlap` controls FFT overlap (higher = better quality, more CPU). Use for pitch shifting without time-stretching. Compare to fftz.pvharm~ (FFTease) — both phase-vocoder-based; sdt.pitchshift~ is self-contained while pvharm~ is part of FFTease's broader spectral toolkit.

sdt.pitch~.use_when: Fundamental-frequency (f0) estimator. Optional `window-size:int` constructor arg. `@overlap` controls FFT overlap; `@tolerance` sets confidence threshold. Outputs f0 estimate. Compare to fzero~ (good for clean monophonic), sigmund~, retune~, fluid.pitch~. Use sdt.pitch~ when already in the SDT ecosystem or when its specific algorithm fits the material.

sdt.reverb~.digest: A computationally efficient yet maximally diffusive reverb.
sdt.reverb~.use_when: Computationally efficient diffusive reverb. Optional `Maximum-length-of-delay-lines:long` constructor arg. Tune via `@time` (broadband decay), `@time1k` (decay at 1 kHz — frequency-dependent damping), `@xSize` / `@ySize` / `@zSize` (room dimensions in metres), `@randomness` (modulation depth on delay lines). Use when yafr2~ / gigaverb~ are too CPU-heavy for the mix density; sdt.reverb~ trades a bit of richness for substantial CPU savings.

sdt.rolling~.digest: [solids, control] Control algorithm to render rolling sounds.
sdt.rolling~.use_when: Control algorithm for rolling sounds (continuous rumble + surface texture). `@mass` sets the rolling object's mass; `@velocity` sets rolling speed; `@depth` sets surface penetration; `@grain` sets surface roughness. Drives sdt.impact~ in continuous mode. Use for ball-rolling, can-rolling, log-rolling textures.

sdt.scraping~.digest: [solids, control] Control algorithm to render scraping/sliding/rubbing sound textures.
sdt.scraping~.use_when: Control algorithm for scraping/sliding/rubbing textures. `@force` sets contact pressure; `@velocity` sets sliding speed; `@grain` sets surface roughness. Drives sdt.friction~ to render the actual scraping sound. Use for sandpaper-on-wood, knife-on-stone, brake-pad-style textures.

sdt.spectralfeats~.digest: Spectral analyzer extracting several audio descriptors.
sdt.spectralfeats~.use_when: Bundled multi-descriptor spectral analyser — outputs centroid, spread, skewness, kurtosis, flatness, etc. all in one object. Optional `window-size:int` constructor arg. `@minFreq` / `@maxFreq` define the analysis band; `@overlap` controls FFT overlap. Use when you want several spectral features at once cheaper than chaining individual zsa.* objects (which each run their own FFT).

sdt.windcavity~.digest: [gases] Sound of airflows passing through cavities, such as tunnels, pipes, valves, etc.
sdt.windcavity~.use_when: Wind through a cavity model — tunnels, pipes, valves. Optional `Buffer-length:long` constructor arg. `@length` sets cavity length (drives resonance pitch); `@diameter` sets bore (drives timbre). Drive turbulence via signal input. Use for whistling pipes, organ-flue tones, howling chimneys.

sdt.windflow~.use_when: Generic wind sound (broadband filtered noise with realistic gust modulation). No tunable parameters beyond signal-driven turbulence input. Use as a general wind backdrop; for whistling/howling variants use sdt.windkarman~ or sdt.windcavity~.

sdt.windkarman~.use_when: Karman-vortex wind — howling/whistling wind around an obstacle. `@diameter` sets obstacle diameter (drives whistle pitch). Drive turbulence via signal input. Use for high-wind dramatic effects, whistling-around-corners textures.

sdt.zerox~.use_when: Zero-crossing detector and counter. Optional `Analysis-window-length:int` constructor arg. `@overlap` controls window overlap. Outputs zero-crossing rate per analysis window. Use as a cheap monophonic pitch estimator, transient detector, or noise/tone classifier (high zerox rate = noise-like, low = tonal) — much cheaper than full spectral analysis when one descriptor suffices.

jit.gl.syphonclient.use_when: Receive a Syphon texture stream from another macOS application. Drop into a Jitter GL chain to consume textures published by any Syphon-enabled app. `@servername` selects which publisher to read; supports alpha. Use for cross-app live video routing without round-tripping through pixel buffers.

jit.gl.syphonserver.use_when: Publish a Jitter GL texture as a Syphon stream consumable by other apps. Drop into a Jitter GL chain to make its output available systemwide. `@servername` sets the published name.

xray.jit.colormap.digest: xray.jit.colormap takes a standard video matrix and generates a colormap for use with jit.charmap for coloring grayscale images. In mode 0, all of the values of the incoming matrix a binned according to luminance accumulatively and then averaged to derive the colormap. In mode 1, only the first color value of a particular luminance is binned. In both modes, if there isn't a particular color value associated with a luminance bin, the map value is interpolated from the nearest existing values.
xray.jit.colormap.use_when: xray.jit.colormap takes a standard video matrix and generates a colormap for use with jit.charmap for coloring grayscale images. In mode 0, all of the values of the incoming matrix a binned according to luminance accumulatively and then averaged to derive the colormap. In mode 1, only the first color value of a particular luminance is binned. In both modes, if there isn't a particular color value associated with a luminance bin, the map value is interpolated from the nearest existing values. XRAY Jitter matrix/math utility (Andrew Benson). Operates on Jitter matrices.
