xorlist.use_when: calculate the accumulated xor vales of a list from one member to the next - minimum length 2 elements, ints only. jasch utility object — geometric/algorithmic helper.

jk.lpx.digest: Lightshow randomizes the lights on your Launchpad X in different ways.
jk.lpx.use_when: Launchpad X randomized lightshow generator. Sends pad-color sequences to a connected Launchpad X for visual displays — useful for performance ambience or visual feedback. Pair with jk.lpxcore for low-level control.

jk.lpxcore.digest: Lightshow randomizes the lights on your Launchpad X in different ways.
jk.lpxcore.use_when: Lower-level Launchpad X interface — direct pad/color control. Use as the building block for custom Launchpad lightshows; jk.lpx is the higher-level random-pattern wrapper.

karma~.use_when: Varispeed audio looper — record/loop/overdub with continuous speed control. The signature `karma~` object: live-buffered overdubbing with full speed/direction control, multi-channel, sample-accurate looping. Inlets: signal in, sync trigger, speed, append/overdub flags. Use whenever you need a looper richer than the native `groove~`+`buffer~` pattern.

link.beat.digest: Synchronize beats with a Link timeline
link.beat.use_when: Synchronize beats with a Link timeline.

link.phasor~.digest: Synchronize phase with a Link timeline
link.phasor~.use_when: Synchronize phase with a Link timeline.

link.session.digest: Start/stop a session, change the tempo, or be notified about these events
link.session.use_when: Start/stop a session, change the tempo, or be notified about these events.

jit.ml.lzw.digest: Lemper-Ziv-Welch string based compression for Jitter matrices.
jit.ml.lzw.use_when: Lemper-Ziv-Welch string based compression for Jitter matrices.

ml.art.digest: Fuzzy Adaptive Resonance Theory Neural Network
ml.art.use_when: Fuzzy Adaptive Resonance Theory Neural Network.

ml.kdtree.digest: k-d tree: organize "k" dimensioned data into a binary-tree for improved, and nearest neighbor searching.
ml.kdtree.use_when: k-d tree: organize "k" dimensioned data into a binary-tree for improved, and nearest neighbor searching.

ml.scalar.digest: Uses distribution of elements to scale lists to -1./1
ml.scalar.use_when: Uses distribution of elements to scale lists to -1./1.

ml.scale.digest: Uses distribution of elements to scale lists to -1./1
ml.scale.use_when: Uses distribution of elements to scale lists to -1./1.

ml.vmm.bin.digest: Binary CTW Variable order Markov Model Predictor
ml.vmm.bin.use_when: Binary CTW Variable order Markov Model Predictor.

ml.vmm.ppm.digest: Prediction by Partial Matchh Markov Model
ml.vmm.ppm.use_when: Prediction by Partial Matchh Markov Model.

mo.adc~.digest: Output audio signals from hardware input devices, and control gain.
mo.adc~.use_when: Multi-channel audio-input abstraction with built-in gain control. Wraps native adc~ with voice-aware gain UI. Use as a hardware-input stage when you want a uniform mo.* look across an installation patch.

mo.arrow.digest: Use keyboard's arrows to trigger events.
mo.arrow.use_when: Trigger events with the keyboard arrow keys. Outputs per-direction triggers. Use as a no-MIDI keyboard control surface for navigation, transport, or selection in performance patches.

mo.average.digest: Adjust mode (normal, absolute) and sensitivity to input data.
mo.average.use_when: Running-average smoother with `mode` (normal vs absolute) and adjustable sensitivity. Use to smooth control-rate streams (joystick, sensor data) — `absolute` mode is useful for unipolar averaging of bipolar input.

mo.calibrate.digest: Calibrate minimum and maximum input over an adjustable time window (s).
mo.calibrate.use_when: Auto-calibrate min/max input over an adjustable time window (seconds). Use for auto-ranging incoming sensor data — start the window, wave the sensor through its full range, get a normalised output afterward.

mo.click.use_when: Multi-mode click behaviour — single, double, hold, etc. Use to expand mouse-click input into multiple events (different actions for different click types) without writing the timing logic.

mo.cpu.digest: Keep track of your CPU utilization.
mo.cpu.use_when: CPU-utilisation tracker. `rate` selects polling interval (0=100ms, 1=500, 2=1000, 3=2000, 4=5000ms). Use to display Max's own CPU load in a performance UI.

mo.crosspatch.use_when: Visual cable-patching UI for routing a list of data inputs to outputs. Click a cable + Backspace to delete. Use for performer-friendly visual routing — drag cables to reroute control streams during performance.

mo.crosspatch~.digest: A multichannel audio signal routing object.
mo.crosspatch~.use_when: Audio version of mo.crosspatch — multichannel signal routing via visual cables. Click + Backspace to delete a connection. Use for ad-hoc multichannel audio routing UI in performance patches.

mo.ctlout.digest: Send continuous MIDI control change values to a MIDI device.
mo.ctlout.use_when: Single-voice MIDI continuous-controller output. Wraps native ctlout with mo.* UI. Use as the standard MIDI CC output in modulo patches.

mo.ctlouts.digest: Send continuous control change values to a MIDI device.
mo.ctlouts.use_when: Multi-voice MIDI CC output — sends a list of CC values to multiple controllers in one object. Use when you need to output many CCs from a list in one place rather than N parallel ctlouts.

mo.cue.digest: Set numbered cues and trigger events.
mo.cue.use_when: Numbered cue trigger system — set cues, recall by number to fire events. Use as a cueing engine for theatrical or installation patches where multiple events need to be recalled by index.

mo.dac~.digest: Send multichannel audio signals to hardware devices.
mo.dac~.use_when: Multi-channel audio-output abstraction with voice-aware gain. Use as the standard hardware-output stage in mo.* patches.

mo.date.digest: Keep track of today's time and date.
mo.date.use_when: Output today's date and time. Use as a clock source for time-stamped logs, scheduled events, or display in installation UIs.

mo.debounce.digest: Useful to filter out jittery values like buttons coming from Arduino.
mo.debounce.use_when: Debounce filter — suppresses rapid value changes shorter than a configurable interval (try 500 ms). Filters jitter from buttons (Arduino, MIDI controllers, mechanical switches). Use whenever a digital sensor produces noisy edges that fire native edge detectors multiple times per real event.

mo.dial.digest: Map this object to a MIDI controller.
mo.dial.use_when: Multi-voice rotary dial array — map to MIDI controller for instant tactile control. Use as a uniform dial UI when you have a knob-style controller and want to drive multiple parameters from one object.

mo.encoder.digest: Map this object to a MIDI controller. Can be controlled by any value (standard is 63-65).
mo.encoder.use_when: Multi-voice incremental encoder UI. Default uses 63/65 increment values (standard relative-encoder convention) but configurable. Map to a MIDI encoder controller. Use for endless-rotation encoder support without abs-position assumptions.

mo.fm~.use_when: FM synthesizer abstraction. Drive with a list of normalised values (0–1) or specific messages. Parameters: carrier, harmonicity, modulation index, etc. Use as a self-contained FM voice — quicker than rolling cycle~ + cycle~ + arithmetic per voice.

mo.gain~.digest: Hold SHIFT+Drag to control all channels simultaneusly.
mo.gain~.use_when: Multi-channel gain UI. SHIFT+drag controls all channels simultaneously. Use as the standard gain stage in mo.* signal chains.

mo.ggate.digest: The number of voices divides the right value equally.
mo.ggate.use_when: Multi-voice gate — divides a value range equally between N voices. Use for distributing a single control value across multiple gated channels (e.g. one CC controls panning across N speakers).

mo.granulator~.digest: A eight-phase shifted stereo granular synthesizer.
mo.granulator~.use_when: Eight-phase shifted stereo granular synthesizer. Drive with normalised list (0–1) or specific messages: (playstop), (mode), playback rate, grain size, etc. Use as a self-contained stereo granular voice without rolling phasor~ + tapout~ chains.

mo.grid.use_when: Step-grid sequencer with constraint and direction controls. Set step count, value constraints, and direction (forward/backward/random). Use for grid-based sequencing where step values fill a 2D pattern.

mo.gridmeter~.digest: Display multichannel signals level as brightness.
mo.gridmeter~.use_when: Multichannel signal-level grid display — channels as brightness-modulated cells. Click to mute/unmute per channel. Use for compact level monitoring of large channel counts (16+).

mo.hi.digest: Provides input from human-interface device interfaces (Trackpad, Gamepad, etc.)
mo.hi.use_when: Generic Human-Interface Device input — trackpads, gamepads, etc. Wraps native hi with voice-aware UI. Use for HID device input without configuring native hi manually.

mo.hrslider.use_when: Horizontal range slider (low-high pair). Constructor args set the (0., 1.) min/max range. Use to define a numerical range graphically (e.g. min/max for randomisation).

mo.hslider.digest: Map this object to a MIDI controller.
mo.hslider.use_when: Horizontal slider — map to MIDI controller. Use as the standard horizontal-slider UI in mo.* patches.

mo.htab.digest: Set the number of tabs and each item's name.
mo.htab.use_when: Horizontal tab UI with named items. Set tab count and item names. Use as a discrete-state selector with friendly labels (cleaner than radiogroup for named states).

mo.if.digest: Use conditional statements to process input data.
mo.if.use_when: Conditional comparison object. `condition` selects operator (1==, 2!=, 3>, 4>=, 5<, 6<=). Use for cleaner inline conditionals than native if statement object — UI exposes the operator choice.

mo.joystick.digest: Output data when passing a certain higher and lower threshold.
mo.joystick.use_when: Joystick input with per-axis dual-threshold output (high and low). Set origin and sensitivity. Use for joystick-driven event triggering — fires events when an axis crosses a threshold rather than continuously streaming.

mo.key.digest: Use spacebar, esc and return keys to trigger events.
mo.key.use_when: Keyboard event trigger. `key` ASCII code (32=space default, 27=esc, 13=return). Use for keyboard-shortcut event triggers without rolling key + select chains.

mo.keyboard.digest: Output numbers from an onscreen keyboard.
mo.keyboard.use_when: On-screen keyboard with mode select: 1=monophonic, 2=polyphonic, 3=touchscreen. Use as a built-in keyboard UI for note input in performance patches.

mo.latch.digest: Output greater/lower value from a list of data.
mo.latch.use_when: Output the greater or lower value from a list. `mode` selects greater vs lower. Use for max/min hold operations on a value stream.

mo.launch.digest: Input, or drag and drop anything. Follow with a bang to launch.
mo.launch.use_when: Drag-and-drop launcher. Drag any file/folder into the UI; click to search disk; bang to launch. Use as a simple file-picker / launcher in installation UIs.

mo.led.use_when: Multi-state LED indicator. `outline mode` toggles active/inactive boundary look. Use for compact multi-state display in performance UIs.

mo.lfo~.digest: Output audio signal from the left outlet, data from the right.
mo.lfo~.use_when: LFO with both signal and data outputs. Configurable jitter, smoothing, offset, and depth. Left outlet = signal, right = control data. Use as a one-stop LFO when you need both signal-rate (for audio modulation) and event-rate (for parameter modulation) streams.

mo.line.digest: Ramp a list of data within a specific amount of time.
mo.line.use_when: Ramp a list of values over time — list-domain line~. Useful for filtering and smoothing parameter streams. Use to crossfade multi-channel parameters synchronously.

mo.makenote.use_when: Multi-voice makenote with optional probability per note. Inputs: pitch, velocity, duration, probability. Use for generative MIDI sequencing where some notes should be probabilistically suppressed.

mo.map.use_when: Universal MIDI/keyboard parameter mapper. Use a controller or computer keyboard to map any parameters in the patch; ESC to exit mapping mode. Use for runtime-configurable controller mapping without writing a separate map-saving system.

mo.matrix.use_when: Click-to-enable matrix routing for list data. Allows multi-input/multi-output connections; auto-loads on patch load. Use for fixed routing matrices (vs mo.crosspatch's cable-style UI) in installation patches.

mo.matrix~.digest: A multichannel audio signal matrix object.
mo.matrix~.use_when: Multichannel audio matrix routing — click cells to enable/disable connections. Use for fixed multichannel audio routing matrices.

mo.meter~.digest: The module will adapt to the number of signals.
mo.meter~.use_when: Multichannel meter that auto-adapts to incoming signal count. Use as a uniform meter object across channel counts without specifying voice count manually.

mo.midiin.digest: Choose your MIDI device from the pup-up menu.
mo.midiin.use_when: MIDI input from a chosen device. `high resolution mode` 1=Off (0–127), 2=Float (-1 to 1), 3=14-bit Fixed (-8192 to 8191). Use for general-purpose MIDI input with mode-controlled output range.

mo.midiout.digest: Choose your MIDI device from the pup-up menu.
mo.midiout.use_when: MIDI output to a chosen device. Same `high resolution mode` options as mo.midiin. Use as the universal MIDI-output companion to mo.midiin.

mo.minmax.digest: Calibrate minimum and maximum looking at highest and lowest values.
mo.minmax.use_when: Adaptive min/max calibration — tracks highest and lowest seen values continuously. Use for auto-ranging that updates throughout a session (vs mo.calibrate's fixed-window approach).

mo.monitor.use_when: Message-stream monitor — keeps a running display of incoming messages. Use for inline patch debugging without sending to the Max console.

mo.mouse.digest: set the 0,0 point from which to measure the mouse location
mo.mouse.use_when: Mouse-position tracker with configurable origin. `0,0 point` settable per-instance; reset to default. Use to track mouse offset from an arbitrary origin (e.g. distance from a fixed UI point).

mo.nodes.use_when: Graphical node-based interpolator — interpolate data by drawing weighted nodes. Use for visual control of multi-parameter blends (similar to native nodes but with mo.* UI consistency).

mo.noteout.digest: Choose the output MIDI port from the pup-up menu.
mo.noteout.use_when: Single-voice MIDI note output to a chosen port. Use as the standard MIDI note output in mo.* patches.

mo.noteouts.digest: Send a list of MIDI note messages to a specified port.
mo.noteouts.use_when: Multi-voice MIDI note output — accepts a list of notes and sends each. Use when sending chords or multi-voice MIDI from a single object.

mo.notepad.digest: Text is stored in a <dict> object.
mo.notepad.use_when: Text-storage UI; data stored in a `dict` object. `;toggle 1` + Return saves text. Use for in-patch text storage (notes, presets-as-text) without external file I/O.

mo.number.digest: Numbers can be int, float, or MIDI.
mo.number.use_when: Single-value number display — int, float, or MIDI mode. Use as the standard mo.* number-display widget.

mo.numbers.digest: Display and output a list of numbers.
mo.numbers.use_when: Multi-voice number display — auto-adapts to list length. Use for displaying parallel value streams without specifying voice count.

mo.pad.digest: Map this object to a MIDI controller.
mo.pad.use_when: Multi-voice pad UI — map to MIDI pads. Use for pad-controller-style trigger UIs.

mo.pads.digest: Set the mode for each pad (trigger or bang).
mo.pads.use_when: Multi-pad with per-pad mode (trigger or bang). Use when different pads in a grid should fire different message types.

mo.pipe.digest: Delay time will be restored to 0 once output is done.
mo.pipe.use_when: Delay with auto-reset of delay time after each output (returns to 0). Use for one-shot delayed events where the delay should not persist on subsequent inputs.

mo.playlist~.digest: Playback, loop and select section of sound files.
mo.playlist~.use_when: Soundfile playback with looping and section selection. Play/stop by index (0 stops). Use as a mo.* playlist~ wrapper with consistent UI.

mo.poll.digest: Output a list of data at a specific rate (in ms).
mo.poll.use_when: List-data resampler — output at a specific rate (ms). Useful for resampling an incoming list stream. Use to throttle a rapid value stream to a fixed rate (vs native speedlim's max-rate behaviour).

mo.prepend.digest: A "target" message followed by the voice number and message can be used.
mo.prepend.use_when: List prepend with multi-voice routing. `target <voice>` followed by message addresses one voice. Use for voice-addressed message construction.

mo.preset.digest: The module is connected to a pattrstorage and preset object.
mo.preset.use_when: Preset UI connected to pattrstorage and preset object. Use as the mo.* drop-in for a uniform preset-management UI.

mo.rand.digest: set step size (size of random steps)
mo.rand.use_when: Random walker with `step size` configurable. Use for bounded random walks (drunk-style) with explicit step control.

mo.receive.digest: Grab from a send or forward object sharing the same device name.
mo.receive.use_when: Cordless receive — paired with mo.send sharing the same device name. Use for clean inter-patcher connections without cluttering with patchcords.

mo.receives.digest: Receive a list of message from specified receive objects without patching cords.
mo.receives.use_when: Multi-stream receive — list of named receives in one object. Type `value-1`, `value-2`, etc. to set names. Use to consolidate many cordless receives into one object.

mo.receive~.digest: Grab audio signals from an mc.send~ object sharing the same device name.
mo.receive~.use_when: Audio receive companion to mc.send~ sharing the same device name. Use for cordless multichannel audio routing.

mo.record.use_when: Data recorder/playback with looping. Use for recording control streams to replay later — e.g. capture a gesture, replay it as automation.

mo.record~.use_when: Multichannel audio recorder. Specify length in seconds upfront; recording is cropped if shorter. Use for fixed-length multichannel capture without manual buffer~ management.

mo.rms~.use_when: Multichannel RMS computation. Use for level analysis on multichannel signals (DSP must restart on channel-count changes).

mo.route.digest: A "target" message followed by the voice number and message can be used.
mo.route.use_when: Multi-voice route. `target <voice>` addresses one voice. Use for voice-aware message routing.

mo.rsliders.digest: Set a minimum and a maximum range for each rslider (a list of two numbers).
mo.rsliders.use_when: Multi-voice range sliders — each slider is a (min, max) pair. Use to set per-voice ranges in a single multi-slider UI.

mo.scale.digest: Values can be changed globally, or individually for each voice.
mo.scale.use_when: Multi-voice scale (range remap). Values can be set globally or per-voice. Use as the multi-voice equivalent of native scale.

mo.schmitt.digest: Threshold can be set globally, or individually for each voice.
mo.schmitt.use_when: Schmitt trigger (hysteresis) — threshold settable globally or per-voice. Use for de-bounced threshold detection where you need different on/off thresholds.

mo.scope.digest: Output data can either be unipolar (0. - 1.) or bipolar (-1. - 1.).
mo.scope.use_when: Data oscilloscope — display data over time. Output unipolar (0–1) or bipolar (-1 to 1). Use for visualising control streams the same way scope~ visualises audio.

mo.scope~.digest: Display multichannel signals level through an oscilloscope.
mo.scope~.use_when: Multichannel signal oscilloscope. Auto-adapts to signal count. Use for multichannel audio visualisation in mo.* patches.

mo.select.digest: It matches floating points using "fuzzy" mode.
mo.select.use_when: Select with fuzzy float matching — matches floats within a tolerance rather than requiring exact equality. Use when native select fails on float comparisons due to precision.

mo.send.digest: Send to a receive object sharing the same device name.
mo.send.use_when: Cordless send — paired with mo.receive on the same device name. Use as the send half of mo.* cordless connections.

mo.sends.digest: The upper message box, if set, will target receive objects sharing the same name.
mo.sends.use_when: Multi-stream send — list of named sends; the upper message box can target receives sharing the name. Use as the multi-target counterpart of mo.send.

mo.send~.digest: Send multichannel audio signals without patch cords.
mo.send~.use_when: Multichannel audio send to mc.receive~ sharing the device name. Note: DSP restarts on channel-count changes. Use for cordless multichannel audio routing.

mo.serial.digest: Provides input and output through serial communication (Arduino, Teensy, etc.)
mo.serial.use_when: Serial communication for Arduino, Teensy, etc. Configurable blink/poll metro. Use as the mo.* wrapper around serial with uniform UI.

mo.shake.digest: Adjust mode (normal, absolute) and sensitivity to input data.
mo.shake.use_when: Shake/movement detector with `mode` (normal/absolute) and sensitivity. Use to detect motion events from accelerometer-style sensor data.

mo.sig~.digest: If the number of channels change, the DSP must be restarted.
mo.sig~.use_when: Multi-channel sig~ wrapper. DSP must restart on channel-count changes. Use as the mo.* signal source.

mo.slide.digest: Set up and down slide motion, and poll data if necessary.
mo.slide.use_when: Slide smoother with separate up/down ramp times and optional polling. Use as a configurable slide where attack and release should differ — wraps native slide with mo.* UI.

mo.sliders.digest: Output data can either be unipolar (0. - 1.) or bipolar (-1. - 1.).
mo.sliders.use_when: Multi-voice slider array. Output unipolar (0–1) or bipolar (-1 to 1). Use as the standard multi-slider UI in mo.* patches.

mo.smooth.digest: Smooth a list of values through a leaky integrator.
mo.smooth.use_when: Multi-voice leaky-integrator smoother. Up/down ramp in ms, optional polling, auto-adapts to voice count. Use for smoothing parallel parameter streams.

mo.snapshot~.digest: If the number of channels change, the DSP must be restarted.
mo.snapshot~.use_when: Multichannel snapshot~ with channel-count-restart caveat. Use as the mo.* snapshot abstraction.

mo.spectroscope~.digest: Display multichannel signals level through an spectroscope or sonogram.
mo.spectroscope~.use_when: Multichannel spectroscope/sonogram display. Use for multichannel spectral monitoring in mo.* patches.

mo.speedlim.digest: Limit the speed of a list of values throughput.
mo.speedlim.use_when: Throughput-limit on a list of values. Use as the multi-voice speedlim — limits update rate per-voice.

mo.split.digest: Output numbers within, below and above range.
mo.split.use_when: Split a number into within-range, below-range, and above-range outputs. Use as a 3-way split for value-band analysis.

mo.steer.digest: If both signals are equal to 0, the output will be 0.5.
mo.steer.use_when: Direction-biased two-input combiner. If both signals = 0, output = 0.5 (centred). Use for joystick-style steering math (combine two control axes into a normalised position).

mo.step.digest: Set steps composed of pitch, velocity, and duration (plus two additional steps).
mo.step.use_when: Step sequencer with per-step pitch, velocity, duration, plus two extra parameters. Use as a generative step-sequencer block.

mo.swatch.use_when: Colour-picker UI — control by hue/saturation/lightness. Send list (1st inlet) or message (2nd). Use for colour-control in jit.gl / video output patches.

mo.tabs.digest: Set the number of tabs and each item's name.
mo.tabs.use_when: Tab-bar UI — set tab count and per-item names. Use as a horizontal tab control with named tabs.

mo.theremin~.use_when: Theremin synth abstraction. Drive with normalised list or messages. Parameters: pitch, volume, timbre. Use as a self-contained theremin voice.

mo.transport.digest: Keep track of the Global Transport.
mo.transport.use_when: Global Transport tracker. Use to expose Max's global transport state to in-patch logic.

mo.udpreceive.use_when: Network UDP receive. Use as the mo.* wrapper around udpreceive with consistent UI.

mo.udpsend.use_when: Network UDP send. Default host=localhost, port=7400. Use as the mo.* wrapper around udpsend.

mo.vector.digest: Combine a list of control signals into speherical coordinates (vector sum).
mo.vector.use_when: Combine a list of control values into spherical / vector-sum coordinates. Outputs combined xy axes plus per-node values. Use for vector-summing N inputs into a single 2D position (e.g. multi-fader → trajectory point).

mo.vrslider.use_when: Vertical range slider (low-high pair). Range default (0., 1.). Use as the vertical complement to mo.hrslider.

mo.vslider.digest: Map this object to a MIDI controller.
mo.vslider.use_when: Vertical slider — map to MIDI controller. Use as the standard vertical slider in mo.* patches.

mo.vtab.digest: Set the number of tabs and each item's name.
mo.vtab.use_when: Vertical tab UI with named items. Use for vertical tabs alongside mo.htab's horizontal layout.

mo.xbendout.digest: Send continuous control change values to a MIDI device.
mo.xbendout.use_when: MIDI pitch-bend output (continuous controller). Use as the mo.* wrapper for high-resolution pitch bend.

mo.xbendouts.digest: Send a list of extra precision MIDI pitch bend messages.
mo.xbendouts.use_when: Multi-voice extra-precision MIDI pitch-bend output. Use for sending multiple high-resolution bends in one object (e.g. MPE-style per-voice bends).

mo.xypad.use_when: Two-dimensional control surface. Use as a 2D control input for parameter pairs (filter cutoff/resonance, panning, etc.).

o.append.digest: Append an OSC address to every OSC address in an odot bundle
o.append.use_when: Append an OSC address to every OSC address in an odot bundle. odot OSC-bundle operator — see package_concepts.md odot section.

o.change.digest: Output a bundle if it changes
o.change.use_when: Output a bundle if it changes. odot OSC-bundle operator — see package_concepts.md odot section.

o.collect.digest: Collect OSC messages to be bundled together
o.collect.use_when: Collect OSC messages to be bundled together. odot OSC-bundle operator — see package_concepts.md odot section.

o.compose.use_when: Create OSC bundles from text. odot OSC-bundle operator — see package_concepts.md odot section.

o.cond.digest: Route an odot bundle out an outlet based on the results of an expression
o.cond.use_when: Route an odot bundle out an outlet based on the results of an expression. odot OSC-bundle operator — see package_concepts.md odot section.

o.dict.digest: Convert an odot bundle to a Max dictionary
o.dict.use_when: Convert an odot bundle to a Max dictionary. odot OSC-bundle operator — see package_concepts.md odot section.

o.difference.digest: Output a bundle containing the difference between two bundles
o.difference.use_when: Output a bundle containing the difference between two bundles. odot OSC-bundle operator — see package_concepts.md odot section.

o.display.use_when: Display incoming odot bundles. odot OSC-bundle operator — see package_concepts.md odot section.

o.edge~.use_when: Report transitions between 0 and non-zero. odot OSC-bundle operator — see package_concepts.md odot section.

o.explode.digest: Explode address hierarchies into nested bundles
o.explode.use_when: Explode address hierarchies into nested bundles. odot OSC-bundle operator — see package_concepts.md odot section.

o.expr.codebox.digest: Evaluate C-like expressions containing OSC addresses
o.expr.codebox.use_when: Evaluate C-like expressions containing OSC addresses. odot OSC-bundle operator — see package_concepts.md odot section.

o.flatten.digest: Flatten the address hierarchy in nested bundles
o.flatten.use_when: Flatten the address hierarchy in nested bundles. odot OSC-bundle operator — see package_concepts.md odot section.

o.gui.attach.digest: Attaches to GUI objects with OSC varnames
o.gui.attach.use_when: Attaches to GUI objects with OSC varnames. odot OSC-bundle operator — see package_concepts.md odot section.

o.if.digest: Route an OSC packet based on the results of an expression
o.if.use_when: Conditional dispatch on OSC bundle contents. Routes a bundle to one of two outlets based on an expression.

o.intersection.digest: Output a bundle containing the intersection of messages between two bundles
o.intersection.use_when: Output a bundle containing the intersection of messages between two bundles. odot OSC-bundle operator — see package_concepts.md odot section.

o.listenumerate.digest: Iterate over a list at a user-defined OSC address
o.listenumerate.use_when: Iterate over a list at a user-defined OSC address. odot OSC-bundle operator — see package_concepts.md odot section.

o.pack.use_when: Build an OSC bundle from individual address/value pairs.

o.prepend.digest: Prepend an OSC address to every OSC address in a bundle
o.prepend.use_when: Prepend an OSC address to every OSC address in a bundle. odot OSC-bundle operator — see package_concepts.md odot section.

o.print.digest: Print an OSC packet to the Max window
o.print.use_when: Print an OSC bundle to the Max console — debug aid.

o.printbytes.digest: Print an OSC packet to the Max window as a sequence of bytes
o.printbytes.use_when: Print an OSC packet to the Max window as a sequence of bytes. odot OSC-bundle operator — see package_concepts.md odot section.

o.route.digest: Dispatch OSC messages according to an address hierarchy stripping off the portion of the address that matched
o.route.use_when: Address-based bundle routing. Modern replacement for the deprecated CNMAT `osc-route`.

o.schedule.digest: Deadline Scheduler for odot bundles using odot timetags
o.schedule.use_when: Deadline Scheduler for odot bundles using odot timetags. odot OSC-bundle operator — see package_concepts.md odot section.

o.select.digest: Dispatch OSC messages according to an address hierarchy preserving the address
o.select.use_when: Dispatch OSC messages according to an address hierarchy preserving the address. odot OSC-bundle operator — see package_concepts.md odot section.

o.timetag.digest: Bind a timetag to an address
o.timetag.use_when: Bind a timetag to an address. odot OSC-bundle operator — see package_concepts.md odot section.

o.union.digest: Output a bundle containing the union of all messages between two bundles.
o.union.use_when: Output a bundle containing the union of all messages between two bundles. odot OSC-bundle operator — see package_concepts.md odot section.

o.var.digest: Store a bundle and bang it out later
o.var.use_when: Store a bundle and bang it out later. odot OSC-bundle operator — see package_concepts.md odot section.

vb.mi.mu~.digest: µ-law companding for degrading audio signals

voice-alligator-audio~.digest: audio-to-audio voice allocator with built-in ADSR and glide

zero.announce.digest: Publish an available service using ZeroConf
zero.announce.use_when: Publish an available service using ZeroConf.

zero.browse.digest: Browse available services published using ZeroConf
zero.browse.use_when: Browse available services published using ZeroConf.

zero.resolve.digest: Resolve a ZeroConf service name to it's IP address and port
zero.resolve.use_when: Resolve a ZeroConf service name to it's IP address and port.

zsa.ampstats~.digest: Report min/mean/max/sum of a signal vector or a FFT frame.
zsa.ampstats~.use_when: Per-vector amplitude statistics on a signal vector or single FFT frame: outputs min, mean, max, sum on four separate outlets. Use for vector-level summaries when continuous running stats (avg~, peakamp~) aren't granular enough — e.g. one stat per FFT frame instead of one rolling value. No list output: each statistic is a discrete float on its own outlet.

zsa.bark~.digest: Calculates and outputs Bark coefficients as a list.
zsa.bark~.use_when: Bark-band spectral coefficients from an FFT spectrum. Wire FFT spectrum into left inlet, FFT size/metadata into right inlet, list of Bark coefficients out. `@automatic 1` (default) emits a list every analysis frame; set `@automatic 0` and send `bang` to poll on demand. Use when perceptual bands (Bark scale) matter — masking-aware analysis, psychoacoustic processing.

zsa.centroid~.digest: Spectral centroid is the barycentre of the spectrum.
zsa.centroid~.use_when: Spectral centroid (perceptual brightness as the spectrum's barycentre). Signal in (FFT spectrum), single float out per frame. No attributes — minimal API. Use as a one-number timbre descriptor for analysis-driven control. Native equivalent: FFT + bin-by-bin weighted-magnitude sum, then divide by total magnitude — substantial chain reduced to one object.

zsa.dct.digest: Calculates and outputs Discrete Cosine Transform
zsa.dct.use_when: Discrete cosine transform of a list (e.g. mel-band coefficients → MFCC). Send `list <coeffs>` to compute DCT. `@fbe` flag toggles 'frequency band energy' input mode. Use as the second stage of an MFCC pipeline (zsa.mel~ → zsa.dct → MFCCs) or any application needing list-domain DCT without dropping into JS.

zsa.decrease.digest: Decrease reports the amount of spectral magnitude decreasing.
zsa.decrease.use_when: Spectral magnitude decrease (rate of magnitude falloff above the peak), list-rate. Send `list <values>` to evaluate. Use for offline / per-event timbral analysis when you have spectral magnitudes already as a list. For continuous signal-rate operation use zsa.decrease~.

zsa.decrease~.digest: Decrease reports the amount of spectral magnitude decreasing.
zsa.decrease~.use_when: Signal-rate version of zsa.decrease — per-frame spectral falloff rate from FFT input. `@binmin` sets the lowest bin to consider (skip DC/low rumble). Use as a continuous timbre descriptor for analysis-driven synthesis where 'how steeply does the spectrum decay' matters.

zsa.dist.use_when: Find the closest list (Euclidean / Minkowsky distance) within a stored set of named lists. Build the corpus by sending `<key> <list>` (or `anything <list>`); query by sending a list. Outputs the matched key (left), distance (middle), and full distance ranking depending on `@outputmode`. Key attrs: `@mode` (distance type), `@order` (Minkowsky order, default 2 = Euclidean), `@useweights` (use weighted dimensions, set via `weights <list>`), `@output_distances` (emit distances on second outlet), `@append` (incrementally add vs replace), `@quiet` (suppress prints). Persist with `read`/`write filename`. Use for lightweight nearest-neighbour matching without a full ML toolkit; FluCoMa's fluid.kdtree~ scales better for large corpora.

zsa.energy~.digest: Calculates the energy of each Frequency Bin (r * r + i * i).
zsa.energy~.use_when: Per-bin energy (r² + i²) from an FFT spectrum. Use as the canonical squared-magnitude calculation: drop-in replacement for FFT magnitude code where you want energy directly (e.g. before flux, flatness, or rolloff calculations). No attributes — just signal in, signal out.

zsa.flatness~.digest: Spectral flatness of key frequency bands.
zsa.flatness~.use_when: Spectral flatness (geometric / arithmetic mean ratio of bin magnitudes) per frequency band. `@freq` accepts a 32-element list of band edges to compute flatness per band rather than overall. Use to distinguish noise-like (flatness near 1) from tonal (near 0) material — a single number per band per frame.

zsa.flux~.digest: Calculates spectral flux (difference of energy between frames).
zsa.flux~.use_when: Spectral flux (energy difference between consecutive frames). `@frame` sets how many frames back to compare (default 1 = adjacent frames); `@mode` selects flux variant (L1, L2, etc.). Use for onset detection, motion / change descriptors, or any 'how much is the spectrum moving' control source.

zsa.freqpeak~.digest: Extract a list of pairs Frequency / Amplitude peaks by FFT frame.
zsa.freqpeak~.use_when: Per-frame list of (frequency, amplitude) peak pairs from an FFT spectrum. `@peaks` sets max number of peaks to return; `@threshold` is the noise floor below which peaks are ignored; `@automatic 1` emits a list every frame, `@automatic 0` waits for `bang` polling; `@autolength` adapts the output list length to peak count rather than padding. Use for partial tracking, sinusoidal modelling, or feeding zsa.fund / zsa.virtfund downstream.

zsa.fund.digest: Estimate fundamental frequency of a list of Frequency / Amplitude Pairs.
zsa.fund.use_when: Estimate fundamental frequency from a (freq, amp) peak list (typically the output of zsa.freqpeak~). Send `list <peaks>` to evaluate. Constrain the search range with `@freqmin` (default 100 Hz) and `@freqmax` (default 22050 Hz); `@grid` sets quantisation grid (default 1 Hz). Use as the second stage of a peak-tracking f0 estimator. Compare to fzero~ (signal-rate, less control) — zsa.fund operates on list-domain peak data so you choose the analysis frame.

zsa.kurtosis~.use_when: Spectral kurtosis (peakedness — fourth statistical moment of the magnitude distribution). `@threshold` excludes bins below noise floor. Use as a timbral descriptor — high kurtosis = single sharp peak (sine-like), low = broadband (noise-like).

zsa.mel~.digest: Calculates and outputs Mel Coefficients as a list.
zsa.mel~.use_when: Mel-band spectral coefficients from an FFT spectrum. `@bands` sets the number of mel bands (typical: 13–40); `@automatic 1` emits per-frame, `@automatic 0` waits for `bang`. Use as the first stage of an MFCC pipeline (zsa.mel~ → zsa.dct → MFCCs) or as a perceptually-spaced spectral feature on its own.

zsa.mfcc~.digest: Outputs Mel-frequency cepstral coefficients as a list.
zsa.mfcc~.use_when: Mel-frequency cepstral coefficients (industry-standard timbral feature for ML and similarity matching). `@bands` sets mel-band count (default ~40); `@fbe` toggles 'frequency band energy' representation. Outputs an MFCC list per FFT frame. Use whenever MFCC is the right feature — corpus matching, classification training, voice/instrument identification. Compare to fluid.mfcc~ (FluCoMa) which runs without external FFT plumbing but requires the FluCoMa ecosystem.

zsa.rolloff~.use_when: Spectral rolloff — the frequency below which a given fraction of total energy lies. `@rolloff` sets the fraction (0.–1., e.g. 0.95 means '95th-percentile frequency'). Use as a brightness descriptor more robust than centroid on noisy material; rolloff is less perturbed by outlier bins.

zsa.roughness.use_when: Psychoacoustic roughness (Plomp-Levelt-style sensory dissonance) computed from a partials list. Send `list <freqs>` to evaluate. `@mode` selects the roughness algorithm. Use to estimate perceived dissonance as a control source — e.g. drive a corrective filter when roughness exceeds a threshold. List-rate: pair with zsa.freqpeak~ to drive from live audio.

zsa.skewness~.use_when: Spectral skewness (asymmetry of the magnitude distribution around the centroid — third statistical moment). `@threshold` excludes bins below noise floor. Use as a timbre descriptor — positive skew = energy concentrated below centroid with a high-frequency tail; negative = inverse.

zsa.slope.use_when: Spectral slope (linear regression of log-magnitudes across frequency), list-rate. Send `list <magnitudes>` to evaluate. Use to characterise overall spectral tilt in one number — bright vs dark, with sign indicating direction.

zsa.slope~.use_when: Signal-rate spectral slope. `@mode` selects slope variant (linear, log, etc.). Use as a continuous brightness/darkness descriptor for analysis-driven control where one number summarises spectral tilt per frame.

zsa.spread~.use_when: Spectral spread (variance of magnitudes around the centroid — second statistical moment). `@threshold` excludes low-energy bins. Use as a 'spectral width' or 'thickness' descriptor: narrow (single tone) vs broad (noise-like) in one number.

zsa.virtfund.digest: Tries to calculate the virtual fundamental.
zsa.virtfund.use_when: Virtual fundamental — estimates the implied f0 of a harmonic series even when the actual fundamental is missing or weak. Send `list <frequencies>` to evaluate. `@gridratio` controls search granularity, `@max_iterations` caps compute, `@quiet` suppresses prints. Outputs the virtual fundamental (left) and confidence (right). Use for missing-fundamental analysis on bandlimited or filtered material — bass tracking on a filtered recording, octave-up illusions in additive synthesis.
